expression and posture should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not
a model selling a product. The scene should feel like a real travel diary
moment shared by someone who trusts this product enough to feature it
unprompted — candid, unstaged, and quietly aspirational, evoking the
freedom of a day spent walking a new city without a second thought about
what's on her feet.

templates.name: Handwritten Note-on-Table Letter Ad
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed background is a warm,
richly-grained dark wood surface shot top-down, filling 100% of the frame.
Centered on the surface, occupying roughly 80% of the canvas, is a single
sheet of white notepaper or index card photographed flat, with a soft
realistic drop shadow suggesting it is physically resting on the wood. In the
top-left corner of the card, place the [BRAND] logo mark plus wordmark, small
and understated, with [TRUST BADGE like a license/registration number] set
directly beneath it in a tiny sans-serif line. Below that, the entire
remaining card is filled with casual handwritten marker-style lettering in
dark ink, left-aligned, arranged as a personal note: a short underlined
handwritten headline at the top ([SHORT HEADLINE]), followed by 2-3 short
handwritten paragraphs of conversational body copy ([YOUR SUBHEAD, one
sentence] style claims and a soft call to action), then a vertical stacked
list of 5-6 handwritten numbers or short value options ([STAT 1 like
12G OF PROTEIN] style figures), with one entry visually singled out via a
hand-drawn arrow and a short annotation implying it is the personal or ideal
choice. At the bottom of the card, one final handwritten line issues the
action step ([CTA like Tap below to get started]), followed by a simple
hand-drawn downward arrow pointing off the bottom edge of the card toward an
implied tappable zone. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and
props only; this ad contains no product at all, only the note itself. The
scene should feel like a real, slightly imperfect personal note someone
scribbled and left on their own kitchen table — intimate, low-fi, and
trustworthy precisely because it looks unpolished and handmade, not like
corporate ad creative.

templates.name: Mock Apology Letter — Brand Confession Statement
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a full-bleed [BACKGROUND]
(near-black or deep brand-dark) vertical canvas styled as an official
"apology letter" statement. Top-center: a small line-art brand icon or
[BRAND] logomark in [ACCENT COLOR], sitting alone with generous negative
space above and below. Below it, a bold, oversized headline in [CONTRAST
COLOR] reading a short confessional phrase like "WE'RE SORRY" with a
decorative accent mark (arrow, dot, or bullet) breaking the words — set in
a tight, heavy sans-serif filling roughly 80% of the canvas width. Directly
beneath, a short italicized byline in [ACCENT COLOR] reading like "An
official apology statement from the team at [BRAND]." Below that, 2-3 short
paragraphs of confessional body copy in [CONTRAST COLOR] at reduced opacity,
left-aligned, addressed to the customer/prospect, admitting a category-wide
shortfall and revealing [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] or the
brand's offer as the surprising, generous alternative — written in a wry,
conversational, slightly self-deprecating tone with one aside in
parentheses for humor. Beneath the body copy, a closing line ("All the
best,") followed by a small handwritten-style signature graphic, a circular
headshot-style avatar placeholder, the signer's name, and title/brand
name in smaller type. Anchor the bottom of the canvas with a vertical
barcode graphic in [ACCENT COLOR], centered, echoing a formal
document/receipt feel. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product
and props only; the avatar circle is an abstract silhouette icon, not a
rendered human face. The scene should feel like a deadpan, too-honest
internal memo that leaked to the public — intimate, confessional, and
subversively funny, using restraint and negative space to make the
confession feel more credible than a typical ad.

templates.name: Playful Confession Letter — Freebie Reveal
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a full-bleed text-only
announcement card on a soft [SOFT BRAND COLOR like blush pink] gradient
background transitioning to [BACKGROUND], with no product photo and no
imagery — 100% typographic layout. Top 15% holds a bold, playful confession-style
[SHORT HEADLINE] in [DARK BRAND COLOR] with a matching emoji accent beside it.
Below it, a paragraph of [YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] in a friendly conversational
tone explains the offer, with the key deal phrase [YOUR OFFER like Free Gift with
Purchase] and the [PROMO CODE] both bolded inline for scannability. Beneath the
paragraph, a numbered list of 5-6 short, witty, one-line reasons (each prefixed
with a small circular numeral badge in [ACCENT COLOR]) builds a lighthearted,
self-aware justification for the offer. Near the bottom, a bold CTA line reading
[CTA like Shop now at yourbrand.com] sits beside a small icon, followed by a warm,
personal sign-off line in [BRAND] voice with a matching emoji. No people, hands,
faces, or body parts — text and iconography only. The scene narrative: this
should feel like a hand-written note slipped into an inbox from a friend who
runs the brand — cheeky, self-deprecating, and generous, like getting caught
being "too nice" and leaning into it with a wink. Typography is clean, rounded,
highly legible sans-serif with generous line spacing and left-aligned text
throughout; mood is warm, cheeky, approachable.

templates.name: Confessional Letter + Stacked Product Tins
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Split composition on a soft
off-white [BACKGROUND] canvas. Left 55% is a clean text column: a bold,
tight-leading headline in [DARK BRAND COLOR] set in a heavy sans-serif,
written as a confessional first-person hook under 12 words, followed by
3-4 short paragraphs of casual, diary-style body copy in a lighter weight
of the same sans-serif, left-aligned, generous line spacing, reading like
a candid personal letter or DM rather than ad copy. Right 45% shows
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] presented as two open,
stacked circular containers photographed top-down at a slight angle,
one resting just above the other, each revealing a swirled cream-textured
product fill in soft [ACCENT COLOR] tones, brushed metal or matte rims
catching soft diffuse studio light from upper left, shallow depth of
field, clean shadows grounding the product against the same off-white
surface as the text panel so the two zones feel like one continuous
plane. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only.
The scene should feel like a trusted friend is confiding a personal
discovery over text — intimate, unpolished, and enthusiastic, as if the
product were caught mid-use rather than staged for a shoot. [MOOD].

templates.name: Bittersweet Goodbye — Brand Letter on Black
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a full-bleed solid
[BACKGROUND] background (deep near-black or [DARK BRAND COLOR]) with no
photography, product shot, or texture — pure color canvas, 100% negative
space used as a text stage. Top-center, place a single symbolic emoji or
simple icon (e.g. a cracked heart, an hourglass, a lightbulb — chosen to
visually echo the headline's emotional twist) at roughly 12% of canvas
height, centered horizontally with generous clear space above and below.
Below the icon, center a two-line bold [SHORT HEADLINE] in large
all-caps [CONTRAST COLOR] sans-serif type, tightly leaded, no more than
2 short punchy lines. Below the headline, center a body paragraph
(4-6 sentences, [YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] tone) in medium-weight
[CONTRAST COLOR] sans-serif at roughly 60% of the headline's size,
generously line-spaced, centered, contained to about 65% of canvas width
so line lengths stay short and readable. Beneath the paragraph, in tiny
italic or light-weight type, place a one-line legal/disclaimer note in a
muted gray tone. At the very bottom, center a short signature line
reading "- [BRAND]" in the same weight as the headline but smaller,
acting as a handwritten sign-off. Maintain wide, even margins on all
sides so the whole composition reads like a formal typed letter or
announcement card rather than a busy ad. The scene should feel like
opening a personal, slightly cheeky letter from a brand you trust — an
intimate, confessional tone dressed as bad news that's secretly good
news, using restraint and whitespace to make the words feel weighty and
worth reading. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — text and a
single symbolic icon only, on a solid color field.

templates.name: Brand Letter — Sale Announcement
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed solid [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR]
background with no photography, product shot, or texture — a pure color field
functioning as letter paper. Top-center: a small circular [BRAND] emblem/seal
badge in [ACCENT COLOR like gold foil], sitting above the copy block with generous
padding. Below the seal, center-aligned text column occupying the middle 70% of
the canvas: a large serif [SHORT HEADLINE] in [CONTRAST COLOR], an italicized
parenthetical subhead beneath it, then a thin horizontal rule divider. Below the
rule, a short center-aligned paragraph block mixing a bolded key sentence with
lighter body copy, written in a warm, self-aware, slightly humorous confidential
tone — like a brand insider letting the reader in on a secret. Generous line
spacing and consistent margins on all sides (roughly 15% side margins) so the
layout reads as a formal announcement rather than a busy ad. Near the bottom,
a hand-written script-style signature in [CONTRAST COLOR] preceded by a small "x",
sitting above a small centered [BRAND] wordmark logo at the very bottom edge.
Typography contrast between elegant serif headline, italic subhead, and casual
script signature should feel like a personal letter from the brand team, not a
promotional flyer. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — text and emblem only,
on a solid color background. The scene should feel like an intimate, insider note
from the brand — confessional, cheeky, and warmly conspiratorial, as if the reader
has been let in on a private joke rather than sold to.

templates.name: Brand Apology Letter — Full-Bleed Gradient
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a full-bleed vertical
letter card: the entire frame is a smooth diagonal gradient running from
[PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] at the top corner to [ACCENT COLOR] at the opposite
corner, with no photography, product shot, or imagery anywhere — text and
color only. Left-align a block of white sans-serif letter copy occupying the
center 70% of the frame with generous margins on all sides: an opening
salutation ("Dear [AUDIENCE / CUSTOMER GROUP],"), 3–4 short paragraphs of
[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence]-style body copy building an apology-to-reveal
narrative, with 2–3 key words or phrases bolded and one phrase underlined for
emphasis, then a sign-off line and the brand's team name. Below the final
paragraph, vertically centered near the bottom of the frame, place a small
circular badge or wordmark containing [BRAND] in a contrasting script or
serif typeface on a [CONTRAST COLOR] fill. No people, hands, faces, or body
parts, and no product packaging or bottle renders — copy and color only. The
scene narrative: the layout should feel like an intimate, handwritten-style
direct address from the brand to its customers — vulnerable, sincere, and
quietly confident, as though confessing something long-awaited and turning it
into a reveal worth the wait.

templates.name: Brand Apology Letter (Direct-to-Reader Note)
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a full-bleed vertical
letter-style ad on a solid [BACKGROUND] surface, generous margins on all
sides so the whole composition reads as a single centered text column
occupying roughly the middle 80% of the frame width. Top-center: [BRAND]
wordmark logo, small and understated, sitting alone with open space above
and below it. Below the logo, a large centered serif headline in
[DARK BRAND COLOR], 3-4 lines, written as a warm, slightly cheeky
first-person confession or announcement from the brand — [YOUR HEADLINE,
under 10 words]. Below that, left-aligned body copy in a smaller serif or
grotesk font, 2-3 short paragraphs written in a conversational, letter-like
voice, with selective bold emphasis on the phrase carrying [CLAIM] or
[KEY INGREDIENT] benefit. Copy closes with a warm one-line sign-off and
signature line reading "Team [BRAND]". Below the copy, a full-width or
near-full-width rounded pill button in [DARK BRAND COLOR] or [ACCENT COLOR]
with centered bold uppercase [CTA like 60% OFF YOUR FIRST ORDER*] in
[CONTRAST COLOR] text. At the very bottom, small italic fine-print
disclaimer text in a muted tone referencing [YOUR OFFER like Free Shipping
over $50] or subscription terms. No people, hands, faces, or body parts —
product and props only; this is a pure typographic composition with no
product photography at all. The scene should feel like a warm, personal
note from the brand landing straight in the reader's inbox — intimate,
self-aware, and disarming rather than salesy, so the reader feels spoken
to directly rather than sold to.

templates.name: Founder's Handwritten Note — Notebook Page Offer
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed background is a single
page of lined notebook paper (pale blue horizontal rules, a red vertical
margin line near the left edge, subtle paper texture) filling 100% of the
canvas — no product, no packaging, no photography anywhere in frame. The
entire composition is handwritten marker text in dark navy/black ink,
casual all-caps script, filling the page top to bottom with generous line
spacing and a slightly imperfect, human hand-lettered feel. Two or three
key phrases are highlighted with a yellow marker-style highlight stroke
behind the text to draw the eye to [DISCOUNT like 30% OFF] and
[YOUR OFFER like Free Gifts with select sets]. The letter opens with a
casual, warm greeting line, moves through the offer details in short
punchy sentences, includes a call-to-action sentence, and closes with a
sign-off naming a real person by first name and title (e.g. "President +
Co-Founder") along with a one-line thank-you to the customer for
supporting [BRAND]. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and
props only; this is a flat notebook-page graphic, not a photograph. The
scene should feel like a personal, handwritten note slipped to a loyal
customer — intimate, sincere, and founder-to-friend rather than corporate
or salesy, so the discount feels like an insider tip, not a mass blast.

templates.name: Apology Letter — Heartfelt Offer Announcement
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a full-bleed letter-style
static ad on a solid [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] to [ACCENT COLOR] diagonal gradient
background with a subtle grain/noise texture — no product photography, no
imagery, pure typographic composition. Center a vertical column of copy with
generous margins on all sides (roughly 12-15% side margins). At the top, place
a bold all-caps headline in [CONTRAST COLOR] text, 2-4 words, paired with one
emotionally-matching emoji. Below it, stack short, conversational paragraphs
in regular-weight [CONTRAST COLOR] text at a smaller size, left-aligned or
centered, separated by generous line spacing so the letter reads like a
personal note rather than an ad. Midway down, set the core offer on its own
line(s) in bold, slightly larger type using [CTA like Get up to 50% off + free gift]
as a standalone visual anchor with extra vertical breathing room above and
below it. Follow with one or two more short reflective lines in the same
regular weight, then a sign-off line ("With love," or equivalent), and finish
with the [BRAND] wordmark logo in small letter-spaced caps centered near the
bottom edge. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — text and background
only. The scene should feel like a handwritten, slightly vulnerable personal
letter from the brand's founder rather than a polished promotional graphic —
intimate, warm, a little self-deprecating, and emotionally disarming so the
reader feels let in on something personal before the offer even registers.

templates.name: Honest Note — Founder Letter Restock Alert
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a text-only "note from the
founder" graphic on a full-bleed crumpled/creased white paper texture
background, evoking a handwritten, personal letter pinned up rather than a
polished ad. Center-aligned type block occupies the middle 70% of the canvas,
generous white-space margins top and bottom. Top line is a short warm greeting
in bold, medium-size serif or clean sans-serif type, e.g. "[SHORT HEADLINE like
An honest note from us]" followed by a small heart or hand emoji. Below it, a
body paragraph in regular weight explains urgency: "[URGENCY PHRASE like High
demand means this batch is already sold out] — this is the last
[YOUR PRODUCT] restock before [TIMEFRAME/OCCASION]. If you want yours, this is
the moment," with the key stat/percentage set in bold inline. A second
paragraph below, separated by spacing, announces the offer in bold: "[YOUR
OFFER like our BOGO offer is live] and limited to the first [STAT like 1000]
orders. Once those are gone, the deal disappears," with the limiting condition
italicized. Near the bottom, a small white rounded-rectangle button/pill
containing bold dark text reads "[CTA like Tap to secure yours]". In the
bottom-right corner, a tiny fine-print disclaimer line in italic, prefixed with
a small warning icon, echoes the urgency stat in miniature. Palette is
monochrome: off-white paper texture, charcoal/black text, no color accents.
No people, hands, faces, or body parts — text and paper texture only. The
scene should feel like a sincere, slightly urgent handwritten update from a
small brand owner to a loyal customer — intimate, transparent, and time-
pressured rather than salesy.

templates.name: Bold Announcement Letter — Solid Color Sale Card
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed solid [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR]
background, no gradient, no texture, no imagery — a pure color field acting as
a stage for typography. Top third: [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] set in large,
bold, condensed sans-serif or monospace-style caps, left-aligned, in [CONTRAST COLOR],
stacked across two short lines with tight leading. Middle third: generous negative
space, then [YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] set in a smaller monospace or condensed
uppercase font, left-aligned, in [CONTRAST COLOR] at reduced opacity, wrapping
across four to six short lines like a typed announcement. Bottom of frame:
[BRAND] wordmark set small, bold, letter-spaced, left-aligned, anchoring the
composition. No product, no packaging, no photography — this is a pure
typographic announcement card. No people, hands, faces, or body parts —
type and color only. The scene should feel like a direct, confident internal
memo from the brand to its most loyal customers — urgent but composed, like a
limited-window insider announcement rather than a loud discount blast. Maintain
generous margins on all sides so the type reads as considered, not cramped.

templates.name: Founder's Letter — "Dear Skeptic" Direct-Address Copy Ad
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a full-bleed, text-only
letter ad on a plain [BACKGROUND] background — no product photography, no
imagery, no graphic elements. Top-left begins with a bold salutation headline
in [DARK BRAND COLOR], "Dear [AUDIENCE PERSON, e.g. Skeptic/Believer/Beginner],"
sized larger than the body copy, left-aligned with generous top margin. Below
it, run 4-6 short paragraphs of plain-spoken body copy in a clean sans-serif
font, [DARK BRAND COLOR] on [BACKGROUND], left-aligned, single column, generous
line spacing and paragraph breaks so the letter reads like a personal note, not
an ad. Copy structure: open with an empathetic acknowledgment of the reader's
skepticism or objection, name the specific everyday friction point, reframe the
product's role as practical rather than disruptive, then close with a low-
pressure, one-line call to try it. Near the bottom-left, place a handwritten-
style signature scrawl in [DARK BRAND COLOR] representing [FOUNDER NAME], sitting
on a thin baseline rule. Bottom-right of the same row, place the [BRAND] wordmark
logo in bold sans-serif, same weight and scale as the signature so they visually
balance. Leave 20-25% of the canvas as clean negative space below the letter
block. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — copy and typography only. The
scene should feel like a founder personally wrote this note to address the exact
doubt the reader is having right now — candid, low-production, trustworthy,
like correspondence rather than marketing.

templates.name: Apology Letter + Resolved-in-Product Scene
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed solid [BACKGROUND]
color canvas, no photographic texture. Top 55% is a clear text zone: an
italic script-style [SHORT HEADLINE] reading like a heartfelt apology,
followed by 2-3 short paragraphs of confessional body copy in a clean
sans-serif, left-aligned, generous line spacing, set in a [CONTRAST COLOR]
that sits softly against the background. End the copy block with a
handwritten-feel sign-off line naming [BRAND] and a small emoji or mark.
Bottom 45% shows a single photographic scene: [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details] shown in its real-world use context, occupying the
lower portion of the frame, lit with soft, even, diffuse light so it reads
calm and resolved against the flat color field above it. Shot straight-on
or slightly elevated, shallow depth of field, no props competing for
attention. The scene should feel like the moment right after the apology
lands — quiet, resolved, and reassuring, as if [BRAND] is finally making
good on a promise. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE
DAILY REALITY]. Her expression and posture should evoke [AUDIENCE
ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], relaxed and at ease, not posed like a model selling
a product. [MOOD] color grading throughout, minimal contrast between text
and photo zones so the whole canvas reads as one continuous confession.

templates.name: Brand Apology Letter — Full-Bleed Gradient (2)
templates.template: Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors, typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed vertical background in a smooth diagonal gradient from [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] to [ACCENT COLOR], no photography, no product shot, no texture — just a clean saturated color field. Center-left aligned block of left-justified white text fills the middle 70% of the frame, set in a simple sans-serif, arranged as a personal letter: a salutation line ("Dear [AUDIENCE PERSON]" or "Dear [BRAND] customers,"), 2-4 short paragraphs of [YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence]-style body copy explaining a promise, apology, or announcement in first-person brand voice, with 2-3 key words or phrases bolded and underlined for emphasis (e.g. a claim, a stat, a guarantee). Generous line spacing and wide margins on all sides keep it readable at a glance. Body copy closes with a warm sign-off line ("With gratitude,") and the team/brand name. [BRAND] logo mark rendered small and centered near the bottom, sitting alone in open space below the text block. No borders, no boxes, no photographic elements anywhere in the frame. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — text and gradient only. The scene should feel like a handwritten, sincere note from a founder rather than a polished ad — intimate, transparent, and trustworthy, as if the brand is speaking directly and personally to one customer who felt unheard.

templates.name: Handwritten Confession Letter — Notepad Testimonial
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed vertical shot of a single sheet of textured notepaper filling the entire frame, photographed flat and straight-on like a page torn from a personal notebook. Background is a solid [BACKGROUND] tinted paper stock with visible flecks, deckled or slightly torn top edge, and soft natural creasing — no border, no card, no table surface visible. Top 15% holds a short handwritten-style headline in a bold marker or felt-tip font reading a personal confession or realization about the problem [PRODUCT CATEGORY] solves, in [DARK BRAND COLOR]. Below it, 3-4 short paragraphs in a casual handwritten-style font in the same [DARK BRAND COLOR] tell a first-person before/after story: stuck in a frustrating loop, noticing someone else already thriving, trying [BRAND], and describing the specific mechanism of how it helped, closing with a warm direct recommendation to the reader. No product photo, no logo lockup, no CTA button, no stock imagery — the letter text itself is the entire ad. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — paper and typography only. The scene should feel like a private, unpolished, handwritten note passed between friends — intimate, confessional, and more trustworthy than a polished ad precisely because it looks unmade for advertising.

templates.name: Confession Letter — Editorial Typography Manifesto
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a pure-typography letter/manifesto ad on a full-bleed [BACKGROUND like warm off-white/cream paper texture] background — no product, no imagery, no illustration, just type set like a printed page. Top third: a large serif headline in [DARK BRAND COLOR] mixing upright and italic weights across 4-5 short lines, left-aligned with generous line spacing, reading like a direct callout of the reader's exact situation — one or two key phrases set noticeably larger or in italic for emphasis. Middle: 4-6 short standalone paragraphs (1-2 sentences each) in a clean serif or humanist body font, left-aligned, generous line spacing and paragraph breaks, written in second person like a personal letter that names the reader's frustration, reframes it, and introduces [BRAND] as the answer. Bottom: a single underlined line of text in [ACCENT COLOR] or [DARK BRAND COLOR] functioning as the [CTA like Just take the 5-minute test], set apart with extra whitespace above it. Generous margins on all sides, no borders, no photography, no logos beyond restrained wordmark use — the entire ad is composition through type hierarchy, contrast between italic and upright serif weights, and negative space. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — text and paper texture only. The scene should feel like a handwritten confession turned into print — quiet, direct, and disarmingly honest, like the brand is speaking one-to-one to someone who has quietly given up before.

templates.name: Apology Letter — "We're Sorry" Confession Ad
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a full-bleed, editorial
letter-format ad on a [BACKGROUND like warm off-white/cream] solid
background — no imagery competing with the copy. Top-center: [BRAND] in a
small serif logotype/wordmark, generously spaced above the body. Below it,
a large serif headline reading like a heartfelt confession, e.g.
[SHORT HEADLINE like "We're Sorry..."], set in an elegant, oversized serif
typeface with tight leading, left-aligned, occupying the upper-middle third.
Beneath the headline, 3-5 short lines of serif body copy in [DARK BRAND COLOR]
set as an intimate first-person letter: it names a customer frustration
([CLAIM]), reframes it as not the customer's fault, and introduces
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] as the fix, closing with a line
about how the product is built to last. Leave generous vertical whitespace
between the copy block and the product. In the lower third, center
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] resting alone on a soft-shadowed
[SURFACE], photographed straight-on or three-quarter, small in frame (roughly
25-30% of canvas width) so it reads as quiet proof rather than a hero shot;
soft diffuse studio light, muted neutral tones matching the background,
minimal shadow. Below the product, centered, an understated underlined
text link CTA such as [CTA like "Upgrade now"] in [DARK BRAND COLOR], no
button shape, no border. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product
and props only. The scene should feel like a personal, handwritten apology
note from the brand rather than an advertisement — confessional, sincere,
and quietly confident that the product resolves the very complaint it
just admitted to.

templates.name: Breakup Letter to the Problem
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a portrait 4:5 static ad
styled as a handwritten-feel breakup letter to the customer's problem. Top 65%
of the canvas is a soft [SOFT BRAND COLOR like blush pink] solid background
functioning as the letter page — flush left-aligned text block starting near
the top edge. Line 1 is a bold italic script-style headline in [ACCENT COLOR
like coral red], "Dear [PROBLEM/CONDITION NAME]," followed on the next line by
"we're breaking up!" in the same script treatment. Below that, 4-5 short
punchy paragraphs in clean black sans-serif body type, each 1-2 sentences,
written in first-person breakup-letter voice that recaps the pain points the
condition caused, then pivots to relief and a new "someone" (the product) who
actually supports them, closing with a confident personal statement. Beneath
the body copy, one bold black line of social proof: "One of [SOCIAL PROOF like
2.8 million] [AUDIENCE PERSON PLURAL like women] who trust [BRAND]." Bottom
30% of canvas, right-aligned: [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details]
standing upright on the [SOFT BRAND COLOR] background, softly lit, sharp focus,
no surface or props beyond the product itself, generous negative space to its
left. A tiny single-line disclaimer sits bottom-left in light gray small type.
No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only. The scene
should feel like a private, cathartic letter finally being sent — someone
publicly and joyfully "breaking up" with a problem that quietly ran their life,
now replaced by a trusted new routine.

templates.name: Founder's Letter — Why We Exist
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a full-bleed [BACKGROUND]
solid-color page laid out like a printed letter, generous white margins on
all sides with all content stacked in a single left-aligned column occupying
the center 80% of the canvas width. At the top, an underlined serif headline
in [DARK BRAND COLOR], [SHORT HEADLINE], sitting alone with open space above
and below it. Below the headline, three short paragraphs of body copy in a
clean sans-serif font, generous line-height, one or two words bolded in
[ACCENT COLOR] for emphasis within the copy — set the tone as a personal,
first-person note rather than ad copy. At the bottom of the column, a founder
credit row: a small circular headshot photo on the left, [PERSON DESCRIPTION
like founder in their 30s, warm approachable expression], next to two lines
of stacked text — [NAME] and their title — with the [BRAND] wordmark logo
aligned to the far right of the same row. No product photography, no CTA
button, no price or offer callouts anywhere in the frame — this is a
trust-building editorial letter, not a product ad. The scene should feel like
reading a sincere, handwritten-style note from the person who built the
brand — intimate, transparent, and quietly confident, as if the founder is
speaking directly to one person rather than broadcasting to a crowd.

templates.name: Choose-Your-Bundle Grid Listicle
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a flat-lay grid listicle
on a solid [BACKGROUND] surface. At the top, a bold serif underlined headline
reads [SHORT HEADLINE, framed as a "Choose N" bundle-builder offer]. Below it,
arrange [NUMBER OF ROWS] horizontal rows, each separated by a thin
[ACCENT COLOR] divider line and labeled on the left in bold sans-serif as
"Choice 1", "Choice 2", etc. Each row displays 4-5 small product-only object
photos evenly spaced left to right, shot top-down or straight-on against the
same clean [BACKGROUND] surface, consistent soft even studio lighting and
consistent scale across every row so the grid reads as uniform catalog
photography. Reserve one product slot per row for [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details], styled to match the flat, evenly-lit product photography
of the surrounding items — not a lifestyle shot. In the lower-right corner,
overlay a bold circular badge in [ACCENT COLOR] containing an offer callout:
[YOUR OFFER like Buy One Get One Free] with [PROMO CODE] displayed in a
contrasting pill/button beneath it. The scene should feel like a curated,
abundant self-care shopping menu — like flipping through a catalog of small
indulgences you get to hand-pick, not a single hero product ad. No people,
hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only.

templates.name: Benefit Bullet Listicle with Floating Hero Product
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Clean, bright, near-white
[BACKGROUND] fills the full canvas as an airy studio backdrop with scattered
translucent liquid droplets falling softly across the frame for texture and
motion. Top-left corner shows [BRAND] logo in bold dark type. Below it, a
horizontal star-rating row with [STAR RATING like five gold stars] and
[SOCIAL PROOF like 4.7/5.0 5,979 Reviews] in small dark text. A large, bold
[YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] sits left-aligned beneath the rating, mixing
upright and italic type weights for emphasis on the key differentiator word.
Directly under the headline, a vertical bulleted list of 6-8 short
[BENEFIT 1-4] lines runs down the left half of the frame in clean sans-serif
type, with one benefit highlighted in a [ACCENT COLOR like lime yellow]
marker-highlight treatment to draw the eye. A small italic [CLAIM] or citation
line sits at the bottom-left in reduced size beneath the bullet list. On the
right side, [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] floats upright at
mid-to-lower frame, lit with soft even studio light and a subtle drop shadow,
positioned as the clear focal hero against the negative space. Above the
product, an oval [CONTRAST COLOR like lime yellow] badge/callout displays
[YOUR OFFER like Limited Program Discount] in bold dark centered text,
overlapping the top-right edge of the product for urgency. No people, hands,
faces, or body parts — product and props only. The scene should feel clinical
yet approachable — like a trusted, research-backed staple being presented
with quiet confidence rather than hype, reassuring a skeptical, detail-driven
buyer that this is the credible, superior choice.

templates.name: Search Bar Autocomplete Native Ad
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a native search-engine
mimicry ad. Top 35% of the frame is a white browser search dropdown panel:
a thin gray search bar showing "[WEBSITE]" as the queried term, followed by
4 stacked autocomplete-style suggestion rows, each starting with "[WEBSITE]"
in gray followed by a benefit phrase in bold black text — [BENEFIT 1],
[BENEFIT 2], [BENEFIT 3], and [YOUR OFFER like Free Shipping over $50]
styled with the discount emphasized. Directly beneath the search panel,
place the [BRAND] wordmark logo in [DARK BRAND COLOR], left-aligned,
slightly overlapping the top edge of the photo below. The remaining 60% of
the frame is a full-bleed lifestyle photograph: [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details] resting on [SURFACE] in [SETTING like a bright modern
kitchen], softly lit by natural window light, shallow depth of field, warm
and homey color grade. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and
props only. The scene should feel like a real search someone just typed
while trying to solve a everyday problem, and the autocomplete answers
below it confirm they've found exactly the right, trustworthy solution.
Overall mood: [MOOD].

templates.name: Native Search Bar Overlay — Lifestyle Table Scene
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a vertical 9:16 lifestyle
photo of a softly lit kitchen table, shot at 50mm f/2.2 with warm diffuse
natural light and shallow depth of field. Background (top 35%, blurred) shows
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] resting slightly out of focus,
brand pattern and logo faintly legible. Midground/foreground (bottom 65%)
shows a rustic table setting with an artfully arranged plate of fresh,
colorful food or ingredients relevant to [PRODUCT CATEGORY], plus one or two
soft-focus supporting props (a small bowl, a folded card or recipe insert,
a napkin) at the edges. Centered over the middle third of the frame, overlay
a realistic mobile search-bar UI element: a white rounded search field
containing the literal typed text "Does [BRAND]" with a small "x" clear icon,
directly beneath it a dropdown of 4 autocomplete suggestion rows in gray
text, each phrased as a real user question about [BRAND] (e.g. "Does
[BRAND] have [BENEFIT 1]?", "Does [BRAND] offer [BENEFIT 2]?"). Below the
search UI, near the bottom of the frame, place bold white all-caps text
reading "[CTA like ORDER NOW]" with a soft drop shadow for legibility over
the photo. The scene should feel like an authentic, unposed moment in
someone's kitchen — the kind of candid table setup a real customer would
photograph, not a staged studio shot — so the search-bar overlay reads as a
native, trustworthy interface element rather than an ad unit. No people,
hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only.

templates.name: Search Results Grid — Native Discovery Mock
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a mock search-engine
results page as the ad canvas. Top 15% is a rounded search bar containing
the text "[SEARCH QUERY like Where can I buy high-quality PRODUCT CATEGORY online?]"
with a magnifying-glass icon at the right edge, and a thin tab row below it
reading "All | Shopping | Images | Video | More" in small gray text on white
background. Below the tabs, arrange a 2-column, 3-row grid of rounded-corner
photo tiles (roughly equal size, small gutter between them) that function as
organic "search result" thumbnails: one tile is a clean top-down or 3/4 hero
shot of [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] resting on [SURFACE], one
tile shows the product's [KEY INGREDIENT] or raw components arranged on a
neutral dark surface, one tile shows the finished product-in-use result
(a plated or styled outcome shot), one tile is a lifestyle moment implying
[SETTING like doorstep delivery or home use], one tile is a close detail or
texture shot, and one tile shows a phone or device screen displaying the
brand's website/app checkout. Overall palette is neutral and photographic
(whites, warm woodgrain, muted reds/greens) so the grid reads as authentic
organic search results rather than a designed ad. The scene should feel like
a real, unpaid Google search a curious shopper just ran — credible, everyday,
and native to the search results feed rather than staged advertising. No
obvious brand logos, banners, discount badges, or CTA buttons anywhere in
the grid — the format's entire power comes from looking unsponsored. If any
person appears in a tile, keep them small, incidental, and candid (e.g.
carrying a delivery box), never posed or looking at camera — this format
must not read as a lifestyle hero shot.

templates.name: Native Notes-App Checklist Screenshot
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Recreate a realistic iOS Notes
app screenshot as the full-bleed canvas. Top 8% is the native app nav bar:
a back chevron with "All iCloud" label on the left, and share/more icons on
the right, in plain black on white. Below that, a bold black title in large
sans-serif type reading [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] framed as a personal
checklist or challenge title. Below the title, a vertical list of 10-13 rows,
each row starting with a filled circular checkmark icon in [ACCENT COLOR
like warm yellow/gold], followed by bold black lead-in text (e.g. a day or
step number) and a regular-weight continuation describing one small,
specific, relatable action tied to [PRODUCT CATEGORY]. Rows are evenly
spaced with generous left padding and consistent line height, mimicking real
note-taking, not designed marketing copy. At the very bottom, a single bold
black line delivers [YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] naming [BRAND] as the
tool/companion behind the list, styled identically to normal note body text
so it reads as an authentic sign-off rather than an ad unit. Background is
solid flat white throughout, no shadows, no gradients, no photography — pure
flat UI. Typography is the native iOS system sans-serif throughout. No
people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only; this is a
plain text/UI screenshot with zero photographic elements. The scene should
feel like a real personal note someone jotted for themselves and screenshotted
to share — unpolished, authentic, and utility-first rather than promotional,
so it reads as organic discovery rather than paid advertising.

templates.name: POV Confession Notes App Screenshot
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Render a realistic iPhone Notes
app screenshot, full-bleed, cream/off-white background (#FBF7F0-style), with
the native Notes UI chrome intact: top-left "< Notes" back button in
[ACCENT COLOR like warm amber/gold], top-right share icon and "···" more
icon in the same accent color. Below the chrome, a bold black title in the
native Notes serif-adjacent system font reading [SHORT HEADLINE, POV-style
confession under 10 words], sized roughly 28-32pt and left-aligned with tight
line height. Below the title, 5-7 short paragraphs of plain black body text
in the native Notes sans-serif font, each paragraph 1-3 sentences, generous
line spacing between paragraphs, mimicking a real personal journal entry
about discovering and trying [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details]. One
sentence partway through the body must be highlighted with a soft
[ACCENT COLOR like pale yellow/amber] text-highlight marker background,
containing the single most persuasive claim: [CLAIM or STAT, e.g. a speed-
to-result number]. The final two lines act as a soft CTA in the same plain
body style — a short encouragement to act, then an instruction naming
[CTA like "Get matched with a qualified provider online"]. No people, hands,
faces, or body parts — this is a UI-only screenshot with no photographic
imagery at all. The scene should feel like a private, unscripted diary entry
someone impulsively screenshotted and shared — intimate, unpolished, and
believable precisely because it looks like a real note, not an ad.

templates.name: Everyday To-Do Screenshot Hook
templates.template: Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors, typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a vertical lifestyle background: a softly lit, out-of-focus indoor scene — a window with horizontal blinds, a leafy plant edge-of-frame lower-left, and the corner of a woven-texture armchair lower-right — all rendered as a shallow-depth-of-field backdrop so the eye goes to the center. Centered in the middle 55% of the frame, float a realistic smartphone mock-up showing [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] — a to-do/notes app UI with a status bar, a back-navigation label reading "[APP SCREEN LABEL like Notes]", a bold list title "[LIST TITLE like To Do]", 3-5 checklist rows using [BENEFIT 1-4] as everyday task phrases with checkmark icons in [ACCENT COLOR], and a bottom icon toolbar in [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR]. Above the phone, overlay a bold headline on a [ACCENT COLOR like bright yellow] highlighter-marker background: "[YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words]" in heavy black sans-serif, angled slightly, sitting just above the phone's top edge. Below the phone, place a second highlighter-style text block: "[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence]" in the same treatment, reinforcing the CTA-style payoff. Small circular brand logo badge in [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] top-right corner. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only. The scene should feel like a quiet, cluttered corner of real domestic life — half-open blinds, a plant that needs watering, an unmade errand list — so the app being shown feels like it belongs in that unglamorous, relatable moment rather than a staged tech demo.

templates.name: Notes App Screenshot — Numbered List Hook
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Recreate a realistic iPhone Notes
app screenshot, full-bleed, filling the entire frame edge to edge as if
genuinely captured mid-scroll. Top status bar shows time, signal, wifi, and
battery icons; below it a thin Notes navigation bar reads "All iCloud" with a
back chevron on the left and undo/redo, share, more-options, and "Done"
icons on the right, plus a small timestamp line reading "[TIMEFRAME], [TIME]".
Below the nav bar, a bold black title in large native Notes serif/system font
renders [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] as if it were the note's title. Under
the title, a clean vertically-stacked numbered list (1 through 8-10 items) in
the native Notes body font lists [BENEFIT 1-4] and related short punchy
line items, each no more than 6-8 words, left-aligned with generous line
spacing on a plain white background. The bottom third of the frame shows the
native iOS keyboard partially open with a predictive-text suggestion bar
above it, implying the note is actively being typed. No people, hands,
faces, or body parts — product and props only; this is a pure UI/screenshot
composition. The scene narrative: it should feel like a candid, unpolished
screenshot someone actually took of their own private notes app while jotting
down a genuine idea — intimate, authentic, and un-styled, so the list reads
as real personal insight rather than an ad. Lighting and rendering should
mimic a true iPhone screen photograph/screen-recording still: crisp UI
edges, native iOS system fonts, standard Apple Notes yellow "Aa" toolbar
icon, no drop shadows or graphic embellishment beyond what the OS itself
renders.

templates.name: Notes App — To-Do List with Connected Phone Preview
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed dark [BACKGROUND]
mimicking a native notes-app screen. Top bar shows a back chevron labeled
"< Notes", a date/timestamp in [ACCENT COLOR], and share/more icons in the
top-right corner. Below the bar, a bold [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words]
in large white sans-serif type sits left-aligned, spanning roughly 80% of
the width. Under the headline, a numbered checklist of 4 short to-do lines
in a smaller white/gray sans-serif font runs down the left 55% of the
frame. On the right 40-45% of the frame, layered partly off the right
edge, float [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] rendered as an
in-app UI card or device screen preview — showing a plan/progress module
with a title row, a small progress bar or completion fraction, a row of
2-3 circular icon buttons, and a stat panel below with a labeled metric
and numeric readout. The [BRAND] logo sits small and centered near the
bottom-left, in [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR], above the device's home-indicator
bar. Palette is near-black background with crisp white text and a single
[ACCENT COLOR] highlight used sparingly for dates, links, and progress
fills. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only.
The scene should feel like a genuine personal notes app screenshot someone
is proud of and about to share — a private planning ritual made public,
organized, ahead of schedule, and quietly aspirational.

templates.name: Notes App Confession — UGC Screenshot Ad
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Recreate a full-screen iPhone
Notes app screenshot, edge-to-edge, as the entire canvas — no product photo,
no background scene. Top bar: a yellow back chevron with "Notes" label at
top-left, a yellow share-icon and yellow "•••" more-options icon at
top-right, on a plain white background. Below the top bar, the note body
fills the remaining space left-aligned with generous margins: a bold black
title line reading [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] written like a personal
confession or a friend's tip, optionally with 1-2 small emoji inline for
emphasis. Below the title, 1-2 short paragraphs of default black
system-font body text in [PERSON DESCRIPTION like someone who just
discovered the product]'s own voice, casual and conversational, mentioning
[KEY INGREDIENT or CLAIM]. Follow with a plain bulleted list (each line
starting with "-") of [BENEFIT 1-4] or use-case scenarios, written in
lowercase native texting style. End with a bold black closing line stating
[YOUR OFFER like Free Shipping over $50] and a bright blue underlined
hyperlink reading [WEBSITE], with one warm emoji directly after it. Bottom
of the frame shows the native Notes app toolbar: yellow checklist icon,
yellow camera icon, yellow drawing/pencil-circle icon, and yellow
compose-square icon evenly spaced on a plain white bar. The entire
composition must look like an authentic, unedited iOS screenshot — plain
white background throughout, only black body text and yellow UI icons,
zero product renders, zero photography, zero graphic embellishment beyond
the native app chrome. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — text and
native app interface only. The scene should feel like a real customer
jotted down an urgent, excited tip to save for later and screenshotted it
to share — candid, unpolished, and trustworthy because it doesn't look like
an ad at all.

templates.name: Notes App To-Do List Over Fresh-Fruit Supplement Flat-Lay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a vertical 4:5 flat-lay.
Top 8% is a thin stat bar on the background color reading [STAR RATING like
five gold stars] [STAT like 4.8/5] "|" [SOCIAL PROOF like 150,000+ Happy
Customers]. The top 55% of the frame shows a realistic mobile notes-app card
floating over the scene: rounded white card with soft drop shadow, a back
chevron and "Notes" label plus edit/share/menu icons along its top edge, a
bold headline styled as an app title such as [SHORT HEADLINE like "2026 To-Do
List"], followed by 4-5 checklist rows each with an orange checkmark bullet
and a short benefit-driven line from [BENEFIT 1-4]. Bottom 45% is a bright,
sunlit flat-lay: [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] standing upright
and centered, surrounded by halved and sliced fresh fruit (citrus and kiwi
work well) fanned symmetrically left and right on a soft pastel [BACKGROUND]
surface, shot from a slight overhead angle with soft natural daylight and
shallow depth of field so the fruit at the edges falls gently out of focus. A
small rounded callout badge near the product's upper right states a
formulation stat such as [STAT 1 like "20 Vital Nutrients"] with a one-line
sub-detail beneath it. A thin footer band across the very bottom holds 3
short trust badges separated by dividers, e.g. [BENEFIT 1-4], plus a
"Loved by" press-logo strip beneath it using generic placeholder wordmarks.
No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only. The scene
should feel like a bright, optimistic morning reset — as if opening a phone
to a freshly written self-improvement list right as the daily supplement
ritual begins: energizing, credible, and habit-forming.

templates.name: Notes App Screenshot — Testimonial Quote Over Held Product
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose the entire frame as a
faux iOS Notes app screenshot: a status/nav bar at the very top reading
"Notes" with a back chevron on the left and "OK" on the right, in the
system's native yellow accent color, on a plain white background. Below the
nav bar, a small centered gray timestamp line reads [DATE/TIME like 21
February 2025, 11:30 AM]. Beneath that, a bold black note-title headline in
native Notes serif/system font: [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words]. Directly
under the headline, a short italicized or quoted testimonial line in the
same note-body font: [PULL-QUOTE — the most emotional 4-8 word phrase from
the review], attributed inline as "-[REVIEWER NAME like Edna]". Below the
text block, a single full-width photograph fills the remaining lower ~55%
of the frame like an inline note attachment: a softly blurred, warm indoor
lifestyle background (wooden table, chairs, large window light) with one
hand and forearm entering the frame from the bottom, fingers holding
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] upright and in sharp focus at
the vertical center of the photo, product label facing camera. Natural
daylight, shallow depth of field, candid unposed framing — must read as a
real, unretouched screenshot of a genuine note someone saved, not a
polished studio ad. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with
[AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. The hand's grip and the casual framing should
evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. The
scene should feel like a private note someone kept for themselves, jotting
down a product recommendation the moment it worked for them — proof
that feels found, not advertised.

templates.name: Notes App Confession Screenshot
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Recreate a realistic iPhone Notes
app screenshot, full-bleed, pure black background filling the entire frame.
Top bar: small orange "< All Notes" back button top-left, a share icon and a
three-dot menu icon top-right, both in orange. Below the top bar, a centered
gray timestamp line reading [DATE AND TIME like September 17, 2025 at 2:55 PM].
Directly beneath, a bold white headline in native Notes serif/system font,
2-3 lines, phrased as a candid personal confession or realization about
[PRODUCT CATEGORY], e.g. [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words, first-person regret
or realization hook]. Below the headline, a left-aligned bulleted list (dash
markers) in white text, 6-8 short lines, mixing a candid pain point, a
specific price or stat about [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details], a
sensory or educational detail, a trust signal like [TRUST BADGE like "30-day
guarantee"], and a warm closing line tying it to a daily ritual. Generous
margins on both sides, tight single-spaced line height, authentic phone-note
plainness — no images, no icons, no branding marks anywhere in the body. No
people, hands, faces, or body parts — text-only note interface. The scene
narrative: this should feel like a real, unscripted note someone typed to
themselves late at night after a moment of buyer's-remorse-turned-relief,
raw and diary-like rather than designed as an ad. Typography must mimic
authentic iOS Notes rendering exactly — no drop shadows, no gradients, no
stylization.

templates.name: Pain-Point Checklist + Floating Product Pour
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a mobile-app-screenshot-style
static ad on a clean white background. Top bar mimics a minimal app header: a
back arrow, small circular icon buttons (undo, redo, share, more) in
[ACCENT COLOR], and a "Done" text button in [ACCENT COLOR] on the top right —
this framing device signals "captured as a note," not a traditional ad. Left
55% of the canvas is the text zone, top-aligned: a bold [DARK BRAND COLOR]
headline in 3 short stacked lines, under 8 words, phrased as "When
[PROBLEM CONTEXT] Often:" followed by a tight bulleted list of 4-5 short
pain-point phrases (2-4 words each), each preceded by a small circular
prohibition/warning icon in [ACCENT COLOR]. Below the list, one bold
[DARK BRAND COLOR] sentence-length line contrasts what "others" do by building
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] into their routine — this is the
pivot line from problem to solution. Right 45% of the canvas shows
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] tilted at a dynamic diagonal angle,
floating mid-frame as if caught in motion, with a visible pour or dispense of
its contents streaming downward from the package into an implied vessel below
frame, rendered with soft realistic motion blur and light droplet detail.
Lighting is bright, even, and studio-clean with a soft shadow beneath the
product to ground it against the white background. No people, hands, faces,
or body parts — product and props only. The scene should feel like a private
note-to-self moment where someone is quietly diagnosing their own bad days and
finding a fix, evoking a personal, confessional-checklist mood rather than a
polished advertisement. [MOOD]

templates.name: Big Dollar-Off Instant Savings Banner
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical banner divided into two
zones. Top 65% is a solid [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] background holding the
[BRAND] logo centered near the top, followed by a bold stacked offer block:
a short lead-in word (e.g. "SAVE"), then an oversized numeral-style discount
figure in [CONTRAST COLOR like bright yellow] rendered dramatically larger
than all other text so it dominates the frame, followed by a short qualifier
word directly beneath it in the same accent color. Below that, centered
white text gives the offer condition in two short lines, then a bold
all-caps CTA line, then a smaller urgency line with an end date. A large
soft-edged diagonal light beam or angled color-block sweeps from the
upper-left corner across the background behind the text stack, adding depth
without competing with the copy. Bottom 35% of the frame transitions into a
full-bleed environmental photo: an open road cutting through a
softly-lit natural landscape (tree line, distant hills, wide sky) with
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] placed hero-forward in the
extreme foreground, angled slightly, resting on or emerging from the road
surface so it feels grounded in the real world the offer applies to. Photo
style is crisp, naturalistic daylight, gently desaturated to let the top
graphic panel stay dominant. No people, hands, faces, or body parts —
product and props only. The scene should feel like a confident,
straightforward retail promise: a big, unmissable number backing a real
product you're about to go use, not a staged studio deal. [MOOD].

templates.name: UGC Hand-Held Discount Reveal
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical 9:16 UGC-style handheld
photo: a hand holds up [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] toward the
camera at chest height, filling the center-to-lower two-thirds of the frame,
shot outdoors in bright natural daylight with a softly blurred backdrop of
sunlit foliage or sky. Top 20% of the frame carries a bold highlighter-yellow
text band with a small eyebrow line "[URGENCY PHRASE like Today Only!]"
above a larger bold headline "[YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words]" set in a
thick, native-social sans-serif, both rendered as if drawn on with a
digital highlighter/marker overlay. Bottom 15% of the frame carries a
matching yellow band with "[CTA like Use promo code: PROMOCODE]" in bold
dark text. A single line of small disclaimer text sits at the very bottom
edge. Lighting should feel unposed and native — slight lens flare, real
handheld angle, authentic phone-camera framing, not studio-lit. Cast
[AUDIENCE PERSON like a real everyday customer, not a model] — someone
living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY like a busy routine who screenshots
deals before they expire]. Her hand, grip, and framing should evoke
[AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY like someone who caught a genuine insider
deal], not a paid actor selling a product. The scene should feel like a real
customer casually flashing their own pack in daylight to share a discount
they're excited about — candid, native, and trustworthy rather than staged
or corporate.

templates.name: Bundle Discount Stack Banner
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed [BACKGROUND] solid
backdrop, soft and clean with generous negative space. Top third holds a
centered three-line headline in [DARK BRAND COLOR] serif type: [YOUR HEADLINE,
under 10 words] ending in [DISCOUNT like 45%]. Center of the frame shows
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] grouped with its included companion
items (e.g. tool, vessel, storage piece) arranged left of a bold "+" divider,
with a second cluster of smaller bundled add-on items (travel-size packs,
a secondary box, a small bottle) to its right — each item sitting on its own
subtle drop shadow with a small strikethrough price label beneath it in
[ACCENT COLOR]. Upper-right corner holds a small circular or heart-shaped
badge in [ACCENT COLOR] with short [URGENCY PHRASE like For a Short Time Only]
in white capital letters. Just above the CTA, a thin horizontal rule flanked
by a one-line trust phrase in small caps: [TRUST BADGE like With Money-Back
Guarantee]. Below that, a wide rounded-pill CTA button in [PRIMARY BRAND
COLOR] with [CTA like ORDER NOW] in bold white capitals plus a right-pointing
arrow. Bottom-right corner carries [BRAND] wordmark/logo in [DARK BRAND
COLOR]; bottom-left carries small fine-print [CLAIM] disclaimer text in
light gray. Lighting is even, soft studio light with no harsh shadows,
packshot-clean and airy. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product
and props only. The arrangement should feel like a limited-time bundle deal
laid out on a store counter — generous, transparent about savings, and
easy to scan in under two seconds.

templates.name: Refer-a-Friend Embrace Offer Banner
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Top 45% of the frame is a solid
[BACKGROUND] backdrop reserved as a clean text zone: centered [YOUR HEADLINE,
under 10 words] in a warm serif typeface, a centered [YOUR SUBHEAD, one
sentence] beneath it in a lighter sans-serif, and a centered pill-shaped
[CTA like Refer Now] button in [ACCENT COLOR] with white text. Bottom 55% of
the frame shows two women embracing warmly at close range, cropped tight from
the chest up, soft natural window light, shallow depth of field, candid and
unposed. Their clothing and skin tones should read as neutral, soft-toned
knitwear against the same [BACKGROUND] tone so the two zones blend seamlessly
with no hard visual break. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] embracing [AUDIENCE PERSON]
— two people who resonate with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Their expressions
should be genuine laughter and closeness, evoking [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL
IDENTITY], not posed stock-photo affection. The scene should feel like a real
moment between people who trust each other enough to share something that
worked for them — warm, intimate, and quietly celebratory, like good news
passed between friends. Small centered legal disclaimer text in muted gray at
the very bottom.

templates.name: Cascading Product Lineup — Offer Banner
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Center the composition on a
deep [DARK BRAND COLOR] solid-color backdrop with a soft directional
spotlight falling from upper-left, creating a warm pool of light on the
floor beneath the products and letting the corners fall into shadow. Arrange
4 units of [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] in a tight cascading
cluster — one unit standing tall and centered as the hero, two flanking it
at slightly shorter heights, and one lying flat/angled in the foreground —
so each unit's distinct pattern or finish is fully visible and none fully
overlap. Reserve the top 20% of the frame for a small centered trust badge
line (icon + short text) directly above a large serif or elegant display
headline announcing the offer, both center-aligned on the backdrop above
the product cluster. Reserve the bottom 10% for a centered wordmark/logo
sitting just above the horizon line of the lit floor. Shot straight-on at
eye level, 50mm equivalent, soft studio diffusion with one warm key light,
shallow-to-moderate depth of field keeping all product faces crisp. The
scene should feel like a confident retail display moment — generous,
celebratory, and abundant, as if the brand is proud to show off its full
range at once rather than a single SKU. No people, hands, faces, or body
parts — product and props only.

templates.name: Build-Your-Own Bundle Offer Flat-Lay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical 9:16 offer banner.
Top 20% is a dark, moodily-lit textured backdrop (e.g. weathered wood
slats or a dark wall) with bold centered headline text: [SHORT HEADLINE]
above a large, oversized bundle-offer wordmark like [YOUR OFFER, short
acronym or 2-3 words] in [ACCENT COLOR], followed by [DISCOUNT like $350 OFF]
in clean white sans-serif. Middle 55% is a dark tabletop surface in
dramatic side lighting where 4-5 units of [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging
details] are arranged in a neat fanned or angled row, evenly spaced,
photographed from a slightly elevated top-down angle so each unit is fully
visible and product materials/finishes catch the light distinctly. Directly
below the product row, centered supporting copy reads [YOUR SUBHEAD, one
sentence describing the bundle mechanic]. Bottom 20% contains complementary
lifestyle props resting on the same dark surface — objects that reinforce
the brand's premium, adult, after-hours mood (e.g. a cut-crystal glass of
amber liquid, a few cigars, or similar) arranged loosely in the lower
corners, with a solid [DARK BRAND COLOR] triangular or angled color-block
element anchoring the bottom edge. Lighting throughout is warm, directional,
and dramatic with deep shadows, evoking a rich, masculine, evening-lounge
atmosphere. The arrangement should feel like a curated display of covetable
objects laid out for a considered, high-value purchase decision — deliberate,
premium, and worth building a bundle around. No people, hands, faces, or
body parts — product and props only.

templates.name: Stacked Product Discount Banner
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Center the composition on a solid
[BACKGROUND] backdrop, softly lit and seamless with no visible horizon line.
Top 30% of the frame is a clean text zone: a bold, large serif or sans-serif
headline reading [DISCOUNT like Up To 46% Off] centered horizontally, with a
smaller italic or regular-weight qualifier line beneath it reading
[YOUR OFFER like *Select Bundles] directly under the headline. Below the text,
the middle 55% of the frame is occupied by [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging
details] arranged as a neat stacked or grouped display, angled slightly toward
camera, showing a variety of the product's available colors or variants
fanned so each one is visibly distinguishable, resting on an invisible
surface with a soft naturalistic shadow beneath. Lighting is soft, diffuse,
studio-style, coming from upper-left, with gentle ambient occlusion between
stacked units to convey premium quality and tactile softness. Bottom-left
corner holds the [BRAND] logo/wordmark in bold dark text, small and
understated. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props
only. The scene should feel like a clean, confident retail moment — a
well-organized shelf display designed to make the discount feel like an
irresistible, easy win rather than a clearance bin afterthought.

templates.name: Stacked Stat-Focus Offer Banner
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed solid [SOFT BRAND COLOR like pale mint/light sage] background, no photography, no texture — a clean flat color field top to bottom. Top-left corner: [BRAND] logotype in [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR], small and understated. Below it, a short bold all-caps eyebrow line combining plain and bold weights: "[SHORT HEADLINE]". Beneath that, a large stacked headline in [DARK BRAND COLOR], 3-4 lines, left-aligned, mixing regular and bold weight on the key phrase: "[YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words]". Directly below the headline, an oversized numeral/stat treatment — a giant "$" or unit symbol plus "[STAT]" rendered in heavy bold [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR], dramatically larger than all other text, dominating roughly a third of the vertical space as the focal point. Below the stat, a full-width pill-shaped button with a thin [DARK BRAND COLOR] outline, transparent fill, centered bold label "[CTA like Open now]". Beneath the button, one line of small gray qualifying copy: "[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence]". At the very bottom, tiny light-gray legal disclaimer text in a condensed block. Generous negative space surrounds all elements; layout is strictly vertical and left-aligned with no imagery, icons, or illustrations anywhere. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only; this is a pure typographic offer layout with zero photographic or human content. The scene should feel like a clean, trustworthy digital finance offer — confident, uncluttered, and instantly scannable, where the dollar figure itself is the hero rather than any product or lifestyle image.

templates.name: Build-Your-Bundle Offer Banner (Hand-Placed Product + Badge Callout)
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed background is a
clean white subway-tile surface shot straight-on, evenly lit, soft neutral
shadows. Compose a tight product grouping in the upper two-thirds: two
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] items stand upright side by side
on the tile ledge, labels facing forward and fully legible, while a third
matching [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] is held mid-air by a
hand entering from the top edge of the frame, fingers gently pinching the
cap as if placing it down — implying an active, in-progress routine rather
than a static shot. In the upper-right corner, add a small circular badge
callout on a [BACKGROUND] backdrop containing a miniature grouped photo of
[BRAND] products, paired with two lines of bold condensed sans-serif text
above it reading [YOUR OFFER like Free Deluxe Sample] to flag the
promotional hook at a glance. Reserve the bottom third as clean negative
space on the same tile background for a bold, tightly-leaded condensed
sans-serif headline in [DARK BRAND COLOR], stacked across two to three
short lines, reading [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] with [DISCOUNT like
15%] emphasized in a heavier weight or contrasting color. Cast [AUDIENCE
PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. The hand's
gesture and calm, unhurried placement of the product should evoke
[AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. The
scene should feel like a real person is mid-way through building their
own curated shelf edit, deliberately adding one more product to a set
they already trust — practical, self-directed, and reward-driven, not
staged. Shot straight-on at eye level, soft diffused daylight, shallow
depth of field keeping tile grout lines crisp but background softly
falling off.

templates.name: Offer Banner — Product-in-Tray Hero with Bold Sale Headline
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a square offer banner.
Top 30% of the canvas is a clean [BACKGROUND] solid backdrop reserved as a
clear text zone: centered, bold, outlined display headline reading
[URGENCY PHRASE like BLACK FRIDAY] in [DARK BRAND COLOR], with a smaller solid
weight subhead directly beneath it reading [DISCOUNT like 25% OFF SITEWIDE] in
[ACCENT COLOR]. Bottom 70% of the canvas is a hero product shot: [YOUR PRODUCT
with key packaging details] photographed at a slightly elevated three-quarter
angle, open or displayed to reveal its interior/key feature, resting inside or
on top of [SURFACE like a utility tray or plain textured tray] against the
same [BACKGROUND] backdrop. Lighting is soft, even, diffuse studio light with
minimal shadow, giving a clean commercial product-catalog feel. The
arrangement should feel like a straightforward, no-nonsense storewide sale
moment — the product presented plainly and confidently, letting the discount
headline do the persuading rather than any styled lifestyle scene. No people,
hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only.

templates.name: Hand-Held Lineup Sale Banner
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a lived-in, natural-light
kitchen countertop scene shot from slightly above. A hand enters from the
lower-left, holding one [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] upright,
while 3-4 additional variants or companion items from the [PRODUCT CATEGORY]
lineup stand fanned out beside it in a row, each in a different [ACCENT COLOR]
so the range reads as a colorful family of products. Background is a bright,
slightly worn tabletop or counter in [SETTING like a bright home kitchen],
softly out of focus at the edges, with natural daylight and casual clutter
just visible at the frame edges to keep it authentic rather than studio-perfect.
Centered over the products, bold stacked text reads [URGENCY PHRASE like UP TO]
on one line and a large [DISCOUNT like 50% OFF] on the next in heavy condensed
white type with a soft drop shadow for legibility against the busy background.
Below the products, place the [BRAND] logo mark, then a short supporting line
[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] in light white type. The scene should feel like a
real person just grabbed their favorite everyday tools off the counter to show
you the whole lineup — casual, hands-on, and unstaged, like a friend showing
you their kitchen drawer during a sale rather than a polished catalog shot.
Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON like a home cook in her 30s who loves color and small
kitchen upgrades] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY like a kitchen
full of mismatched, worn-out tools she's been meaning to replace]. Her hand and
grip should feel casual and confident, evoking [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY
like someone who treats her kitchen as a source of everyday joy], not a model
posing for a product ad.

templates.name: Sitewide Discount Hero with Lifestyle Proof Strip
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical banner on a solid
[BACKGROUND] field. Top third: small bold kicker text reading
"[URGENCY PHRASE like SITEWIDE]" centered, in [DARK BRAND COLOR]. Below it,
a massive oversized numeral treatment showing "[DISCOUNT like 20%]" in a
heavy geometric sans-serif filling nearly the full width, rendered in
[PRIMARY BRAND COLOR], with "OFF" stacked directly beneath the percentage
in the same weight and color. Beneath the numeral, a single line reads
"Use Code: [PROMO CODE]" in [DARK BRAND COLOR], with the code itself bolded.
Below that, a bold underlined CTA label "[CTA like SHOP NOW]" centered. The
bottom third is a row of three equal-width, edge-to-edge square-cropped
photos sitting flush against the bottom edge of the canvas, each showing
[AUDIENCE PERSON] wearing or using [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details]
in a relaxed, sunlit outdoor setting — one candid, one showing the back
detail of the product, one close and joyful — all shot in warm natural
daylight with a soft, candid, real-life feel rather than studio polish. The
overall palette stays restrained to two or three brand tones so the massive
offer numeral reads instantly before anything else. The scene should feel
like a casual, sunny day spent outdoors in comfortable everyday wear — easy,
warm, and unposed, like a moment caught between friends rather than a staged
shoot. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY].
Her expression and posture should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not
a model selling a product.

templates.name: Bundle Savings Arc Banner
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical banner on a
[BACKGROUND] gradient background transitioning from teal to soft mint, with
a large soft-edged organic arc/wave shape in a deeper tone of [BACKGROUND]
sweeping across the upper-middle portion of the frame. Top-center holds the
[BRAND] logotype in [DARK BRAND COLOR]. Directly below it, a bold oversized
headline stacked on two lines reads [SHORT HEADLINE, e.g. dollar amount +
