"SAVINGS"] in large [DARK BRAND COLOR] serif or bold sans type, dominating
the upper third. Beneath the headline, a single-line subhead in smaller
medium-weight type states [YOUR SUBHEAD summarizing the bundle contents,
one sentence]. A thin curved arrow in [ACCENT COLOR] points diagonally
downward-left from the subhead toward the product grouping, guiding the
eye. The lower half of the frame shows [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging
details] arranged as a lineup of 3-4 items in ascending height order,
front-facing, resting on an invisible baseline near the bottom edge, softly
shadowed beneath each item. To the right of the product lineup, overlapping
it slightly, sits [BONUS ITEM like a woven tote or gift-with-purchase
accessory] rendered in warm neutral tones as the free-gift anchor. Lighting
is soft, even, studio-bright with no harsh shadows, giving a clean
e-commerce-promo feel. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and
props only. The arrangement should feel like unwrapping a well-earned deal
— generous, clear-value, and irresistible at a glance, making the savings
feel tangible before a single word of body copy is read.

templates.name: Warehouse Stock Discount Banner
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Fill the entire frame edge-to-edge
with a tightly packed, slightly overhead stack of [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details] shown in its retail carton, repeated in soft focus across
the whole background as if freshly packed in a fulfillment warehouse — cartons
overlapping at varied angles, warm ambient light, shallow depth of field so
the outer cartons blur while a few stay crisp. In the upper-third, overlay a
bold offer banner: a [ACCENT COLOR] rectangular badge with white sans-serif
type reading "[URGENCY PHRASE like Subscribe and save!] [DISCOUNT like 15%]
OFF" set in two tight lines, banner edges bleeding off the left and right
sides of the frame. Center-lower third, place one hero carton of [YOUR PRODUCT
with key packaging details] upright and in sharp focus, angled slightly toward
camera, resting on top of the stacked cartons so it reads as the featured
unit. Small italic disclaimer text ("[CLAIM like individual results may vary]")
sits quietly just below the hero carton in low-contrast gray. A solid
[DARK BRAND COLOR] bar spans the full width at the very bottom of the frame,
holding a centered line of bold white text: "+ [YOUR OFFER like Free
Shipping]". The scene should feel like a real fulfillment-center moment —
abundant, freshly packed stock ready to ship — signaling high demand and
trustworthy supply rather than a staged studio shot. No people, hands, faces,
or body parts — product and props only.

templates.name: Floating Product Offer Banner
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical 9:16 studio composition
on a soft [BACKGROUND] gradient that fades from pale [SOFT BRAND COLOR like lavender/light purple] at the top to a slightly deeper tone at the bottom, evoking a clean, airy, premium showroom feel. Top 12% of the frame centers the [BRAND] wordmark logo in [DARK BRAND COLOR], small and understated. Middle third is a bold offer stack, center-aligned: a large [SHORT HEADLINE] in heavy [DARK BRAND COLOR] type spanning two lines, followed by a smaller one-line [YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] beneath it that folds in [YOUR OFFER like Free Shipping over $50] and a bolded [PROMO CODE]. Bottom half of the frame showcases [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] arranged as two hero units overlapping at a slight diagonal angle, resting on an unseen glossy surface that casts a soft mirrored reflection directly beneath them, fading to transparent. Lighting is soft, diffuse, and directionless, studio-clean with gentle shadow falloff, shallow depth of field on the trailing unit. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only. The composition should feel like a premium retail app promo card — spacious, calm, and confident, letting the offer breathe above a quietly desirable product pairing rather than shouting for attention.

templates.name: Friends Laughing, Price-Slash Offer Banner
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical banner, top 65% shows
three [AUDIENCE PERSON] laughing together outdoors in soft natural daylight,
mid-torso up, cropped close, candid and joyful, shot handheld-style at
35-50mm with shallow depth of field and a blurred green outdoor background.
Cast them enjoying [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] together or
using it in the moment — genuine laughter, eyes crinkled, natural gestures,
not posed model energy. Bottom 35% is a solid [ACCENT COLOR like
lime-green] rectangular panel overlaid on the image, containing centered
text in bold sans-serif: a white pill-shaped badge with [BRAND] logo above,
then a white line reading "[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence]", then dark bold
text "[YOUR OFFER like OUR HIGH PROTEIN MEAL PLAN]" with a struck-through
original price "WAS [PRICE]" in red, followed by an oversized bold dark
price line "JUST [DISCOUNTED PRICE]". The scene should feel like a candid
moment between close friends sharing something they genuinely enjoy —
warm, social, unscripted, and inviting rather than staged. Cast
[AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Their
expression and posture should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not
a model selling a product. Bright, cheerful, high-contrast color grading.

templates.name: Trio Lineup Offer Banner
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR]
vertical gradient background (deeper tone at top and bottom, lighter tone at
mid-canvas). Top 20% is a clean text zone: centered [BRAND] logo/wordmark in
[CONTRAST COLOR], with [DISCOUNT like 20% Off] as a large bold headline directly
beneath it, and [YOUR OFFER like Your First Purchase With Email Sign-Up] as a
smaller supporting line under that — all centered, generous negative space
around the type. Middle 55% of the canvas is the hero zone: three units of
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] arranged in a tight overlapping row,
each in a different [ACCENT COLOR] colorway to signal variety, shot straight-on
at eye level, softly cropped at the bottom edge of the frame so they appear to
rise up from below. Even, soft diffuse studio lighting with minimal shadow,
shallow depth of field keeping all three products in crisp focus against the
soft gradient. Bottom 10% holds a single small centered line of fine-print
disclaimer text in a muted, low-contrast tone: [PRODUCT] — [YOUR SUBHEAD, one
sentence]. The scene should feel like a clean retail window display — confident,
uncluttered, and premium, letting the product variety and the offer do all the
persuading with zero visual noise. No people, hands, faces, or body parts —
product and props only.

templates.name: Beach Bundle Offer — Lifestyle Silhouette + Bag Reveal
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical full-bleed lifestyle
photo: a sun-drenched beach or coastal scene fills the entire frame as the
background, with soft ocean and greenery visible behind. In the upper two
thirds, crop tight on [AUDIENCE PERSON] shown from behind/side at the hip
and shoulder — candid, cropped, not face-forward — with [YOUR PRODUCT with
key packaging details] arranged in a tidy vertical cluster resting against
her hip/forearm as though just pulled from a bag, sizes and shapes varied
but grouped tightly together. A small circular callout badge sits near the
product cluster reading [YOUR OFFER like Exclusive Free Gift], connected to
the products with a thin curved arrow. Bottom third of frame is a woven/
textured tote or bag partially in frame, softly out of focus, grounding the
scene in a real beach-day moment. Directly beneath the photo, bold stacked
all-caps headline in [CONTRAST COLOR] reads [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words],
followed by a shorter line in [ACCENT COLOR] stating [YOUR OFFER like Bundle
4 Full-Size Items & Save $DISCOUNT]. Natural warm directional sunlight, shallow
depth of field, editorial-lifestyle color grade, bright and breezy. Cast
[AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her posture
and the candid, unposed framing should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY],
not a model selling a product. The scene should feel like a real, sun-kissed
beach day where reaching for this bundle is second nature — effortless,
sun-ready, and worth celebrating with a bonus gift.

templates.name: Bathroom Flat-Lay Member Offer Banner
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical 9:16 offer banner. Top
30% of frame is a bold two-line headline in oversized [CONTRAST COLOR] block
type set on a [ACCENT COLOR] highlight bar, tilted slightly for energy,
reading [SHORT HEADLINE, under 4 words]. Just below it, an oval or circular
badge in [ACCENT COLOR] contains the price offer in large type: [YOUR OFFER
like FOR ONLY $15.00]. The remaining 65% of the frame is a full-bleed
lifestyle-adjacent surface shot: [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details]
arranged in a confident lineup on a [SURFACE like a mosaic-tiled countertop],
backed by a [SETTING like a tiled bathroom wall with soft round mirror edge
visible]. Products stand upright, evenly spaced, front labels facing camera,
styled like a proud "starter kit" reveal. Soft, even studio-bright lighting
with a slight cool undertone, shallow shadows grounding each item on the
surface. Palette leans on [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] packaging against a naturally
patterned, textured backdrop for contrast. The scene should feel like someone
just unboxed their first shipment and lined everything up on the counter to
admire it — proud, official, "I'm officially a member now" energy. No people,
hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only. Leave clear negative
space in the badge and headline zones for text legibility.

templates.name: Free Gift With Purchase — Pedestal Hero Banner
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed solid [BACKGROUND]
backdrop in a soft monochrome tone, no texture or gradient breaks. Top 35%
of the frame is a clean text stack, center-aligned: small [BRAND] logo
lockup at the very top, then a bold italic-leaning [SHORT HEADLINE]
announcing the free gift in a bright [ACCENT COLOR] that pops against the
background, followed by a smaller two-line [YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence]
in [CONTRAST COLOR] stating the purchase condition. Center 55% of the
frame holds [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] standing upright,
placed dead-center on a small transparent acrylic riser block that lifts
it slightly off the floor of the frame. A [YOUR OFFER like Free Shipping
over $50]-style bonus item (the free gift being promoted) rests draped or
clipped against the base of the product or the riser, rendered as a small
tactile accessory in a contrasting saturated color so it reads as a
distinct bonus object, not part of the core product. Soft, even studio
light from the front-top, gentle shadow beneath the riser, shallow depth
of field keeping product and gift both crisp. No people, hands, faces, or
body parts — product and props only. The composition should feel like a
limited-time gift-with-purchase reveal — generous, covetable, and
slightly playful, as if the bonus item is the surprise the eye finds
after the headline. Leave bottom 10% clear for [CTA like SHOP NOW] or
disclaimer text.

templates.name: Looks Familiar? — Clinic Collage Pattern Interrupt
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed macro close-up of
[AUDIENCE PERSON]'s face fills the entire frame as the background layer,
skin texture and fine lines visible in soft, slightly desaturated light.
Collaged on top, scattered across the upper half and middle of the frame,
are 4-5 smaller cutout photos of clinicians in white coats performing
in-office aesthetic procedures on the same face — handheld devices, wands,
and tools held close to the skin — each cutout with a clean drop shadow so
it reads as a pasted photo, not part of the base scene. Bold serif
headline "[SHORT HEADLINE]" sits upper-left in [CONTRAST COLOR], with
[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] directly beneath it in a lighter weight. In
the lower third, [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] appears as two
small rendered units near a pink pill-shaped badge reading
"[YOUR OFFER like Buy 1 Get 1 Free]" in [ACCENT COLOR]. Below that, one
closing line of body copy states the product's core mechanism claim in
plain, confident language. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] as the base face and
[PERSON DESCRIPTION like clinicians in white coats] for the collaged
procedure photos — expressions neutral and clinical, not smiling, to
sell credibility over aspiration. The scene should feel like flipping
through your own procedure history and recognizing every needle, wand,
and treatment you've already tried — mildly unsettling, a little exposed,
and searching for a better way. Overall palette is warm neutral skin
tones with clinical white coats, punctuated by one saturated
[ACCENT COLOR] pop for the offer badge and CTA. Lighting is soft,
diffuse, documentary-style on the base face; the collaged clinic photos
can carry slightly harsher, more clinical light to visually separate them
as "memories" layered on top.

templates.name: Sliced-Surface Product Reveal
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Center-frame a single unexpected
organic object — cut or sliced in cross-section so its curved, textured
interior fills roughly 55% of the canvas — set against a solid, saturated
[BACKGROUND] color field with no gradient or texture. [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details] is arranged draped, pinned, or resting directly on the
exposed cross-section surface, as if the object itself were a display
pedestal: pieces cascade from the upper-left curve down toward the
lower-right in a loose diagonal line, spaced with clear negative space
between each so every unit reads individually. Lighting is soft, even, and
diffuse from the front, casting minimal shadow so texture and color contrast
between the object surface and the product are crisp. The bottom 15-20% of
the canvas is left as a clean solid-color margin for two left-aligned text
lines: [BRAND] in bold caps, and [SHORT HEADLINE] beneath it in a lighter
weight, both in a color that contrasts against [BACKGROUND]. No people,
hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only. The scene should feel
like a visual double-take — a familiar everyday object repurposed as a
surreal display stand, creating a beat of confusion-then-delight that makes
the viewer stop scrolling to understand what they're looking at before the
product itself even registers. Mood: playful, tactile, unexpected, elevated.

templates.name: Absurdist Desk-on-the-Beach Defiance
templates.template: Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors, typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a surreal, deadpan scene: an office desk setup — [OFFICE PROP like a boxy CRT monitor], a stacked pile of books, a small potted plant or trinket, and a desk chair — has been planted directly in shallow ocean water on a wet sand beach, waves lapping at the desk legs. A person stands barefoot on top of the desk, shot from behind at a slight angle so their face is not visible, wearing [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] worn on their back exactly as intended, gazing out at the horizon where grey sky meets sea. The overall palette is desaturated, overcast, and cool — flat diffuse daylight with no strong shadows, evoking documentary realism rather than a polished studio shot. Left two-thirds of the frame is negative space (sea and sky) reserved for bold stacked headline copy in [DARK BRAND COLOR]; a small "NEW" badge in [ACCENT COLOR] plus a one-line product label sits in the upper right, clear of the horizon line. The scene narrative: this should feel like a quiet act of rebellion — someone has physically dragged their entire desk out of the office and into the wild, choosing open water over four walls, and [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] is what goes with them either way. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Their posture, faced away and still, should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. Mood: [MOOD].

templates.name: Pattern Interrupt — Body-as-Surface Visual Gag
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Frame a tight, cropped shot of
[AUDIENCE PERSON]'s bare torso/midsection filling roughly the top 70% of the
canvas, lit with soft, warm, slightly overexposed natural light so skin tone
reads bright and the mood feels candid rather than staged. Turn an everyday
food or object into an unexpected visual gag arranged directly on the body's
surface, held in place by [AUDIENCE PERSON]'s own hand or a utensil, so the
composition reads as a jarring, almost absurd sight-gag rather than a
traditional product shot. [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] rests
in-frame, held or placed against the body near the visual gag, close enough
to feel like it belongs to the moment, not staged beside it. The bottom 25-30%
of the canvas is a solid dark, near-black panel that breaks hard from the
photo above — no gradient, no blend — creating a jarring below-the-fold
contrast. On that panel, bold white [SHORT HEADLINE] sits top-left, followed
directly beneath by a smaller [YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] in a lighter
weight. A single line of fine-print disclaimer/legal text sits at the very
bottom edge in low-contrast gray. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with
[AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her framing should feel unposed and real, cropped
at the neck and thighs so the visual gag and product stay the sole focus, not
a model selling a product. The scene should feel deliberately uncomfortable
for a half-second — a visual pattern interrupt engineered to stop the scroll
before resolving into a clear, reassuring product message.

templates.name: Sticky Note Confession — Back Pocket Candid
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Tight cropped candid shot of
[AUDIENCE PERSON], framed from mid-back to upper-thigh, faceless — no head or
face in frame, only torso, arms, and hips visible. A small, slightly crumpled
handwritten note is tucked into the back pocket of their pants at a natural
angle, edges lifting realistically as if just pressed in. On the note, in
casual all-caps handwritten marker lettering across multiple short lines:
"[YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words]." The note is a plain [BACKGROUND] paper
square and is the only text element anywhere in frame — no logos, no other
graphics. Clothing is ordinary and unstaged, with visible natural fabric
texture (denim, cotton) and a small brand tag or label detail on the garment
for realism. Lighting is soft, diffuse daylight — like a quick phone photo
taken at home, not a studio setup. Shallow depth of field keeps the note in
crisp focus while the body and background soften slightly at the edges. The
note fills roughly 20-25% of the frame, positioned in the lower two-thirds,
slightly off-center. [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] does not need
to appear in frame; the note itself carries the entire message. The scene
should feel like a candid, almost-caught-you moment — as if the viewer just
noticed something funny and true stuck onto a stranger, not a staged
advertisement. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with
[AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Their posture and the mundane, everyday setting
should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product.

templates.name: Sticky Note Kitchen Flat-Lay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical flat-lay composition on
a light [SURFACE like a pale marble kitchen counter]. Top 60% of the frame:
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] shown partially opened with a few
units spilling naturally out of the box onto the counter, styled like a
real, just-opened kitchen item rather than a staged studio shot. A circular
badge in the upper-left third contains [BRAND] logo and [PRODUCT CATEGORY]
name on a [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] background. In the upper-right third, a
bright [ACCENT COLOR like sunny yellow] sticky note sits at a slight
natural tilt, edges softly curled, with [SHORT HEADLINE, under 6 words]
written in a casual, handwritten-style font as if jotted down by a real
person. Bottom 40% of the frame transitions to a softly overhead, slightly
diffused shot of the product's real-world use context relevant to
[PRODUCT CATEGORY] (e.g. the environment or surface where it gets used),
shot from directly above with even, bright daylight. No people, hands,
faces, or body parts — product and props only. The scene should feel like a
genuine, unscripted moment of someone jotting a quick personal recommendation
right after trying the product, rather than a designed advertisement — candid,
trustworthy, word-of-mouth energy. [MOOD].

templates.name: Sticky Note Price Flat-Lay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Overhead flat-lay on a clean
[BACKGROUND] surface. Scatter 4-6 units of [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging
details] radially around the frame at varying angles, as if casually set down
— filling roughly 70% of the canvas edge to edge with visible variety in
color or finish across units. In the center-left, place a single square
[ACCENT COLOR like sunshine yellow] sticky note lying flat on the surface,
slightly overlapping one or two products, with a faint soft-edged drop
shadow suggesting it's peeled and placed by hand. On the sticky note render
handwritten-marker-style lettering reading "[YOUR OFFER like Just $10 with
free shipping]" in [DARK BRAND COLOR] ink, imperfect and human, not a
typeset font. Lighting is soft, even, diffuse from directly above, minimal
shadow, true-to-color studio quality with a slight candid/desk-photo
informality rather than glossy polish. No people, hands, faces, or body
parts — product and props only. The arrangement should feel like a quick,
personal reminder note left right where the products live — casual,
unstaged, and trustworthy, like a friend jotted down a deal instead of a
marketing team designing an ad.

templates.name: Sticky Note Callouts on Hero Box
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Center-left of the frame,
place [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] as the hero object, large
and upright, resting on [SURFACE] against a clean [SETTING like a bright
modern kitchen with white subway tile]. Stick two to three yellow Post-it
notes directly onto the top-front face of the product, each bearing a
short handwritten-style phrase from [BENEFIT 1-4] in dark marker script,
slightly overlapping and tilted at different casual angles as if
hand-placed. In the lower-right foreground, add a secondary product or
accessory item related to [PRODUCT CATEGORY], slightly softer focus, with
one additional Post-it note reading [YOUR OFFER like Free Gift Included]
stuck on top of it. In the upper-right background, include a softly
out-of-focus prop cluster (utensil holder, small plant, or category-
relevant accessory) that reinforces the everyday, lived-in setting without
competing with the hero product. Lighting is soft, even, diffuse studio
light with a warm neutral color grade; shallow depth of field keeps the
hero product and its Post-its in crisp focus while background elements
fall gently out of focus. Small fine-print [DISCLAIMER TEXT] sits bottom-
center in a thin sans-serif. The scene should feel like a real customer
excitedly annotated their own delivery with quick handwritten notes,
flagging exactly what's new and what's included — spontaneous, personal,
and self-authored rather than studio-copywritten. No people, hands,
faces, or body parts — product and props only.

templates.name: Sticky Note Confession — Bathroom Shelf Hero
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a vertical bathroom-counter
scene. Bottom 55% shows a white subway-tile surface with [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details] standing upright and in sharp focus, centered-to-left of
frame. Soft-focus bathroom props flank the edges — a folded towel, a drinking
glass, a soap pump bottle, a small jar lid — suggesting a real, lived-in
bathroom, not a studio set. Top 30% of frame shows a bright [ACCENT COLOR like
sunny yellow] Post-it note stuck directly to the tile wall just above the
product, slightly curling at one corner, in a casual handwritten marker font
reading "[SHORT HEADLINE]" as if scrawled as a personal reminder to self. Soft,
diffuse daylight from the upper left, shallow depth of field on background
objects, sharp focus held on the note and the product. The scene should feel
like someone left themselves an honest, unfiltered note in their own bathroom
and set the product right below it as the quiet answer — intimate, low-fi, and
trustworthy rather than staged or salesy. No people, hands, faces, or body
parts — product and props only.

templates.name: Sticky Note Confession Flat-Lay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Overhead flat-lay shot at a true
top-down angle on a softly textured striped fabric surface (like a bed or
pajama set), suggesting a bedside or morning-routine setting. Upper-left-of-center:
a single [SOFT BRAND COLOR like mint/teal] square sticky note, slightly
tilted, with a short handwritten-marker headline in dark ink reading
[YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] with a small hand-doodled accent (heart, star,
or check). The sticky note's bottom-right corner overlaps and partially covers
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details], fanned or slightly stacked, resting
directly on the fabric in the lower-right quadrant, with any printed label
copy soft and secondary to the sticky note. Lighting is soft, natural, diffuse
window light from one side, casting a faint realistic shadow beneath both
objects — candid phone-photo energy, not a polished studio shot, slightly
imperfect framing. Tiny disclaimer-style text sits small and unobtrusive at the
very bottom of the frame. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and
props only. The scene should feel like a private, funny confession jotted down
in a spare moment — relatable, unposed, and personal, like a real customer
left themselves a note next to their daily essential.

templates.name: Sticky Note Discount Reveal — Product Trio Flat Lay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Arrange a trio of [YOUR PRODUCT
with key packaging details] on a warm wood tabletop surface, staggered in
depth with one item slightly blurred in the back-left, one centered mid-
ground, and one sharp in the back-right — all in [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR].
Background is softly out-of-focus, hinting at a bright indoor setting with a
window and exposed brick or textured wall, keeping focus on the products.
In the lower-center foreground, a hand holds up a single square [ACCENT
COLOR like soft lavender/purple] sticky note at a slight tilt, angled toward
camera, large enough to cover roughly 30% of the frame. Handwritten-style
dark marker text fills the note across 4-5 short lines: [URGENCY PHRASE or
OFFER like "UNLOCK [DISCOUNT] OFF WHEN YOU CHOOSE SUBSCRIPTION"]. The note
partially overlaps the products, as if just placed in front of them. Shot
at 50mm, soft natural window light from the left, shallow depth of field
isolating the note and nearest product. No face or body visible — only a
single hand holding the note; product and props only otherwise. The scene
should feel like a quick, candid reminder someone left themselves — an
in-the-moment nudge to grab a deal on a product they already trust, not a
polished studio ad.

templates.name: Handheld Sticky-Note Verdict (Outdoor UGC)
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical frame: a hand and forearm
enter from the bottom-right, fingers pinching [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging
details] upright, positioned directly above a small square [SOFT BRAND COLOR
like pastel yellow] sticky note held in the same hand. The sticky note sits
in the lower-third of the frame, angled slightly, with [YOUR SUBHEAD, one
sentence] handwritten across it in casual dark marker-style script on two
lines. The background is a wide-open outdoor scene in soft, overcast natural
light — [SETTING like a quiet suburban street in winter] — thrown out of
focus with heavy bokeh so the hand and note stay the sharpest plane in the
shot. Shot at roughly 35mm equivalent, eye-level, shallow depth of field,
candid smartphone-photo energy rather than studio polish. The scene should
feel like a spontaneous, authentic moment of someone pausing mid-day to jot
down and show off a real result they noticed — unscripted, believable,
peer-to-peer rather than staged advertising. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] —
someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her hand's casual grip and the
unfiltered outdoor backdrop should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY],
not a model selling a product. Keep color grading natural and slightly cool,
true to daylight, with no added filters.

templates.name: Sticky Note Testimonial on Opened Product Box
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Overhead flat-lay shot looking
down at an opened [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] shipping box
resting on a [SURFACE]. The box lid is folded open behind the product,
revealing branded interior print and a die-cut circular cutout in the inner
sleeve. [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] sits nestled inside the
box, partially visible with the [BRAND] wordmark readable along the box
interior. A bright [ACCENT COLOR like neon yellow] square sticky note is
placed diagonally on top of the box, angled slightly off-grid and casting a
soft natural shadow, as if just stuck there by hand. On the sticky note, a
candid handwritten-marker note reads "[PULL-QUOTE — the most emotional 4-8
word phrase from the review]," with the key result word underlined for
emphasis. Lighting is soft, warm, natural daylight from the upper left,
shallow depth of field so the box interior and surface blur gently at the
edges while the sticky note stays in sharp focus. No people, hands, faces,
or body parts — product and props only. The scene should feel like a real
customer paused mid-unboxing, so struck by their result that they scrawled
it on a sticky note and left it right on top of the product as unfiltered,
unstaged proof.

templates.name: Sticky Note Benefit Callouts — Kitchen Counter Hero
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Center-frame [YOUR PRODUCT with
key packaging details] standing upright on a warm wood kitchen countertop,
positioned in the lower two-thirds of the frame, with a softly blurred
kitchen background (cabinets, shelving) behind it. A hand enters from the
top-right edge of the frame holding one unit of [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details] lifted slightly above and in front of the main product,
as if just removed from the pack — cropped at the wrist, no face or body
visible. Surround the product with three bright yellow square sticky notes
in bold, handwritten-style black marker capital letters, angled slightly
off-axis and placed at the top-left, mid-left, and bottom-right of the
frame, each calling out one short benefit: [BENEFIT 1-4]. Keep each note to
2-4 words, all-caps, no punctuation, marker-style lettering. Lighting is
bright, even, and natural, as if from a nearby window, giving a candid
"someone just labeled their kitchen staple" feel rather than a polished
studio shot. The scene should feel like a real person annotating their own
pantry favorite to show a friend exactly why they love it — practical,
honest, and enthusiastic, not staged or corporate. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] —
someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. The hand and gesture should
evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. Shot
at eye level, 50mm equivalent, shallow depth of field so the background
kitchen softly falls away while the product and notes stay crisp.

templates.name: Sticky Note Testimonial Flat-Lay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Shoot a candid tabletop scene from
a slightly elevated angle: [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] stands
upright on a round wooden side table, positioned in the right 55% of the
frame. Leaning against the table on the left 40%, propped at a slight angle
as if just stuck there, is an oversized [ACCENT COLOR like soft pink] sticky
note bearing a handwritten-style testimonial in bold black marker script:
[PULL-QUOTE — the most emotional 4-8 word phrase from the review] and
[FULL QUOTE 2-3 sentences]. Background is a softly blurred, bright neutral
interior — pale wall, hint of a decorative object (mirrored disco-ball lamp
or similar reflective accent) out of focus in the upper left, suggesting a
lived-in home rather than a studio. Natural soft daylight from the left,
shallow depth of field (85mm f/2.8 feel) so the product and note stay crisp
while the room falls away. The scene should feel like an unscripted glimpse
into someone's actual living room — like a real customer set their product
down after writing this note about it, unpolished and believable, not staged
for an ad. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only.
Small blurred watermark-style text may sit in the lower corner as if this is
a screenshot from a review site.

templates.name: Hand-Held Reveal with Sticky Note Offer
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. A hand and forearm enter from the
lower-left, holding [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] upright at
center-frame so the front label faces the camera dead-on. A bright
[ACCENT COLOR like sunflower yellow] sticky note is pressed to the lower-right
corner of the product, angled slightly, with a bold handwritten-style marker
message reading [YOUR OFFER like 50% OFF YOUR FIRST BOX]. Background is a
softly blurred, warm indoor setting with green leafy plants framing both
sides, natural daylight falling across the scene, shallow depth of field so
only the product label and sticky note stay sharp. The composition should
feel like someone just pulled this product out for the camera to share a
personal find, casual and unstaged, not art-directed. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] —
someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her posture and the natural,
unposed way she holds the product should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL
IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. No visible face — only hand, arm,
and product are in frame.

templates.name: Sticky-Note Callout Box Flat-Lay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Overhead-angled flat-lay looking
down into an open [BRAND] product box lined with white kraft packaging paper,
shot close and cropped tight so the box fills the frame edge-to-edge. The
[BRAND] wordmark is printed oversized across the back panel of the box in
white, partially cropped at the top of the frame. Inside the box, [YOUR
PRODUCT with key packaging details] is arranged left and right, standing
upright in the box's molded compartments, labels facing forward and fully
legible. Between the two products, a round protective insert or accessory sits
in its own cutout, wrapped in translucent protective film. Two square sticky
notes are laid diagonally overlapping the products: one [ACCENT COLOR like
neon yellow-green] sticky note near center-left handwritten in bold black
marker-style capital letters with [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words], and one
[CONTRAST COLOR like hot pink] sticky note upper-right handwritten in bold
black marker-style capital letters with [CTA like GET 20% OFF TODAY]. Both
notes appear slightly askew, corners lifted, as if just pressed on by hand —
casual, unpolished, DIY energy. Even, bright, slightly cool studio lighting
with soft shadows pooling at the box's inner edges; no glare on the plastic
wrap. The scene should feel like a real customer just tore into their
delivery box and slapped a candid, no-filter tip and a limited-time deal
note onto their own product photo — DIY, spontaneous, and screenshot-native,
not brand-polished. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and
props only.

templates.name: Sticky Note Peer Recommendation Flat-Lay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Top-down flat-lay on a
[SOFT BRAND COLOR like blush pink] round tabletop surface set against a
warm wood background visible at the edges. Left-center of frame: [YOUR
PRODUCT with key packaging details] shown both in its retail box (angled
slightly) and removed from the box resting beside it, so the product
form is clearly visible. Right of the product, slightly overlapping the
edge of the round surface: a bright [ACCENT COLOR like tangerine orange]
square sticky note with a handwritten-style short note in black marker
reading "[PULL-QUOTE — the most emotional 4-8 word phrase from the
review]" with one word underlined or starred for emphasis. Soft, even
overhead studio light, minimal shadow, crisp focus across the whole
scene, slight desaturated matte finish for an authentic non-staged feel.
No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only. The
scene should feel like a trusted friend jotted down a spontaneous,
excited recommendation and left it right next to the product on your
desk — candid, word-of-mouth, and instantly believable rather than a
polished studio ad.

templates.name: POV Action Moment — Product Stays in Place
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed vertical action shot:
[AUDIENCE PERSON] stands confidently on a board cutting across open water,
arms thrown up in a triumphant celebratory pose, wind and spray moving
through hair and a sheer flowing cover-up. Overcast sky and city skyline
with palm trees fill the upper third; teal-green wake water fills the
bottom third. Bold white sans-serif caption text is centered in the top
15% of the frame over the sky, styled as a native social caption starting
with "POV:" — reserve this zone for [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words]
framed as a first-person realization. She is wearing or using [YOUR
PRODUCT with key packaging details] naturally as part of the outfit/moment,
not held up or presented to camera. Shot handheld/POV style from a trailing
boat or drone, natural daylight, slightly desaturated, authentic UGC
motion-blur energy rather than polished studio lighting. The scene should
feel like a spontaneous, victorious real-life moment — someone doing
something physical and bold and discovering, mid-motion, that [YOUR
PRODUCT] simply performs and doesn't let them down. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON]
— someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her expression and posture
should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a
product.

templates.name: Native News Article Reveal
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed lifestyle photo fills
the entire frame: a candid, real-life moment where [PERSON DESCRIPTION like
man in his 30s driving a car] is actively using or reaching toward [YOUR
PRODUCT with key packaging details], shot from an over-the-shoulder or
interior POV angle so the moment feels observed, not staged. Natural window
light, slightly candid framing, mid-depth of field so the environment
(street, room, or vehicle interior) stays recognizable but secondary. Over
the bottom 40% of the photo, overlay a fake news/editorial website module:
a thin top bar with a hamburger icon, a bold wordmark reading
"[SHORT HEADLINE]" and a search icon, sitting on a white background. Below
the bar, a large bold black headline in a serif or heavy sans typeface —
"[YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words]" — followed by a smaller all-caps gray
subhead line: "[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence]". Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] —
someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Their posture and the framing
should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a
product. The scene should feel like an ordinary, unremarkable moment
someone stumbled into mid-scroll — not an ad, but a real news story they
were about to click on, with the product simply part of their everyday
environment.

templates.name: Press Article Mockup — Headline Over In-Use Photo
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a mock news/editorial
article screenshot. Top 8% is a browser-style nav bar on white: a hamburger
icon at left, [BRAND] wordmark logo centered with a small icon mark, and a
search icon at right, separated by a thin hairline rule. Below the nav, a
white panel occupies the top 38% of the frame: a small [ACCENT COLOR]
category label (e.g. topic/vertical tag) sits above a bold black headline in
[YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] set in a tight serif-free news typeface, 3
lines max, followed by a lighter-weight one-line subhead in
[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] in gray. Bottom 62% is a full-bleed lifestyle
photograph showing [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] actively in use,
tight-cropped on the torso/limb area relevant to its use-case, shot outdoors
in natural daylight with a softly blurred green/neutral environment behind,
believable and candid rather than posed, mid-day sun, shallow depth of field
isolating the product against the blurred backdrop. The scene should feel
like a real moment lifted from an editorial story — a trusted publication
showing a genuine person mid-activity, not a staged ad — lending the product
third-party credibility and authority. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone
living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Their posture and framing should evoke
[AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], captured candidly rather than posed for a
camera, reinforcing that this is editorial coverage, not paid promotion.

templates.name: Press Endorsement Card — Publication Quote + Product Shot
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a press-endorsement card
on a soft neutral [BACKGROUND] canvas. A rounded-corner white card fills
roughly 85% of the frame, sitting centered with even margin on all sides.
Top-left of the card shows a [PUBLICATION LOGO/WORDMARK] in bold serif
lettering; top-right shows a small hamburger-menu icon to suggest an
authentic editorial/web-article clipping. Below the masthead, centered body
text presents a [PULL-QUOTE — the most emotional 6-12 word phrase praising
the product] in clean sans-serif, followed on the next line by an em-dash
attribution "— [NAME]". Below the quote, the lower half of the card contains
a bright, evenly-lit lifestyle product photograph: [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details] arranged neatly on a [SURFACE], with a softly blurred
[SETTING like a modern kitchen backsplash] behind it. Lighting is bright,
even, and editorial — no harsh shadows, true-to-life color. No people,
hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only. The scene should feel
like a real magazine or online editorial has spontaneously featured and
praised the product — credible, aspirational, and third-party validated
rather than brand-authored.

templates.name: Editorial "Best-Of" Roundup Screenshot
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Recreate the look of a screenshotted
editorial "best-of" roundup article. Top 20% of the frame shows a masthead-style
publication logotype in [PUBLICATION NAME] serif wordmark, centered on a plain
white background. Below it, a bold serif article headline reads "[ARTICLE
HEADLINE like The 13 Very Best Down Comforters]", followed by a smaller italic
subhead line reading "[SUBHEAD CATEGORY LABEL like Best lightweight option]" in
regular sans-serif. Below the subhead, a clean editorial product card occupies
the lower half: a small square product thumbnail on the left third showing
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] photographed as a plain studio
product shot on a light neutral background, with a small heart/save icon
in the top-right corner of the thumbnail. To the right of the thumbnail, in a
two-column text block, show the bold product name "[PRODUCT]" by "[BRAND]"
stacked over a plain-text price "[PRICE like $399]", followed by a short line
of bolded spec labels and values in a compact list format such as
"[SPEC LABEL 1]: [SPEC VALUE 1] | [SPEC LABEL 2]: [SPEC VALUE 2] |
[SPEC LABEL 3]: [SPEC VALUE 3]". Entire composition sits on a pure white
background with generous margins, no photography lighting effects, flat
even illumination like a web page screenshot, thin hairline dividers between
sections, black text on white, no color accents beyond the product itself.
The scene should feel like a trusted third-party editorial source has
independently vetted and ranked [YOUR PRODUCT] among the best in its
category — credible, understated, and stripped of any promotional tone.
No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only.

templates.name: Media Feature — "As Seen In" Editorial Screenshot
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a mock editorial article
screenshot styled as authority press coverage. Top 22% of the frame is a soft
neutral [BACKGROUND] margin containing a bold publication masthead/logo
lockup, e.g. "[PUBLICATION LOGO/WORDMARK]", left-aligned on a clean white
strip. Below it, a thin hairline divider separates the masthead from the
article body. The article body occupies the remaining canvas on a white
background: a large bold serif or sans headline reads "[ARTICLE HEADLINE,
under 12 words, gift-guide or roundup style]", followed by a small byline row
in gray uppercase text — "[AUTHOR NAME(S)]" on the left and "[PUBLISH DATE]"
on the right. Beneath the byline, one short paragraph of editorial body copy
in dark gray serif/sans text references the product naturally as an editor's
pick, ending mid-sentence to feel like a real clipped article excerpt: "[EDITOR
QUOTE, 1-2 sentences praising the product's key differentiator]". Below the
copy, a large photo occupies the bottom ~45% of the frame: [YOUR PRODUCT with
key packaging details] resting at a slight angle on a warm [SURFACE], shot
from a three-quarter overhead angle, soft natural window light from the left,
shallow depth of field, photographed like a real product-in-use lifestyle
photo rather than a studio render. No people, hands, faces, or body parts —
product and props only. The overall arrangement should feel like credible,
unpaid third-party journalism — an editor genuinely recommending a product
they tested, not an advertisement, lending instant borrowed trust to the
brand.

templates.name: Editorial "As Seen In" Publication Feature
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a press/editorial feature
layout in the style of a beauty magazine roundup article. Top 10% is a bold
masthead bar in [DARK BRAND COLOR] or black with the publication-style wordmark
"[PUBLICATION NAME]" centered in white uppercase serif or sans type. Below it,
on a white background, center-align a bold editorial headline "[YOUR HEADLINE,
under 10 words]" in dark serif type, with a lighter subhead "[YOUR SUBHEAD, one
sentence]" beneath it, followed by a thin horizontal accent-color divider line,
then a small byline and publish date in muted gray uppercase text. Below the
text block, a full-width lifestyle product photograph occupies roughly 40% of
the frame: [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] resting upright on a
[SURFACE], softly lit with diffuse, even, editorial studio lighting against a
[SOFT BRAND COLOR like blush pink] seamless backdrop, shadows soft and minimal,
shot straight-on at eye level like a clean product still life. No people,
hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only. Beneath the photo, a
citation block in white space presents the product name and price in bold
black text, followed by 1–2 sentences of descriptive editorial copy in regular
weight, and two black rectangular CTA buttons side by side reading
"[CTA like BUY ON RETAILER]" in bold white uppercase type. The scene should
feel like a trusted third-party publication is vouching for the product —
credible, editorial, and effortlessly authoritative rather than promotional.

templates.name: Press Quote Overlay on Cropped Lifestyle Motion Shot
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed vertical lifestyle
photo in [MOOD] tones on a seamless [BACKGROUND] studio backdrop. Frame a
tight, cropped composition on [AUDIENCE PERSON] mid-movement — only a partial
view of limbs/body in a stretch or dynamic pose is visible, shot from a low,
intimate angle so the body fills most of the frame and no face is shown.
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] is worn or held naturally as part
of the pose, integrated into the motion rather than posed for display.
Centered in the middle third of the frame, place a solid [CONTRAST COLOR]
rectangular card with generous padding, floating over the photo like a
citation card. Inside it, set a serif-italic pull-quote in [DARK BRAND COLOR]:
"[PULL-QUOTE — the most emotional 4-8 word phrase from the review]," followed
by a smaller serif publication wordmark reading [SOCIAL PROOF] centered
beneath the quote. In the bottom-left corner, overlay the [BRAND] logotype in
[CONTRAST COLOR], stacked in two lines, small and understated. Lighting is
soft, diffuse, and even, evoking an editorial studio feel rather than a
commercial one. The arrangement should feel like a magazine clipping brought
to life — as if a respected publication's words are floating over a real,
unposed moment of movement, lending third-party credibility rather than
salesmanship. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with
[AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her posture and the fragment of body shown should
evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product.

templates.name: Editorial Article Mock — Press Feature with Product Hero
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a mock editorial article
card on a soft blurred gradient background in [SOFT BRAND COLOR like lavender/light purple]
and [ACCENT COLOR]. Center a white rounded-corner article panel occupying about
85% of the frame width, with a thin horizontal rule and a hamburger-menu icon at
the top-left to signal a real publication's website. Above the headline, place a
small pink/red eyebrow label reading [PUBLICATION SECTION like PRODUCT REVIEWS]
followed by an arrow and a second gray category label [PUBLICATION CATEGORY like
HEALTH & LONGEVITY]. Below that, set a large serif [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words]
in near-black text, a small italic [TIMEFRAME]-style dateline, and a short serif
[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] beneath it. In the lower 40% of the panel, place a
full-bleed photograph on a seamless light [BACKGROUND] surface showing
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] resting beside a complementary
food or lifestyle prop that visually echoes [KEY INGREDIENT] or the product's
core benefit, shot at eye-level macro with soft even studio light and a shallow
depth of field so the product is crisp and the prop slightly softer. The
arrangement should feel like a credible third-party product review just
published on a health and wellness site — measured, editorial, and
trustworthy rather than promotional, as if a journalist chose this pairing to
illustrate the story rather than a brand styling its own ad. No people, hands,
faces, or body parts — product and props only.

templates.name: Decision-Tree Quiz Flowchart
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a clean decision-tree
infographic on a plain white background. Top center: bold black headline
"[YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words]" in a simple sans-serif font. Below it, a
thin-outlined oval node containing "[QUIZ QUESTION 1]", with two thin arrows
branching down-left and down-right to two rounded-rectangle answer boxes
reading "[ANSWER OPTION 1]" and "[ANSWER OPTION 2]". From each answer box, a
single arrow drops to a second oval node asking "[QUIZ QUESTION 2A / 2B]",
which again branches into two rounded-rectangle answer boxes with short
answer labels. From each final answer box, an arrow leads down to a small
square image cell (equal size, thin black border, generous white padding)
showing [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] photographed on a plain
white background, isolated, no shadow drama — one distinct product variant
or color per cell so each path resolves to a visually distinct result. All
connecting lines are thin, uniform-weight black arrows on white space; all
text is black on white for maximum legibility; the whole composition reads
top-to-bottom as a symmetrical binary tree with generous breathing room
between nodes. The scene should feel like a quick, playful self-quiz that
helps the viewer discover which exact product variant fits their situation —
approachable, logical, and instantly skimmable rather than salesy. No people,
hands, faces, or body parts — diagram, text nodes, and product images only.
Maintain even spacing and consistent box/oval sizing across all tiers of the
tree.

templates.name: The Right Match Comparison Card
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a "compare and choose" quiz-style
card layout on a [BACKGROUND like warm neutral beige] background. Top 15% shows a
bold headline "[YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words]" in [DARK BRAND COLOR] serif/mixed
type, one word emphasized in bold. Center 55% is a raised, drop-shadowed white
card slightly overlapping two dimmer, desaturated flanking cards (only their edges
visible left and right, cropped at ~15% width each) — this center card is the
"winning" choice: it contains a portrait-style photo of [AUDIENCE PERSON], a small
[BRAND] logo badge with checkmark, and a caption row reading "[SHORT HEADLINE]"
next to a price/value tag "[STAT like $100/hr]". The flanking side cards show
partial glimpses of alternative people with a visible price tag on the right one,
implying rejected options. Below the card row, place a red X icon and a green
checkmark icon side by side to reinforce a right-choice/wrong-choice decision
moment. Bottom 12% is a solid [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] banner with centered white
text: "[CTA like Find your match at any budget]". Lighting is soft, even studio
light, clean editorial product-catalog feel, shallow depth of field on the side
cards to keep focus on the center. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with
[AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her expression and posture should evoke [AUDIENCE
ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. The scene should feel like
the viewer is mid-decision, flipping through options and landing on the one that
finally feels right — reassuring, confident, and worth the price.

templates.name: True or False — Myth-Bust Quiz Hero
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a "True or False?" myth-bust
quiz static. Top 15% of the frame is a large centered serif headline reading
"True or False?" in [DARK BRAND COLOR] over the [BACKGROUND] surface, with a
one-to-two line myth statement in a lighter, smaller serif/sans beneath it
posing a common misconception about [PRODUCT CATEGORY]. Center 45% of the frame
features [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] resting upright on a
textured [SURFACE], lit with dramatic directional side light that casts a soft
natural leaf-shadow pattern across the surface and product, giving the scene an
editorial, almost cinematic stillness. Directly beneath the product, place two
horizontally aligned pill-shaped toggle buttons of equal width: one outlined
"TRUE" button and one solid-filled "FALSE" button in [CONTRAST COLOR], set
against the same [BACKGROUND] tone. The bottom 15% holds two to three lines of
centered body copy that reveals the myth-busting answer, bolding [KEY
INGREDIENT] and [BENEFIT 1-4] within the sentence. The scene should feel like a
quiet, considered moment of discovery — as if a trusted expert is gently
correcting a widely-believed myth rather than selling a product, lending the
brand quiet authority and credibility. No people, hands, faces, or body parts —
product and props only.

templates.name: Quiz Selector — 3-Variant Product Grid
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical layout on a solid
[SOFT BRAND COLOR like lavender/light purple] background. Top 25% holds a
bold, centered two-line headline in [DARK BRAND COLOR]: [YOUR HEADLINE
phrased as a quiz question, under 8 words]. Below it, three evenly spaced
vertical columns span the middle 55% of the frame, each presenting one
variant of [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] shot top-down or
front-on as a clean studio product photo, floating with a soft drop
shadow, no surface or props. Each column has, top to bottom: a small
[ACCENT COLOR] variant label (e.g. under 3 words), the product render,
a simple line-icon in [DARK BRAND COLOR] below it, and a one-line use-case
caption (2-4 words) naming who or when that variant is for. The three
product renders should feel like a matched family — same framing, scale,
and lighting — so the eye compares them instantly. Bottom 12% holds a
centered pill-shaped [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] button with [CTA like Take the
quiz] in white bold text. Lighting is soft, even, shadowless studio light
with no harsh highlights, giving a clean e-commerce catalog feel. The
arrangement should feel like a fast, friendly self-diagnostic moment —
inviting the viewer to see themselves in one of the three options and
tap through to find their match, not a hard sell. No people, hands,
faces, or body parts — product and props only.

templates.name: Handwritten Cardboard Sign + POV Hands-On Demo
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Frame a first-person POV close-up
shot looking down and slightly forward over a cluttered workbench or table
surface. Upper third of frame: a handheld piece of plain cardboard/kraft paper
propped upright, hand-lettered in casual black marker handwriting with
[YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] — the sign should look genuinely handmade, slightly
uneven letter spacing, real marker texture, no digital font. Lower two-thirds
of frame: cropped at the wrist, [AUDIENCE PERSON]'s hand and forearm shown mid-task,
actively holding or using [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] as though
demonstrating it in real use. Surround the hand with a few loose, authentic
supporting props scattered on the surface (tools, small objects relevant to the
task) to sell the "real workspace, not a studio" feeling — not staged, not
symmetrical. Lighting is flat, practical overhead/ambient light, slightly
desaturated and warm, like a candid phone photo, not commercial studio lighting.
Shallow depth of field with the hand and product in sharp focus and background
clutter softly blurred. The scene should feel like an unscripted, real-life
moment of someone quietly proving that a task once considered difficult is now
simple — a lived-in workspace, not a showroom, communicating hard-won ease.
Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. The hand's
posture and steady, confident grip should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY],
not a model demonstrating a product. Sign board is plain cardboard/kraft tone
only — no brand colors on the sign itself, all brand identity lives in the
product and typography callouts overlaid separately if needed.

templates.name: Handheld Proof Sign + Floating Product
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical full-bleed outdoor shot:
a person stands centered in a sunlit parking-lot / crosswalk setting, torso
and arms filling the middle 60% of the frame, holding up a large white
cardboard sign with bold black handwritten marker text reading
[SHORT HEADLINE like #1 Selling Shot at Sprouts] — the sign fully covers
the person's face and head, keeping the subject anonymous and the sign as
the visual anchor. [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] floats or
rests beside the sign at chest height on the right third of the frame,
lit with the same warm natural daylight as the scene, sharp and slightly
larger in scale than life to draw the eye. Background is a sun-bleached
concrete lot with long natural shadows, candid and unstaged — no studio
backdrop. Bottom 15% of the frame is a soft dark gradient overlay carrying
[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] in clean white sans-serif text, with a
white pill-shaped [CTA like LEARN HOW...] button centered beneath it.
The scene should feel like an unscripted, almost dare-like moment of public
proof — as if someone grabbed a marker and cardboard to make an honest,
unpolished claim about a product they genuinely stand behind, not a
polished studio endorsement.

Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON like a woman in her late 20s who values honest,
no-nonsense wellness claims] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY
like skepticism toward overhyped supplement marketing]. Her posture and the
plainness of the handmade sign should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY
like someone who trusts real proof over polish], not a paid model selling
a product.

templates.name: Whiteboard "Before/After" Kitchen Counter Sign
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a full-bleed kitchen
counter scene shot at eye level, 50mm f/4, soft natural window light, warm
neutral tones. Center-frame a white dry-erase whiteboard in a thin metal
frame, propped upright against a kitchen backsplash, filling roughly 70% of
the vertical frame. On the whiteboard, hand-drawn in black marker, show a
simple "before" illustration relating to [KEY INGREDIENT] or [PRODUCT
CATEGORY]'s core benefit labeled "FROM THIS...", an arrow, and an improved
"after" version labeled "TO THIS!" — both doodles in a casual, imperfect,
felt-tip-marker illustration style with one element in [ACCENT COLOR] as a
hand-colored highlight. Below the doodles, bold black hand-lettered marker
text reads [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] referencing [YOUR OFFER like a
timeframe or year]. In the lower-left foreground, resting on a marble
countertop in sharp focus, place [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details]
sized so its label is clearly legible against the whiteboard backdrop.
Background shows a softly blurred domestic kitchen — cabinet edge, stovetop
hint — grounding the scene as a real home, not a studio. The arrangement
should feel like a friend sketched an honest, no-frills explainer on a
kitchen whiteboard to make the case for [PRODUCT] in plain terms — candid,
homemade, and convincing precisely because it isn't polished. No people,
hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only.

templates.name: Handwritten Whiteboard Offer Sign + Companion Pet
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical 9:16 frame shot in a
warm, lived-in indoor space (wood shelving with books/plants softly out of
focus in the background, dark textured floor). Left 55% of the frame holds a
large white handwritten-style sign propped at a slight angle, filled with
bold hand-lettered marker copy: an urgency headline like [URGENCY PHRASE] at
the top in black block letters, a giant colorful hero line [SHORT HEADLINE]
in bright contrasting marker colors (magenta/pink and purple) taking up the
most visual weight, a vertical checklist of 3-4 [BENEFIT 1-4] each preceded
by a hand-drawn checkbox with a checkmark, a bright red accent line calling
out [YOUR OFFER like Free Shipping over $50] or [DISCOUNT like 40%], and a
bottom CTA in bold black caps [CTA like CLAIM NOW!!] paired with a
hand-drawn arrow pointing toward the product on the right. Right 45% of the
frame shows [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] resting directly on
the floor at a friendly, approachable angle, lit with soft ambient light
that matches the room. A small friendly pet (dog or cat) sits naturally
behind or beside the product, facing camera with an alert, happy expression,
grounding the scene in genuine home life rather than a studio set. The scene
should feel like a real person made this sign by hand to flag an offer too
good to scroll past — spontaneous, DIY, and trustworthy rather than
corporate or overly polished. Natural indoor lighting, slight camera
handheld feel, candid framing, no studio gradients or glossy retouching.

templates.name: Handwritten Cardboard Sign — Street Portrait
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical full-bleed photo of
[AUDIENCE PERSON] standing mid-frame on a busy city sidewalk, softly blurred
pedestrians and storefronts behind them for candid depth of field. They hold
a large piece of plain cardboard with both hands at chest height, angled
slightly toward camera, displaying a bold hand-lettered marker message:
"[YOUR HEADLINE, under 12 words, written in a casual all-caps marker
scrawl]". Natural overcast daylight, documentary street-photography feel,
85mm-equivalent framing, shallow depth of field isolating the subject and
sign from the background crowd. A solid-color bar spans the bottom of the
frame in [ACCENT COLOR] holding a short action line "[CTA like Swipe Up to
Check Rates]" in bold white type, with a small fine-print disclaimer line
beneath it. The scene should feel like an ordinary person stopped mid-walk
to make an honest, slightly cheeky confession directly to the viewer —
candid, relatable, and disarmingly sincere rather than polished or staged.
Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Their
expression should be warm, self-aware, and a little wry, like they're letting
the viewer in on something true, evoking [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY]
rather than a paid actor selling a product.

templates.name: Handwritten Whiteboard Comparison Sign
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Center the frame on a dry-erase
whiteboard sign propped upright in a brushed-metal poster frame, resting
against a countertop backsplash, angled slightly and catching soft window
light. The sign fills roughly 65% of the frame, vertically centered, with
a blurred kitchen or home interior (cabinetry, tile backsplash) softly
out of focus behind it. On the whiteboard, render bold black handwritten
marker lettering: a crossed-out rejection line naming the old/competitor
way at top, a small hand-drawn crossed-out icon of [COMPETITOR PRODUCT
FORM like a generic tub], a hand-drawn green checkmark beside a simple
sketch of [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details], and below it a bold
handwritten claim line stating [CLAIM] in 3-4 short stacked lines with one
key phrase underlined for emphasis. Letters should look genuinely
handwritten — uneven stroke weight, slight slant, marker texture — not
typeset. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product sketch and sign
only. The scene should feel like a real, personal desk-side note someone
scrawled to make an honest point to a friend — candid, direct, and
trustworthy rather than polished ad copy. Natural daylight, shallow depth
of field, photographed handheld at eye level, 50mm f/2 look.

templates.name: Whiteboard "Life Advice" Testimonial Sign
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Medium shot of [AUDIENCE PERSON],
seated comfortably in a warm, softly lit living room (neutral wall, a leafy
houseplant to one side, a wooden side table with a lamp on the other), smiling
warmly and looking directly at the camera. They hold a white dry-erase
whiteboard sign at chest height with both hands, angled slightly toward the
lens so every word is legible. The board is hand-lettered in black marker in
a casual, imperfect handwriting style, structured top to bottom as: a bold
title reading "LIFE ADVICE," a small "Name:" and "Age:" line filled in with
[AUDIENCE PERSON]'s name and age, a blank line of space, then a short,
punchy personal-advice statement about [PRODUCT CATEGORY] with the key
benefit phrase underlined for emphasis, and a playful closing line ending in
an exclamation mark. Soft, even, natural-looking indoor light with gentle
shadow falloff; shallow depth of field so the background softly blurs behind
the in-focus subject and sign. The scene should feel like an unscripted,
candid moment of someone sharing genuine, hard-won wisdom rather than a
staged endorsement — warm, homey, and disarmingly sincere. Cast
[AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Their
expression and posture should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a
model selling a product. The handwriting, wording, and underlined phrase
should feel like something a real person would actually write, not polished
ad copy.

templates.name: Mirror Selfie + Overlaid IG Comment Card
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed vertical mirror-selfie
photograph, phone-camera aesthetic, filling the entire frame — no visible ad
chrome. Subject stands sideways-on to the mirror in a softly lit, plain
residential room (neutral wall, closet door, muted carpet), body framed from
shoulders to knees, one hand raised holding the phone, the other resting
naturally on [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] or against her body to
echo the reference pose. Lighting is soft, even, overhead-diffuse, true-to-life
color, unretouched and candid rather than studio-polished — this must read as
a real person's phone photo, not a commercial shoot. In the lower third,
overlay a native social-app comment card: small circular profile photo,
[REVIEWER NAME like Dawn K.] in bold above a 2-3 line comment in regular
weight reading [PULL-QUOTE — the most emotional 4-8 word phrase from the
review], set on a white rounded-corner card with soft drop shadow, left-
aligned, occupying roughly 30-35% of the frame height, positioned left-of-
center so it doesn't cover the subject's face. The scene should feel like a
genuine, unscripted moment a real customer chose to share — intimate,
unpolished, and proud — with the comment card looking screenshotted-in rather
than designed, reinforcing that this is authentic word-of-mouth, not brand
copy. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON like a woman in her third trimester who has
started prioritizing herself and her baby's needs] — someone living with
[AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY like the daily uncertainty of preparing for a baby
while still wanting to feel like herself]. Her posture and expression should
evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY like a woman who feels confident,
prepared, and taken care of], not a model selling a product.

templates.name: Social News-Post Clinical Stat Split
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Render a realistic social media
post mockup on a solid black background, styled like a verified news/media
account's feed post. Top zone (top 15%): small circular account avatar icon,
bold account name with a blue verified checkmark, and a muted @handle beneath
it in light gray on black. Below that, a headline block of white sans-serif
text (3-4 lines, left-aligned, comfortable line height) stating a bold
clinical or performance claim about [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details],
ending with an emoji that reinforces surprise or attention. Below the text,
a two-panel image strip filling roughly 45% of the canvas height, divided by a
thin black gutter: left panel shows [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details]
standing upright on a plain light [BACKGROUND] surface, labeled and lit like a
clean studio product shot; right panel shows a real, unposed close-up
photograph relevant to the product's result area (e.g. skin, scalp, body
region depending on category), lit with ordinary indoor light, candid and
unretouched rather than styled. Bottom zone: a row of engagement icons (reply,
retweet/share, like with count, bookmark/view count, share-out icon) in thin
gray line-art beneath the image strip, and a single line of small italic gray
disclaimer/citation text at the very bottom referencing a study or timeframe
in [TIMEFRAME]. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with
[AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. The candid right-panel photo should read as if it
belongs to a real person, not a model, evoking [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY]
rather than a staged testimonial. The overall arrangement should feel like a
real, screenshot-worthy news update that stopped someone mid-scroll because
the result looked too specific and too real to be an ad — credible,
matter-of-fact, and quietly stunning rather than glossy or promotional.

templates.name: UGC Twitter/X Testimonial Screenshot — Selfie + Product-in-Hand
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Recreate a screenshotted social
media post mockup on a solid [ACCENT COLOR] textured background, the post
card centered and floating with a soft drop shadow, occupying roughly 85% of
the canvas width. At the top of the card, show a small circular profile
avatar, a bold display name, and an @handle in gray, styled exactly like a
native X/Twitter post header. Below the header, place 2-3 lines of casual,
punchy, first-person testimonial-style caption text in a plain sans-serif
font, written like a real user raving about [PRODUCT CATEGORY] — informal
tone, may include one emoji. Under the caption, place a two-image grid
side-by-side with a thin white gutter: left image is a chest-up selfie-style
photo of [AUDIENCE PERSON], smiling, holding [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details] toward the camera in one hand, shot in a casual indoor
setting with natural window light; right image is a close-up crop of a hand
holding [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] against a neutral
countertop backdrop, soft top-down natural light, shallow depth of field.
Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Their
expression and posture should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a
model selling a product. The scene should feel like an unscripted, candid
recommendation from a real person who trusts this product enough to show
their own face and hands holding it — authentic, unpolished, believable, the
opposite of a studio ad.

templates.name: Aerial Environment Social Post Overlay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed background is a
dramatic top-down aerial photograph of [SETTING like a striking real-world
location tied to the brand's world], shot from directly overhead with bold
graphic shapes, saturated natural color, and crisp midday light so the
environment itself feels iconic and scroll-stopping. Centered in the lower
half, a realistic social media post card floats above the photo like a native
in-app screenshot: circular [BRAND] logo avatar top-left of the card, bold
account name and @handle beside it, a small overflow-menu icon top-right,
and below that 2-3 lines of short, casual, lowercase [SHORT HEADLINE]-style
post copy in a plain sans-serif font on a white or near-white card with soft
rounded corners and a subtle drop shadow so it reads as an authentic embedded
post rather than a designed graphic. A thin strip of fine-print legal or
[TRUST BADGE like membership/eligibility details] sits at the very bottom of
the frame, small and unobtrusive. No people, hands, faces, or body parts —
environment, structure, and the social post card only. The scene narrative:
this should feel like scrolling a feed and stumbling on a brand's own witty,
lightweight post laid over a genuinely impressive real-world shot of their
space — the environment does the flexing, the copy does the charm, and
together they read as effortlessly confident rather than hard-selling.
[MOOD].

templates.name: Fake Tweet UGC Product Reveal
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a mock social media post
card on a black background. Top zone: a circular profile avatar in
[SOFT BRAND COLOR like lavender/light purple] on the left, next to bold
[PERSON DESCRIPTION like woman in her 30s] name in white and a lighter gray
@handle beneath it, with a three-dot menu icon aligned top-right. Below the
profile row, one bold white headline in a native, conversational voice
(under 12 words) framed as a relatable tip or hook — [SHORT HEADLINE].
Beneath the headline, a large rounded-corner photo panel on a soft
