[BACKGROUND] surface shows a close-up of a hand holding [YOUR PRODUCT with
key packaging details] at a diagonal angle, product label facing camera,
shot in soft even studio light with shallow depth of field so the label
is crisp and the hand is naturally blurred at the edges. Bottom row shows
four flat white/gray social engagement icons in a horizontal strip — comment,
repost, like, share — each paired with a casual, believable engagement
number, with the like icon in [ACCENT COLOR]. A thin divider line separates
the icon row from the card above it. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living
with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her expression and posture (visible only
through her hand's confident, casual grip) should evoke
[AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. The scene
should feel like a genuine, off-the-cuff social post from a real person
sharing a life-hack discovery — candid, unpolished, and instantly relatable
rather than staged advertising.

templates.name: Relatable Social Post Callout on Bold Color Field
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a full-bleed solid
[PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] background occupying the entire canvas. Centered in the
middle of the frame, place a realistic social-media post card mockup (white
rounded-corner rectangle, subtle drop shadow) taking up roughly 65-70% of the
canvas width. At the top-left of the card, show a small circular avatar icon
in [DARK BRAND COLOR] containing the [BRAND] logomark or initial, followed by
the [BRAND] name in bold and a small verified-badge checkmark icon beside it.
Below the avatar row, render [YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] or
[FULL QUOTE 2-3 sentences] as left-aligned black body text in a clean
sans-serif font, written in a candid, funny, relatable first-person brand
voice — the kind of unfiltered, knowing observation that makes the target
customer feel truly seen. Optionally end the copy with one small relevant
emoji for tone. Leave generous negative space above and below the card so the
bold background color dominates the composition. No people, hands, faces, or
body parts — text card and background only. The scene narrative: this should
feel like scrolling past a brand's social post that says the quiet part out
loud — the specific, slightly taboo or messy truth of the customer's daily
experience that other brands are too polite to mention, creating instant
trust through candor and humor. Flat, evenly lit, screenshot-clean rendering
with no photographic elements — pure typographic/UI composition.

templates.name: Fake Tweet UGC Product Hold — Outdoor Blur Backdrop
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a mock social media post
card centered on a softly blurred outdoor green foliage background (bokeh,
natural daylight, no visible subject in the backdrop itself). The card occupies
the middle 55% of the vertical frame with generous blurred backdrop above and
below. Inside the card: top row shows a small circular profile photo of
[AUDIENCE PERSON], a bold display name, and a muted @handle beside it. Below
that, 2 lines of tweet-style body copy in plain dark sans-serif text reading
like a candid, slightly hyperbolic customer callout: [PULL-QUOTE — the most
emotional 4-8 word phrase from the review] followed by a casual sign-off
mentioning [BRAND]. Below the text, a square-cropped photo fills the width of
the card showing a close-up of fingers holding [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details] up against a softly blurred outdoor background, shallow
depth of field, natural window/daylight lighting, shot at a slight downward
angle as if captured candidly on a phone. Beneath the photo, a thin row of
faux engagement icons (reply, retweet, like, share) with small numeric counts
in light gray. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY
REALITY]. The overall feel should read as unpolished, native, and
believable — a real person's spontaneous post, not a studio ad — so the
product recommendation feels like organic word-of-mouth rather than
advertising. The scene should feel like a genuine, off-the-cuff share from
someone who just discovered a better way to do something and wants their
followers to know immediately.

templates.name: Social Screenshot Before/After Testimonial
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a realistic social media
post screenshot mockup centered on a solid [BACKGROUND] canvas, framed like a
native X/Twitter post card with rounded corners and soft drop shadow. Top row
shows a small circular profile photo of [AUDIENCE PERSON] beside a bold
display name and "@[HANDLE]" in gray, with a three-dot menu icon at the far
right. Below that, a short first-person post caption in plain dark text
referencing an emotional realization or turning point related to
[PRODUCT CATEGORY] tied to [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details], ending
with a single expressive emoji. Directly beneath the caption, a large
edge-to-edge two-panel before/after image split down the center with a thin
white divider — left panel shows the "before" state, right panel shows the
visibly improved "after" state, both tightly cropped on the same facial
feature or body area for direct comparison, natural skin tones, soft even
lighting. Below the image, small gray timestamp and view-count text, then a
thin horizontal divider line, then a row of engagement icons (reply, retweet,
like as filled heart, bookmark, share) each paired with an abbreviated
count. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY].
Her expression in the profile photo and the emotional tone of the caption
should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product.
The scene should feel like a real, unscripted social post someone shared
because the result genuinely surprised them — candid, credible, and
screenshot-native rather than staged advertising.

templates.name: Twitter/X Reveal Mockup — Product Duo Flat-Lay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Recreate an authentic Twitter/X
post mockup UI: top-left shows a small circular generic profile avatar icon,
a verified checkmark, bold username, gray handle, and timestamp ("11h") on
one line. Below that, a tweet caption in plain black text, 1-2 short lines,
phrased as an excited casual discovery ("Why didn't anyone tell me THIS is
[SHORT HEADLINE]?"). Below the caption, a large embedded photo card with
rounded corners and a thin light-gray border fills roughly 65% of the
canvas height: on a bright white marble-textured surface, [YOUR PRODUCT with
key packaging details] is shown twice side-by-side (e.g. two coordinated
variants/flavors of the same product), centered in the upper half of the
photo. In the lower half of the photo, loose contents from the product
(such as capsules, pieces, or units in a mix of colors matching
[PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] and [ACCENT COLOR]) are scattered informally in two
small clusters directly beneath each product unit, as if just poured out
for a demonstration. Lighting is bright, even, diffuse overhead studio
light with soft shadows — clean but unstaged, like a real customer photo,
not a polished product shoot. Below the photo card, a row of native
platform engagement icons (reply, retweet, like, view count, bookmark,
share) sits in light gray, each with a small realistic count. No people,
hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only; the avatar icon is a
generic flat UI placeholder, not a rendered human. The overall arrangement
should feel like a genuine, unscripted social post from a satisfied
customer showing off a smart discovery — candid, believable, and
screenshot-native rather than art-directed.

templates.name: Social Post Card Over Lifestyle Interior
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a vertical lifestyle
photograph of a softly lit, styled interior — a cozy living room corner with
a sofa, a chunky knit throw draped across the cushions, a woven jute rug, and
softly blurred styled shelving in the background holding candid decor objects
(books, a small plant, a retro radio, ceramics). Natural diffuse daylight from
the side, warm neutral palette, shallow depth of field so the background
softens while the foreground throw stays in gentle focus. Overlaid in the
lower-left to center portion of the frame, layer a realistic social media post
card mockup (rounded white card with soft drop shadow, platform-style UI: a
small circular [BRAND] logo avatar, [BRAND] handle and verified badge, a
timestamp, and row of engagement icons — reply, repost, like, share) containing
short native-voice copy: "[YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words]." The card should sit
naturally atop the furniture as if genuinely screenshotted and pasted into the
scene, not floating. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and
props only; if [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] is referenced elsewhere
in copy, it should not physically appear in this frame — the scene is a pure
lifestyle backdrop for the social card. The arrangement should feel like a
genuine, low-key moment of everyday domestic comfort interrupted by a piece of
timely, reassuring advice — as if the brand's message just arrived exactly when
the viewer needed permission to slow down.

templates.name: Tweet Mockup — Influencer Endorsement + Product Split
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a realistic social media
post screenshot mockup centered on a plain black background, taking up the
middle ~75% of the frame. At the top of the card, show a small circular
profile photo on the left and, beside it, a bold display name with a
verified/handle line below in gray (e.g. "@[HANDLE]"). Below the profile row,
place a bold black headline statement (2-3 short lines, large sans-serif
type) delivering [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] as a direct, punchy claim
aimed at the target audience, followed by one supporting line naming
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] as the reason. Beneath the text
block, place a horizontal two-image row: left image shows an artistic
fanned/cascading arrangement of [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] in
a gradient of its available shades or variants against a soft neutral
backdrop; right image shows the product opened, angled to reveal its texture
or contents, with the [BRAND] logo/wordmark visible on the packaging. Below
the image row, add a thin timestamp/metadata line in light gray (e.g. "9:32
AM · [DATE] · [PLATFORM] for iPhone") to sell the authenticity of the
screenshot. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] as the poster — someone living with
[AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her expression in the small profile photo and the
confident, first-person tone of the headline should evoke [AUDIENCE
ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a paid model selling a product. The scene should
feel like a genuine, spontaneous endorsement from someone the viewer already
trusts — candid, credible, and screenshot-native rather than art-directed.

templates.name: Fake Tweet Alarm Overlay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed vertical frame filled
edge-to-edge with an extreme close-up macro photo relevant to [PRODUCT CATEGORY]
— shot tight and slightly unsettling, shallow depth of field, natural outdoor
light, candid and unposed, filling the entire canvas top to bottom with no
visible background or studio setting. Overlaid in the lower-middle third is a
realistic social media post card mockup (rounded corners, subtle drop shadow,
platform-style header with small circular avatar icon, verified checkmark,
handle name, and timestamp/client text below the body). The card contains a
short alarming or myth-busting claim about [PRODUCT CATEGORY] as the post body
copy: [SHORT HEADLINE], followed by a line break and a call-to-action phrase
such as [CTA like "See what experts recommend →"]. Below the text, render
small engagement icons (reply, retweet, like, share) with realistic
abbreviated counts. The scene narrative: the jarring, too-close photograph
paired with an alarming social post caption should feel like a scroll-stopping
moment of unexpected concern — the viewer feels a jolt of "wait, is this about
me/my pet/my situation?" before curiosity pulls them into the claim. No
people, hands, faces, or human body parts — the close-up subject and mockup
card only. Keep the area behind and above the card slightly darkened at the
edges via natural vignette so the card remains legible.

templates.name: Fake Tweet Testimonial Grid (4-Panel UGC)
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a mock social media post
screenshot on a plain white/off-white background, styled like an X (Twitter)
post. Top zone (roughly top 12%) shows a small circular profile photo of
[AUDIENCE PERSON], a bold display name, and a lighter @handle beside it. Below
that, a bold black headline in a clean sans-serif, [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10
words], framed as if a real person is sharing a discovery about [PRODUCT
CATEGORY]. Below the headline, a 2x2 grid of four square photo tiles with thin
white gutters, each tile showing a different everyday woman using [YOUR
PRODUCT with key packaging details] in her own home — one wiping a kitchen
counter, one spraying near a stovetop, one close-up mid-wipe, one cleaning a
marble-look surface. Lighting is natural and inconsistent tile-to-tile, like
real phone photos, not studio-matched. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] across the
tiles — women living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY], caught mid-task rather
than posing, expressions neutral-to-focused, hands doing real work with the
product visible but not staged as a hero shot. Their body language should
evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. The
scene should feel like an organic viral post — several ordinary people
independently discovering the same trick, screenshotted and reposted because
it feels too relatable and true to be an ad. No visible ad disclosure, no CTA
button, no logos beyond what's naturally in each home. Grain and slight
imperfection in each tile are intentional — do not polish into a glossy
studio composite.

templates.name: Native Social Post Hook Mockup
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a full-bleed [BACKGROUND]
canvas that mimics a native social media post, not an ad. In the vertical
center, place a compact post block occupying roughly the middle 20% of the
canvas height: a small circular brand icon in [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] on the
left containing a simple bold glyph, followed inline by the [BRAND] handle
name in bold black sans-serif and a blue verified checkmark badge immediately
after it. Below that line, on its own row, set one short line of body copy in
[SHORT HEADLINE] using plain black sans-serif text at a size roughly matching
the handle text — this line is the hook and should read like a relatable,
conversational thought, not a sales claim. Leave generous empty white space
above and below the post block — at least 35% of canvas height on each side —
so the composition feels like a cropped screenshot of a real feed rather than
a designed ad. No photography, no product, no gradients, no borders, no drop
shadows — flat, native app aesthetic only. The scene should feel like a
genuine, everyday social post that surprises the viewer with its relatability,
disarming their ad-recognition instinct before the hook line lands. No people,
hands, faces, or body parts — text and UI iconography only.

templates.name: UGC Social Post Mockup — Habit-Stack Mixing Ritual
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a realistic social media
post mockup (X/Twitter-style card) with a rounded-square profile photo,
[CREATOR NAME] and [CREATOR HANDLE] at the top, a bold two-line caption above
the photo reading like a native scroll-stopping hook, and a full-width
rounded-corner photo below the caption. In the photo, a pair of hands mixes
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] into a clear glass of liquid on a
light wood surface, with the product's own container resting beside the glass
and softly blurred fresh produce in the background suggesting a bright,
lived-in kitchen. Light is soft, diffuse, natural window light from the side;
shallow depth of field keeps the hands and glass sharp while the background
falls away. Below the photo, include a timestamp/date line and a native
engagement bar (reply, repost, like, bookmark, share icons with counts). Cast
[AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her hands,
gesture, and the unstyled, candid framing should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL
IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. The scene should feel like a small,
sustainable daily ritual she actually keeps up with — effortless, real,
camera-phone candid rather than staged.

templates.name: Filmstrip Stat Callout
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Top 45% of the frame is a
horizontal filmstrip of 5 candid, real-life lifestyle photo tiles placed
edge-to-edge, cropped tight and bleeding off the left and right edges of the
canvas, set against a [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] solid background. The tiles show
varied everyday moments relevant to [AUDIENCE PERSON] — a hand or arm in
motion, a family or team moment, a product-in-use close-up, an outdoor or
candid lifestyle scene — giving the impression of a real customer's life,
not a staged shoot. Bottom 55% is solid [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] background:
a large bold [STAT] headline sits left-aligned in heavy black sans-serif
type, followed by a thin horizontal divider rule, then a bottom row with
[BRAND] in bold caps on the left and [ATTRIBUTION like Name, Title at Company]
in smaller regular type to its right. The scene should feel like proof
pulled straight from a real team's day-to-day — candid, unpolished snapshots
stitched together to back up a hard number, so the stat reads as earned
truth rather than a marketing claim. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living
with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY] — implied through the filmstrip's candid
moments; their presence should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not
posed stock photography. High-contrast flat color field, no gradients,
clean geometric text layout, confident and direct mood.

templates.name: Clinical Stat Overlay — In-Use Shower Moment
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical full-bleed lifestyle photo:
top two-thirds of frame shows a person mid-routine in a wet, steamy setting
relevant to [PRODUCT CATEGORY] (e.g. shower, bathroom, kitchen), shot from
behind or at an intimate cropped angle so the face is not the focus — hands,
arms, and motion communicate real use. [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging
details] or its point of use (e.g. showerhead, applicator, dispenser) is
visible at the top-right or top-left third of frame, in sharp focus, water or
texture droplets visible for tactile realism. Upper-left quarter of the frame
is reserved as a clear text zone overlaid directly on the photo (no solid
panel): a small all-caps eyebrow claim line with one word underlined for
emphasis, below it a bold large-type headline stating [STAT] as the hero
statistic in [CONTRAST COLOR] over the photo, and beneath that a smaller
one-sentence methodology caption in muted gray explaining the stat's context
(e.g. sample size, timeframe). [BRAND] logo sits lower-left in clear space
against the photo, minimal and small relative to the stat. Lighting is soft,
naturalistic, diffused daylight or bathroom light — believable, not
overly polished. The scene should feel like an ordinary daily ritual caught
mid-motion, proof lived-in rather than staged, so the statistic reads as
something that happens in real routines, not a lab claim. Cast
[AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her
expression and posture should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a
model selling a product.

templates.name: Clinical Stat Hero — Floating Product on Glossy Splash
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a vertical 9:16 studio
shot on a solid [SOFT BRAND COLOR like lavender/light purple] background
filling the full frame. Center-right, angled diagonally, place [YOUR PRODUCT
with key packaging details] as the hero — glossy, gradient-lit, resting as if
levitating above a pooling, glossy liquid splash in [ACCENT COLOR] that spreads
organically behind and beneath it, implying the product's texture and
richness. Top-center, small and minimal, show the [BRAND] logo/wordmark in
[DARK BRAND COLOR]. Upper-left third is the clear text zone: set [STAT] in
large bold type with [YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] beneath it in a lighter
weight, both in [DARK BRAND COLOR], left-aligned, sitting directly on the
background with no card or box behind it. Bottom-right corner holds a small
italic disclaimer/citation line in a muted tone. Lighting is soft and diffuse
studio light from upper-left, shallow depth of field, glossy specular
highlights on the product surface and liquid pool. The scene should feel like
a clinical result made tangible — the glossy pooling liquid visually "proves"
the formula's texture and efficacy while the bold stat anchors it in
credibility, giving the product a levitating, almost precious presence. No
people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only.

templates.name: Dockside Wear-It Moment — Apparel Lifestyle Portrait
templates.template: Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors, typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a vertical lifestyle portrait: [AUDIENCE PERSON] reclines forearms-forward toward camera, filling the lower two-thirds of the frame, wearing [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] as the visible garment, against a soft-focus outdoor waterside backdrop (water, distant shoreline, overcast sky) filling the top third. Hair is wind-tousled and slightly damp, expression a relaxed, genuine half-smile looking off-camera — candid, not posed. Natural overcast daylight, soft and diffuse, muted cool-neutral palette with the garment in [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR]. Shallow depth of field keeps the person crisp and the background gently blurred. Reserve the lower-center third as a clean text zone over the garment for [SHORT HEADLINE] in white sans-serif type stacked two lines, with [BRAND] logo lockup placed just beneath it. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her expression and posture should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. The scene should feel like an unscripted, just-arrived outdoor moment — effortless, fresh-air confidence, as if she just stepped off a dock into her favorite [PRODUCT CATEGORY] piece.

templates.name: Fake iMessage Recommendation Thread
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Recreate an authentic iPhone
iMessage conversation screenshot, full-bleed, true-to-life iOS chat UI: dark
mode background, contact name "[PERSON]" with small circular avatar centered
at top, signal/battery status bar, back-arrow and video-call icons at the
corners. Show a 4-6 message exchange as alternating chat bubbles — sender's
outgoing bubbles in iOS blue on the right, friend's incoming bubbles in dark
gray on the left — with realistic bubble corner radius, tail shape, and line
spacing. The outgoing bubbles build a casual, excited first-person story
about discovering [PRODUCT CATEGORY] and getting a surprising, specific result
using [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details], written in lowercase, texting
shorthand, and 1-2 emoji for authenticity — never polished ad copy. The
incoming bubbles react with genuine disbelief and curiosity, escalating to
directly asking what it's called and where to get it. The final outgoing
bubble names [BRAND] as the answer with an enthusiastic, slightly disbelieving
tone. Bottom of frame shows the native iOS message compose bar (camera icon,
app-drawer icon, "iMessage" placeholder text field, mic icon) fading into the
top rows of the iOS emoji/QWERTY keyboard, cropped at the bottom edge exactly
as a real screenshot would be. No people, hands, faces, or body parts —
interface and typography only. The scene should feel like a screenshot a
real friend forwarded you, not an ad — unpolished, believable, native to the
feed, so the viewer's first instinct is "this is someone's actual conversation,"
not "this is a promotion."

templates.name: UGC Workshop Palm-Reveal with Bold Caption Overlay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical iPhone-style UGC shot,
shot from a first-person point of view looking down at an open palm. The palm
sits center-lower-frame holding [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details],
shown loose and unstaged as if just pulled from a pocket or bag. A second hand
enters from the right edge of frame holding a handheld tool relevant to
[PRODUCT CATEGORY], slightly out of focus to keep focus on the product in the
palm. Background is a cluttered, authentic workspace/workshop setting —
pegboard wall with hanging tools, storage bins, spools, and everyday clutter —
lit with flat, unglamorous overhead work light, slightly warm, no studio
polish. Bold black sans-serif caption text on a white rounded-rectangle
background sits in the upper third: [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words], styled
like a native app text overlay, not a designed graphic. Lower-third holds a
second white text card with a short intro line plus a stacked bulleted list of
[BENEFIT 1-4], same bold native-caption typography. The scene should feel like
a real tradesperson interrupted their work to excitedly show a coworker
something they just discovered — candid, unpolished, and genuinely
enthusiastic, not staged or scripted. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living
with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her/his posture and framing should evoke
[AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. Slight
handheld camera imperfection, natural skin tone, visible grain, no retouching.

templates.name: UGC App-Screenshot Testimonial Overlay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed candid lifestyle photo
fills the entire frame as the background — a real social moment in
[SETTING like a casual outdoor brunch table with friends], shot on what looks
like an iPhone camera, natural daylight, slightly imperfect framing, warm and
unposed. In the upper-left ~40% of the frame, overlay a realistic phone
screenshot mockup of [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details]'s app or
digital interface, cropped at the edges like a real screen-record, showing
[KEY INGREDIENT] or a core in-app result/status the product delivers. On the
right side of the frame, stack two native-style caption overlays in bold black
sans-serif text on a solid white rounded-rectangle background, styled like a
screenshot repost caption, not a designed graphic: the first line reads like a
first-person hook e.g. "[SHORT HEADLINE]", the second is a longer explainer
line e.g. "[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence]". Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone
living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her expression and posture (only
partially visible — hands, arms, the back of the moment) should evoke
[AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. The scene
should feel like a friend casually shared their phone screen mid-brunch to
show you something that changed their routine — candid, unforced, and
believable as an organic post rather than an ad. Grain and casual imperfection
should be preserved; do not polish or over-light the photo.

templates.name: UGC Selfie Hold with Stacked Text-Bubble Argument
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical iPhone-selfie-style
UGC shot, front-facing camera angle, shot from slightly above chest height
so the subject fills the upper two-thirds of the frame. She stands in a
casual home entryway (soft daylight from a window/door behind her, neutral
walls, out-of-focus household clutter) wearing relaxed everyday clothing,
smiling naturally at the camera. She holds [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details] up toward the lens in both hands at chest/stomach
height, angled slightly so it reads clearly. To the right of her face,
stacked black-text-on-white rounded rectangle caption bubbles run top to
bottom like a text-message thread, each one a short punchy line building
an argument for the product — first bubble poses a common frustration,
middle bubbles reframe the problem and tease the solution, final bubble
delivers the offer/CTA. In the lower background, softly out of focus,
[BRAND]'s branded packaging or delivery box is visible, grounding the
scene in a real unboxing/delivery moment. Lighting is bright, flat,
available/ambient — no studio polish, slightly imperfect focus and
white balance, exactly like a real phone video. The scene should feel
like a real person caught mid-moment, unscripted, casually convincing a
friend rather than performing an ad. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone
living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her expression and posture should
evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product.

templates.name: UGC Collector's Haul — Face-Forward Multi-Product Callout
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical iPhone-style selfie
crop, shot close and slightly wide-angle like a handheld front camera.
[AUDIENCE PERSON] fills the upper half of the frame, face bright and
grinning directly at camera, one hand raised beside the face holding
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] tilted toward the lens. The
lower half of the frame is stacked with 2-3 additional units of [YOUR
PRODUCT with key packaging details] in varying colorways, angled and
slightly overlapping like a proud haul display, resting against his
chest/torso and a plain neutral wall backdrop. Bold black sans-serif
caption text is overlaid across the upper-third, breaking naturally
across 2-3 lines: [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] with an emoji accent.
White hand-drawn-style arrows and small black text labels point from the
headline down to individual products, calling out [KEY INGREDIENT] or
[SHORT HEADLINE] per item — mimicking a native app annotation sticker.
Flat, even on-camera flash lighting, slightly overexposed and casual, no
studio polish. The scene should feel like a genuine collector or superfan
excitedly showing off their personal stash, not a paid endorsement —
proud, in-the-know, unpolished. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living
with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. His expression and posture should evoke
[AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product.

templates.name: Candid Close-Up with Caption Overlay (UGC)
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical 9:16 crop, tightly
framed from chin to mid-torso, phone-camera candid energy — not a studio
shot. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]
— seated indoors in soft, slightly warm natural window light, wearing
[SETTING like a casual denim or everyday outfit]. She wears [YOUR PRODUCT
with key packaging details] layered naturally at the neckline, one hand
raised toward it mid-gesture as if adjusting or admiring it, the other
hand relaxed in frame with visible rings or small accessories for texture.
Skin tones and fabric should read true-to-life, shallow depth of field,
soft grain, no retouched gloss. Right-third of the frame reserved for a
plain sans-serif caption in [CONTRAST COLOR] text, lowercase, casual
voice-of-friend tone: [YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] mentioning [BRAND].
Her expression and posture should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY],
not a model selling a product. The scene should feel like a genuine
personal styling moment — the product folded into daily life, not staged
for an ad, so a scrolling viewer reads it as a real friend's recommendation
rather than a paid post.

templates.name: UGC Lineup with Testimonial Text Overlay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Frame a vertical 9:16 phone-photo
style shot: three units of [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] standing
upright in a row, evenly spaced, on a [SURFACE like a dark wood tabletop] shot
from a slightly elevated angle as if a real person set them down and snapped
a quick photo. Lighting is casual indoor ambient light, slightly warm, with
natural soft shadows — not studio-polished. A thin [ACCENT COLOR] border frames
the entire canvas edge-to-edge. The bottom third of the frame holds a
semi-transparent dark overlay panel containing bold, black, hand-picked
sans-serif text: [PULL-QUOTE — the most emotional 4-8 word phrase from the
review] as the large primary line, with [FULL QUOTE 2-3 sentences] or a short
supporting line in smaller white text beneath it. No people, hands, faces, or
body parts — product and props only. The scene should feel like an unfiltered,
candid snapshot a genuinely satisfied customer took at home and shared online,
not a staged studio photo — authentic, unpolished, and instantly believable.

templates.name: UGC Vanity Flat-Lay with Caption Overlay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Shoot a candid overhead/angled
flat-lay on a light marble vanity surface, with a slim brass or gold-edged
tray visible along the upper edge of the frame. Arrange two or three open
units of [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] scattered naturally
across the surface, lids off, product texture visibly swirled or
fingertip-dipped so it reads as mid-use rather than staged. One unit sits
closer to camera at a slight tilt, others recede toward the upper background,
creating casual depth rather than a symmetrical grid. Light should feel like
soft ambient daylight from a bathroom window — warm-neutral, slightly
diffuse, with gentle real shadows, iPhone-photo quality rather than
studio-lit. In the upper-right negative space over the marble, overlay
[YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] in bold white sans-serif caps, styled like a
native social app caption stacked on 3 short lines. No people, hands, faces,
or body parts — product and props only. The scene should feel like a real
person's candid vanity moment — snapped quickly mid-routine to share a
genuine find, not a produced product shot.

templates.name: Story Caption Flat-Lay UGC
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical 9:16 frame shot from
directly above at a slight angle, like a personal Instagram Story screenshot.
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] rests open/displayed on [SURFACE],
softly lit by natural daylight falling from one side, casting long gentle
shadows across a light, textured backdrop (e.g. a knit cushion or linen
throw) in the lower-right third of the frame. Stack two native-style white
caption boxes with black sans-serif text in the upper-left half of the
frame, vertically spaced with breathing room between them, mimicking an
Instagram Story text sticker: first box holds [SHORT HEADLINE], second box
below it holds [YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence]. Leave the lower-right quadrant
uncluttered so the product remains legible. Grain and color should feel
candid and phone-shot, not studio-polished — natural white balance, slightly
imperfect framing, authentic UGC energy. The arrangement should feel like a
private, reflective morning moment someone chose to share — quiet,
unhurried, personally meaningful rather than promotional. No people, hands,
faces, or body parts — product and props only.

templates.name: UGC Treatment POV — Pain/Solution Text Stack
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Frame a candid, handheld-feeling
POV shot as if captured mid-treatment on a phone camera, not a staged studio
photo — natural indoor light, slightly clinical but intimate framing, shallow
depth of field with soft grain. A practitioner's gloved hands operate a
handheld device near the client's face; the client lies back, eyes closed,
relaxed. [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] or the treatment tool
should be visible mid-use near the top third of the frame, held naturally in
the practitioner's hand — not posed like a product shot. Top 20% of the frame
carries a bold white sans-serif callout in a black pill/box: [YOUR HEADLINE,
under 10 words] naming the old, ineffective approach the viewer has tried.
Bottom third carries a second bold callout in a [ACCENT COLOR] pill/box:
[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] naming the real solution and its 2-3 core
benefits. Both text blocks sit as overlay call-out boxes with rounded corners,
high-contrast text, native-app caption styling — not designed graphics. Cast
[AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her
expression and posture should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a
model selling a product. The scene should feel like real, unscripted proof
that this treatment is happening right now — reassuring, credible, and worth
stopping to watch.

templates.name: UGC Hand-Held Duo Stack with Testimonial Overlay
templates.template: Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors, typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical 9:16 frame split into two stacked, near-identical UGC photo panels, each showing a hand entering from the bottom of the frame holding [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] up toward the camera against a plain tiled or neutral wall background, shot like a candid iPhone photo — slightly close, handheld angle, flat on-camera flash lighting, no styling or props. A white rounded-rectangle text card sits centered across the seam between the two panels, containing [PULL-QUOTE — the most emotional 4-8 word phrase from the review] in bold black sans-serif, styled like a native Instagram/TikTok caption sticker. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. The hand's posture and framing should feel like [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. The scene should feel like a real customer pausing mid-unboxing to show off their haul — unpolished, authentic, and proof-driven rather than staged. Mood: candid, trustworthy, unpolished.

templates.name: UGC Selfie Treatment-Room Bold Claim Overlay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical full-bleed 9:16 iPhone-style
mirror/arm's-length selfie shot inside a real treatment or service room, candid
and slightly imperfect — visible ceiling, equipment, and a device screen in soft
background blur. Subject reclines casually in the setting, one arm extended
toward camera in a natural selfie angle, relaxed genuine smile, looking directly
into the lens as if messaging a friend. [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details]
or its use-context is implied through the environment and equipment visible in
the background rather than staged in-hand. A bold black-outlined white speech
bubble sits in the upper third of the frame, rounded corners, containing
[YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] in bold black sans-serif text stating the core
claim plainly and confidently. Lighting is flat, slightly harsh indoor
fluorescent/ambient — unpolished, authentic, not studio-lit. Grain and minor
imperfections are welcome; this must not look like a produced ad. Cast
[AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Their
expression and posture should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a
model selling a product. The scene should feel like a real customer casually
documenting their own treatment session and sharing an honest, almost
too-good-to-keep-quiet result with a friend.

templates.name: UGC Screen-Recording Pain/Solution Overlay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical 9:16 UGC-style shot,
framed like a candid screen-recording captured on a phone. Top 35% of the
frame is a softly blurred, moody room background with ambient [ACCENT COLOR]
and [SOFT BRAND COLOR like lavender/light purple] bokeh glow — shallow depth
of field, low dramatic ambient light. Two stacked bold text overlay cards sit
in the upper-left area on solid black backgrounds with white text: first card
reads [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] framed as a relatable pain-point
question, second card directly below reads [YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence]
positioning the product as the solution. Bottom 65% of the frame shows
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] displayed prominently on-screen
(as if on a laptop or device display) resting on a dark desk surface with a
keyboard visible in the soft-focus foreground, angled slightly toward camera.
A bright neon [ACCENT COLOR] arrow or hand-drawn annotation points toward the
key feature or interface element being highlighted on the product display.
The scene should feel like an unscripted, late-night personal discovery
moment — someone stumbling onto the one tool that finally gets their chaos
under control, filmed candidly rather than staged. No people, hands, faces,
or body parts — product and props only.

templates.name: POV Hand-Hold Multi-Product Reveal (UGC Sticker Overlay)
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical iPhone-style candid shot,
9:16. A hand and forearm enter from the bottom-right, fingers fanned to hold
up 2-3 units of [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] in a neat parallel
row, labels facing camera. Background is a softly blurred, authentic home
interior — [SETTING like a bedroom dresser with a mirror and everyday
clutter] — shot in ambient indoor light, shallow depth of field, slightly
off-level handheld framing so it reads as a real phone photo, not a studio
setup. Overlay native TikTok/Instagram-style caption stickers directly on the
image: a rounded white text box mid-frame with dark text reading
[YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] styled as a POV/relatable caption, and below
it 2-3 smaller lines each paired with a small emoji-bubble icon reading
[BENEFIT 1-4] (e.g. "for my [USE CASE 1]", "for my [USE CASE 2]"). Cast
[AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her hand
posture (confident, casual, mid-conversation gesture) and the unpolished

model selling a product. The scene should feel like a genuine phone-camera

templates.name: Bathroom Counter Lineup with Bold Text Overlay

HEADLINE, under 10 words]. Add a second, smaller bold caption near the

The scene should feel like someone just set out their whole routine on

show it off, like a real customer's bathroom, not a studio set.

templates.name: UGC Selfie Discovery Caption Overlay

Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,

templates.template:

covering the mug or the hand. The scene should feel like a real person
paused their morning drink-mixing ritual to film a candid, half-confessional

REALITY]. Her hand's posture and the unhurried pace of the frothing motion

product. [MOOD].

Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,

product. The scene should feel like a genuine bathroom-mirror check-in

canvas rather than a face. Directional studio light, soft and slightly
warm, with high sharpness on skin/lip texture and shallow falloff into the
dark mouth interior. Bold condensed sans-serif headline copy is typeset
directly onto the tongue/mouth-interior surface as if tattooed or
projected onto the flesh itself — [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] in
[CONTRAST COLOR] sits on the tongue, followed by smaller supporting body
copy in the same typeface continuing further back on the tongue:
[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence]. A final short CTA line sits lowest on the
tongue near the throat, styled as two centered lines: [CTA like TAKE THE
3-MINUTE TEST]. All text is center-aligned, high-contrast, and appears
printed flat onto the curved wet surface of the tongue, following its
natural sheen and shadow so it reads as embedded in the body part, not
floating above it. No product, no packaging, no logo anywhere in frame —
this is a pure shock-value text-on-anatomy composition. Cast
[AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. The
mouth's expression (parted lips, visible tension in the jaw) should evoke
[AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. The scene
should feel confrontational and intrusive in a way that stops the scroll —
like the ad is speaking a thought the viewer has had but never said aloud,
delivered from inside their own mouth.

templates.name: Engraved Prop Callout — Text Carved Into a Themed Object
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical 9:16 frame. Left 55-65%
of the canvas is filled diagonally by an oversized [SETTING-RELEVANT PROP
that thematically ties to the product, like a wooden baseball bat] rendered
in sharp macro detail, its surface engraved/carved with hand-lettered script
reading [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] plus a small signature-style line
underneath. Right 35-45% shows a tight close-up of [PERSON DESCRIPTION like
woman in her 20s with soft wavy hair, wearing a casual cap] from jaw to
shoulder, worn or held to display [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details]
in extreme detail — one visible piece styled naturally on the body (ear,
wrist, neck, or hand). In the top-left negative space, add a hand-drawn-style
label reading [PRODUCT] with a simple curved arrow pointing toward the
product detail, mimicking a casual annotation rather than a designed graphic.
Soft, bright, neutral studio lighting, shallow depth of field, warm neutral
background in [BACKGROUND]. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON like a young woman who
loves mixing sporty, casual pieces with elevated accessories] — someone
living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY like feeling that most jewelry looks the
same as everyone else's]. Her styling should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL
IDENTITY like someone whose accessories reflect her personality, not a
catalog]. The engraved prop text should feel like a playful, personal
plaque — as if the object itself is endorsing the product, not a caption
placed over the photo.

templates.name: Skin-Written Headline (Body-as-Billboard)
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Extreme close-up crop from chin to
upper chest of a person's neck and jawline, shot straight-on, filling the
entire frame — no visible product, no background context beyond a sliver of
plain wall and the collar of a solid-color t-shirt. [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10
words] is hand-lettered directly onto the skin of the neck in bold, dripping,
marker-style capital letters in [ACCENT COLOR], as if written by hand
mid-routine — slightly uneven baseline, letters wrapping naturally around the
curve of the throat across two or three stacked lines. Lighting is flat,
neutral, and close, like a phone camera held at arm's length — soft daylight,
minimal shadow, slight visible skin texture and a few small dark specks on the
shirt for raw, unpolished authenticity. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone
living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Their expression stays out of frame
entirely; the neck and lettering carry the whole message. The scene should
feel like a confession scrawled straight onto the body — blunt, urgent, and
impossible to scroll past, as if the product's promise mattered enough to
write directly on skin. No product shot, no logo, no packaging visible
anywhere in frame.

templates.name: Meme Hook Sandwich — Product Framed by Handwritten Text
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Background is a full-bleed,
slightly crumpled white paper or tissue-paper texture with soft creases and
subtle shadow folds, filling 100% of the canvas — no studio backdrop, no
gradient. Center the middle 40% of the frame on [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details], shot straight-on, floating with a soft contact shadow,
no surface or props visible. Above the product, in the top 20% of the frame,
place a bold, hand-scrawled, slightly wobbly marker-style headline reading
[SHORT HEADLINE, phrased as a scroll-stopping dare or warning, under 6 words]
— rendered in loose, imperfect capital-letter hand lettering, black ink,
uneven baseline, as if scribbled quickly by hand, not a clean font. Below the
product, in the bottom 20% of the frame, place a second hand-scrawled line in
the same lettering style delivering [YOUR SUBHEAD, one punchy sentence under
8 words] as the payoff or twist to the top line. If the product has printed
graphics or text on it, keep that copy legible and crisp against the product
surface as a visual contrast to the raw hand lettering surrounding it. The
scene should feel like a meme torn out of someone's notebook and pointed
directly at the scroller — irreverent, funny, DIY, and deliberately
low-production so it reads as authentic rather than as an ad. No people,
hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only. Overall mood: playful,
cheeky, attention-jacking, black-and-white-and-[PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] palette.

templates.name: Text Written on the Body
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Frame a cropped, headless shot
of [AUDIENCE PERSON] from behind, waist-up to mid-thigh, standing in soft
indoor light with a warm, blurred domestic background (lamp glow, out-of-
focus furniture). She wears [ACCENT COLOR] fitted bottoms with a wide
elastic waistband and, if applicable, a [ACCENT COLOR] cropped top —
skin exposed across the back and lower back to serve as a text canvas.
Overlay bold, condensed, all-caps sans-serif headline copy directly on
her bare skin as if written or projected onto her body, split across two
stacked blocks: [SHORT HEADLINE] positioned across the upper back/
shoulder blades, and [YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] positioned across the
lower back just above the waistband. Text is dark near-black, high
contrast against skin tone, kerned tight, sized to fill the width of the
torso. Reserve a thin solid [DARK BRAND COLOR] bar at the very bottom
edge of the frame for a logo or CTA lockup. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] —
someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her posture (relaxed,
grounded, unposed) should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a
model selling a product. The scene should feel like a private, almost
diagnostic moment — as if her own body is quietly asking the question
the copy poses, making the message impossible to scroll past.

templates.name: Handwritten Skin-Marker Offer (UGC)
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Tight, cropped close-up of a
person's bare thigh/hip, shot like a candid iPhone photo with natural
available light and shallow depth of field — no face in frame. Diagonally
across the skin, handwritten in black marker-style script, is
[YOUR OFFER like Free Shipping over $50] alongside [DISCOUNT like 15%] and
[URGENCY PHRASE like Limited Offer], filling roughly the left 55% of the
frame as if jotted down as a personal reminder. In the lower-right third,
a hand with casual, natural nail polish holds [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details] resting against the hip, label facing camera and legible.
Background is a softly blurred, cozy indoor setting (bed or couch edge, knit
throw) suggesting an unstaged, at-home moment. Small disclaimer text sits
centered near the bottom edge in tiny plain sans-serif. Cast
[AUDIENCE PERSON like woman in her late 20s who treats self-care as a small
daily ritual] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY like the habit of
jotting must-remember reminders anywhere handy, including her own skin]. Her
posture and the casual grip on the product should evoke
[AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY like someone who's found a deal too good not
to share], not a model posing for a product shot. The scene should feel like
she just remembered an offer was ending and scrawled it on herself before she
forgot — spontaneous, funny, and completely unstaged. Warm, candid, intimate
mood; no studio lighting, no graphic design elements beyond the handwriting.

templates.name: Handwritten Note + Phone Screenshot Testimonial
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed background is a plain
paper or whiteboard card resting on a [SURFACE], photographed top-down with
warm natural light. In the upper-left third, place a smartphone frame graphic
containing a UGC-style full-body selfie photo of [AUDIENCE PERSON], shot
outdoors in soft natural daylight, casual and candid, phone tilted slightly.
Fill the remaining canvas — right side and below the phone — with loose,
handwritten marker-style text simulating a personal note: a curved headline
at the top asking a question, several short handwritten paragraph lines
narrating a first-person before/after story, and a closing line near the
bottom pointing to a call-to-action. Add hand-drawn annotation marks —
colored arrows, circles, underlines, and a small icon — pointing at 2-3 key
phrases to mimic emphasis a real person would add. Include tiny disclaimer
text in small print at the very bottom edge. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone
living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her expression and posture in the
screenshot should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling
a product. The scene should feel like a real customer photographed their own
phone screenshot and handwrote a candid note around it to share with a
friend — authentic, unpolished, and trustworthy rather than staged.

templates.name: Toasted-Message Flat-Lay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Overhead flat-lay on a warm wood
serving board set against a soft cream backdrop with gentle natural light
falling from the upper right. On the left half of the board, two slices of
toasted bread rest side by side, split down the middle, with [BRAND] burned/
branded directly into the toasted surface in a rustic stenciled typeface —
the toast itself becomes the logo lockup, an unexpected canvas for the
brandmark. On the right half, [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] lies
diagonally on the board, its pattern or finish echoing the same warm,
handcrafted palette as the toast. Centered above, in an elegant serif
headline style, place [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] in [PRIMARY BRAND
COLOR]. Below the board, small caps benefit copy reads [BENEFIT 1-4],
separated by bullet dots, with the [BRAND] logo centered beneath in dark
brand-colored wordmark type. The scene should feel like a playful, handmade
kitchen-table moment — as if the brand toasted its own name into breakfast to
prove it belongs in your everyday routine, warm, witty, and craft-made rather
than mass-produced. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and
props only.

templates.name: Stat-on-Skin Beauty Result
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Frame a close, warm portrait
from behind/three-quarter angle so [AUDIENCE PERSON]'s hair and bare
shoulder dominate the composition — hair filling roughly 65% of the frame,
softly lit, tactile, catching natural light with visible texture and
movement. Place [SHORT HEADLINE or STAT] in a loose, hand-lettered script
directly overlaid across the skin of the shoulder/upper back, as if drawn
on the body itself rather than set on a graphic panel — dark ink tone,
irregular baseline, intimate scale relative to the skin. Small
[BRAND] wordmark sits quietly in a top corner in a thin serif or
custom logotype; a single line of [CLAIM or DISCLAIMER, small print]
sits near the bottom edge, understated. [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging
details] rests naturally near the shoulder or held loosely near the hair,
never centered, positioned as a lived-in companion to the styling ritual
rather than a studio hero shot. Background is a soft, neutral, out-of-focus
tone that keeps all attention on hair and skin. The scene should feel like
a private, unfiltered moment of admiring one's own hair after a real
result — proud, sensory, and quietly confident, as though the statistic
were a fact about her, not an ad claim. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone
living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her expression and posture should
evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product.
Soft, diffused natural light, shallow depth of field, warm color grade.

templates.name: Floating Letter Tiles on Water
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Shoot a top-down overhead angle
looking directly into a bright white vessel (bathtub, basin, or wide bowl)
filled with pale [SOFT BRAND COLOR like lavender/light purple]-tinted water,
lit by warm natural sunlight that casts soft caustic ripple patterns and
dappled shadows across the interior surface. Arrange the message as physical
3D floating letter tiles or magnets resting directly on the water's surface,
each letter a different bold [ACCENT COLOR] — not overlaid graphic text, but
tangible objects that appear to bob and catch wet reflections and highlights.
Compose the letters in three stacked lines occupying the vertical center of
the frame: top line spells [BRAND], second line spells [SHORT HEADLINE], and
a bottom line spells [YOUR OFFER like Up to 40% Off] beneath a short divider
mark. Leave generous negative space of plain water and vessel walls above and
below the text cluster so the tiles feel discovered, not designed. The scene
should feel like a playful, low-fi surprise tucked into an everyday domestic
ritual — bath time turned into an unboxing moment, candid and joyful rather
than staged. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only.

templates.name: On-Garment Print Callout — Worn Product Close-Up
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Frame a tight vertical crop from
mid-torso to upper-thigh height, positioning [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging
details] as a worn/in-use garment or item that fills roughly 80% of the frame.
Print [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] directly onto the surface of the product
itself, stacked across 3-4 short lines, in [CONTRAST COLOR] type that reads as
if it were manufactured into the material — not a floating graphic overlay.
Place a small [BRAND] wordmark just beneath the headline, same on-surface
treatment, noticeably smaller scale. Shoot with natural directional light from
one side, warm and slightly golden, shallow depth of field, candid editorial
lifestyle style — as though caught mid-moment rather than posed for a
catalogue. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY
REALITY]. Her/his posture (arms crossed, weight shifted, caught off-guard) and
the surrounding environment should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not
a model selling a product. The scene should feel like the viewer just noticed
a beautifully made detail on someone they were standing behind — an
unplanned, real-life discovery of the product's craftsmanship, not a staged
reveal.

templates.name: Iced-On Message Surface
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Fill the frame with a close-up,
top-down-tilted hero shot of [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details],
styled so its top surface fills roughly 75% of the canvas and serves as the
literal canvas for the message. Decorate the visible surface edge with
piped detailing, small edible-style accent shapes, and a color gradient in
[PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] transitioning into [ACCENT COLOR] and
[SOFT BRAND COLOR like lavender/light purple], with a ring of tall
decorative accents (candle-like or stick-like elements in mixed pastel
tones) rising from the back edge. Render [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words]
directly onto the flat top surface in a soft rounded script in
[DARK BRAND COLOR], as if piped, iced, or printed onto the product itself
rather than overlaid as a separate text graphic — the copy should curve
gently to follow the surface's natural contour. Scatter small thematic
accent icons ([KEY INGREDIENT] motifs or brand-relevant symbols) across the
surface and rim to reinforce the message. Background is softly blurred with
a hint of celebratory props in muted tones, keeping full attention on the
product surface. Lighting is soft, warm, and diffuse from above, shallow
depth of field, slightly elevated three-quarter angle. No people, hands,
faces, or body parts — product and props only. The scene should feel like a
personal, hand-finished moment of celebration — as though this exact
product were custom-made to carry this one message, warm, thoughtful, and
a little playful.

templates.name: Body-Written Confession Hook (UGC Skin Text)
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Tightly cropped vertical UGC-style
shot of [AUDIENCE PERSON]'s torso from collarbone to hip, one hand resting
flat on her chest, the other hand casually holding [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details] or a related drink/vessel at frame edge. Handwritten-marker
style text is inked directly across her bare midriff in place of any graphic
overlay: [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] written in loose, slightly uneven
black marker script, angled naturally to follow the curve of the stomach —
this IS the ad's primary text placement, not a caption below the image. Below
the waistband, a soft rounded pill-shaped CTA button reading [CTA like Shop
Limited Time Deal] with a small emoji accent floats naturally over the fabric
line. Neutral warm indoor lighting, soft and diffuse, shallow depth of field,
skin tone true-to-life, athleisure wardrobe in [ACCENT COLOR] tones. Cast
[AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her posture
(hand on chest, relaxed stance) should evoke [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY],
not a posed model selling a product. The scene should feel like an unscripted
phone-mirror moment of quiet self-realization, not a studio shoot — candid,
intimate, and confessional, as if she just had to share the epiphany the
handwriting captures.

templates.name: Surface-Integrated Stat/Numeral Callout
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed vertical composition:
the entire canvas is a single textured environmental surface in [BACKGROUND]
tones (e.g. a turf, fabric, stone, or granular texture native to
[PRODUCT CATEGORY]) with faint repeating linear guide-marks running
horizontally across it, like yard-line or ruler markings, to suggest scale and
progression. Top-third: [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] set in bold all-caps
white/[CONTRAST COLOR] type, centered, sitting directly on the textured
surface with no card or panel behind it. Mid-upper zone: [BRAND] logo mark
displayed large and centered, paired beside a secondary partner or product
mark separated by a thin vertical divider line, evoking a co-branded
authority pairing. Just below, a horizontal row of 3-4 small badge-style
logo marks representing [BENEFIT 1-4] or sub-offerings, evenly spaced. Lower
third: the composition's signature device — an oversized numeral or
[STAT] rendered in the same bold sans-serif as real surface markings (e.g.
painted field numbers), scaled dramatically larger than all other text,
split or bisected by the surface's own guide-line so it reads as if it were
physically part of the surface rather than a text overlay. Lighting is even,
slightly overhead, mimicking outdoor daylight on a textured plane; no studio
gloss. The scene should feel like the offer itself has been staked into the
ground as a permanent marker — authoritative, high-stakes, and impossible to
scroll past, the way a giant field number claims territory. No people, hands,
faces, or body parts — surface, texture, and typographic elements only.

templates.name: Handwritten-on-Skin Testimonial
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Frame a tight crop of
[AUDIENCE PERSON]'s bare arm and leg extending diagonally across the frame from
upper-left to lower-right, standing in [SETTING like a warm home kitchen with
wooden cabinetry], soft natural indoor light, candid documentary-style framing
with no face visible. Handwritten in dark marker-style script directly on the
skin, running vertically along the length of the limb, is the quote
[PULL-QUOTE — the most emotional 4-8 word phrase from the review], styled like
real ballpoint or marker handwriting with natural imperfection, not a clean
overlaid font. [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] rests upright on the
floor at the base of the limb, small in frame relative to the body, in sharp
focus as the only "designed" object in an otherwise raw, unstaged scene. Tiny
disclaimer text ([CLAIM] individual results may vary) sits bottom-center in a
plain sans-serif. No other people, no additional hands, faces, or body parts —
only the single limb and the product. The scene should feel like a private,
almost confessional home-video moment — someone testifying to a change on
their own body, in their own kitchen, with the truth written where it happened
rather than said in words. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with
[AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. The posture and setting should evoke
[AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. Mood:
[MOOD].

templates.name: Text Iced Onto the Product Surface
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Frame [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details] as a tall, tiered, hero-centered object filling roughly
70% of the vertical frame, shot straight-on against a softly blurred neutral
backdrop with a warm wooden surface beneath it. Render the product's outer
surface with a tactile, hand-finished texture (like swept frosting or matte
coating), and print [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] directly onto the
mid-section of that surface in [DARK BRAND COLOR] serif type, as if piped,
stamped, or hand-lettered into the material itself rather than added as a
graphic overlay. Beneath it, in smaller matching type, place
[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] and a short supporting line of
[BENEFIT 1-4] copy, keeping all text confined to the flattest tier so it
reads like part of the object's construction. Near the base, add a small
circular badge or medallion — the same tone as the product surface — bearing
[CTA like TRY NOW] in bold condensed type, resting against the product like
a wax seal or charm. Lighting is soft, warm, diffuse, and directional from
one side, with a shallow depth of field that keeps the product crisp and the
background gently out of focus. No people, hands, faces, or body parts —
product and props only. The scene should feel like a quiet, intimate
discovery — as though the viewer has stumbled on a confession baked into
something ordinary, turning a familiar object into a moment of
self-recognition.

templates.name: Fortune Cookie Reveal
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a close-up macro shot of
a single fortune cookie cracked open in mid-air by two hands just out of
frame at the bottom corners, filling the lower 55% of the frame against a
clean pure-white seamless background. A small paper fortune slip emerges
from the crack in the cookie, printed with [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words]
in a typewriter-style serif font — the slip is the primary text delivery
surface, not a graphic overlay. A few cookie crumbs scatter naturally on the
white surface below the crack for realism. Above the cookie, in the top 25%
of the frame, set [SHORT HEADLINE] in bold, oversized [DARK BRAND COLOR]
sans-serif type, left- and right-aligned to the canvas edges, wrapping to two
lines. Below the cookie, in the bottom whitespace, place a smaller two-line
supporting line in bold+regular mixed weight: bold lead-in phrase followed by
[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] referencing [STAT] and [SOCIAL PROOF]. Beneath
that, center a full-width rounded-rectangle button in [DARK BRAND COLOR]
with white text reading [CTA like Start your free trial →]. Lighting is
soft, even, shadowless studio light with a subtle contact shadow under the
cookie to keep it grounded on the white field. No people, hands, faces, or
body parts should be fully visible — only cropped fingertips may hint at the
cracking motion at the very edge of frame, product and props only. The scene
should feel like a lighthearted, almost playful "sign from the universe"
moment — as if [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details]'s promise arrived
disguised as fortune-telling, turning a moment of curiosity into an
unmissable, personal message.

templates.name: Chalkboard Handwritten Ledger
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed background is a dark
[BACKGROUND like weathered charcoal chalkboard] surface with visible chalk
dust, smudges, and eraser streaks for authentic texture. Left 65% of the frame
is covered in loose, hand-chalked lettering arranged like a handwritten list
rather than a designed layout: a bold [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] at the
top in large casual chalk script, followed underneath by 2-3 short chalk-drawn
lines each paired with a small hand-sketched icon or emoji (e.g. a flame, a
checkmark) acting as a bullet — [BENEFIT 1-4] written as if jotted quickly by
hand, not typeset. Below that, a [CTA like £1 per meal] line in the same chalk
style, with a small hand-drawn arrow or doodle pointing toward the product.
Tiny fine-print [CLAIM] text sits chalked in the bottom-left corner at a much
smaller scale, mimicking a disclaimer scribbled as an afterthought. Right 35%
of the frame shows a hand entering from the bottom edge, cropped at the wrist,
holding [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] upright at a slight angle
against the chalkboard, lit with soft even light so the product stands out
crisply against the matte dark background. The scene should feel like a
no-frills, straight-talking classroom or cafe chalkboard menu — the kind of
place that writes real offers by hand because they don't need a polished ad to
prove they're legit; unpretentious, direct, confident. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON]
— someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. The hand's gesture should feel
casual and unposed, like someone just grabbed the product off a shelf, evoking
[AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY] rather than a staged product shot.

templates.name: Steamed Mirror Fingerwriting
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Fill the frame with a close-up of
a fogged, water-condensation-covered bathroom mirror. Across the upper 60% of
the frame, render [SHORT HEADLINE] as if finger-written into the steam/fog —
uneven, hand-drawn stroke width, condensation droplets beading along the
letterforms, soft warm bathroom light glowing through the glass behind the
text. Below the misted headline, in the lower 40% of the frame, show [YOUR
PRODUCT with key packaging details] standing in sharp focus on a white tiled
countertop, reflected faintly in the clear (non-fogged) lower portion of the
mirror. Overlay a small rounded white pill/bar in the bottom third containing
[CTA like try it for DISCOUNT] in bold dark sans-serif, with a smaller
line of fine print beneath it for [YOUR OFFER like +P&P]. Lighting is soft,
diffuse, and warm, evoking an early-morning or post-shower haze; shallow
depth of field keeps the product crisp while the mirror text stays painterly
and soft-edged. The scene should feel like a real, lived-in bathroom moment —
someone paused mid-routine and left a message in the steam, product waiting
for them at the sink. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and
props only.

templates.name: Bar Chart Comparison — Old Way vs New Way
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Solid [BACKGROUND] fills the
full canvas. Top 20% holds a bold, centered [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words]
in white, framing the comparison as an obvious upgrade. Below it, a white
rounded-rectangle card takes up the remaining 75% of the frame, containing
a simple two-bar comparison chart: a tall bar labeled "[BEFORE STATE]"
on the left and a short bar labeled "[AFTER STATE]" on the right, both in
[ACCENT COLOR], drawn in a flat, hand-drawn infographic style with thin
black axis lines and simple sans-serif labels above each bar. Directly
beneath the two bars, small product renders sit in matching left/right
positions: on the left a generic, unbranded old-way item; on the right
[YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details], rendered larger and sharper
than its counterpart to signal it is the superior, current choice. The
scene narrative: this should feel like a quick, satisfying "aha" moment —
proof, at a glance, that switching to [BRAND] removes a chore you didn't
realize you could skip. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product
and props only. Flat, clean studio lighting, no shadows, infographic
clarity over photographic realism, minimal color palette limited to
[BACKGROUND], [ACCENT COLOR], and black text.

templates.name: Circled Comparison — Us vs Them Split Shot
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Vertical split-comparison layout
against a dim, textured [SETTING like a cluttered workshop/garage background],
slightly out of focus to keep focus on the two subjects. Top 20% of the frame
is a bold, punchy [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] in large white condensed
uppercase type with a subtle drop shadow, centered. Left half of the frame
shows the "before" / competitor method — a messy, failed, or outdated
approach to the problem [PRODUCT CATEGORY] solves — circled with a thick red
ring to draw the eye to the flaw. Right half shows [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details] or its result in clean, confident use — circled with a
thick green ring to signal the correct choice. The two circles sit at the
same height and scale so the eye compares them instantly. Bottom 15% of the
frame holds two pill-shaped badge labels side by side: a red badge reading
["OTHERS" or [COMPETITOR LABEL]] under the left circle, and a green badge
reading [CTA like WITH BRAND NAME] under the right circle, both in bold white
uppercase text. Lighting is moody and directional, low-key with practical
highlights, creating contrast between the cluttered dark background and the
bright, saturated red/green comparison rings. The scene narrative: this
should feel like catching someone mid-task and proving, in one glance, that
the old way is a frustrating failure waiting to happen while [YOUR PRODUCT
with key packaging details] is the obviously smarter fix — visceral,
immediate, no explanation needed. No people, hands, faces, or body parts —
product and props only.

templates.name: Split-Screen Honest Comparison
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Create a vertical split-screen
comparison layout. Left 50% has a [BACKGROUND like off-white] solid
background; right 50% has a [DARK BRAND COLOR like deep charcoal-teal] solid
background, divided by a clean vertical seam down the center. Top of the
frame carries a small centered pill-shaped badge reading [SHORT HEADLINE like
Honest Brand Reviews] straddling the seam, with a bold centered headline
below it reading [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] comparing two options,
also straddling the seam in a contrasting rounded pill banner. Below the
headline, each half displays its own stacked checklist of 3 short claims:
the left side shows [BENEFIT 1-3] each preceded by a green checkmark icon;
the right side shows [BENEFIT 1-3 negative/competitor drawbacks] each
preceded by a red X icon. In the lower half of each panel, present [YOUR
PRODUCT with key packaging details] centered on the left side against its
matching light background, and a rival product's silhouette-style shape
centered on the right side against its dark background — both photographed
from a three-quarter angle on a plain seamless backdrop, soft even studio
light, shallow shadow beneath each item, no surface or props, so the product
itself is the sole visual anchor of its half. Maintain generous negative
space around each product so it reads clearly at small sizes. No people,
hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only. The arrangement should
feel like a neutral, trustworthy reviewer laying two options side by side and
letting the checklist do the persuading — clinical, confident, and impartial
rather than salesy.

templates.name: Comparison Table Over Hero Jar
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a vertical 9:16 layout on
a warm gradient background moving from [DARK BRAND COLOR] at the edges to
[ACCENT COLOR like warm amber/copper] glowing behind the product. Top 12%:
centered two-line italic headline, [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words], in soft
