packaging details] upright, product label facing camera, sharp focus with a
soft drop shadow separating it from the background. Six small hand-drawn-style
icons (droplet, sparkle, zigzag arrow, heart, stopwatch, eye outline) are
placed evenly in the surrounding negative space — three on the left, three on
the right — each paired with a short bold benefit phrase in [DARK BRAND COLOR]
from [BENEFIT 1-4], connected to the product by a thin curved arrow pointing
inward toward it. A solid [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] banner spans the bottom 12% of
the frame containing [YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] in white text. Cast
[AUDIENCE PERSON] — the hand and wrist visible should read as belonging to
someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]; the gesture of presenting the
product should feel like a confident personal reveal, evoking [AUDIENCE
ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. The arrangement should
feel like this person is personally showing a friend the one product replacing
several old habits — direct, proud, and reassuring. Soft diffuse studio
lighting, no harsh shadows, clean and airy mood.

templates.name: Problem-Agitation Splash Hero
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Top 55% of the frame is a soft
[BACKGROUND] gradient serving as a clean text zone: a problem-agitation
headline in [DARK BRAND COLOR] mixed-weight type calling out a common
frustration with [PRODUCT CATEGORY], followed by a short bold rebuttal line
in all-caps [ACCENT COLOR], then a large 3-line stacked benefit headline in
bold [DARK BRAND COLOR] type reading like [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words].
Bottom-right third shows [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] resting
at a slight angle, breaking through a dramatic swirl of clear water/liquid
splash that arcs diagonally from the product up toward the text zone,
connecting copy to product visually. Studio lighting is soft and diffuse
overall with one dramatic directional highlight catching the water droplets
and the product's lid for sparkle and shine. Shallow depth of field keeps
the product and splash crisp while the background stays a smooth blush
gradient. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only.
The scene should feel like the moment relief finally arrives — the water
mid-motion conveys instant, visceral hydration bursting through and washing
away the old, ineffective routine the headline just called out.

templates.name: Bundle Offer — Floral Frame Promo Card
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a square promotional
graphic, no photography — pure typographic/illustrative layout. Background is
a soft diagonal gradient wash from [SOFT BRAND COLOR like mint green] in the
top-left corner through [ACCENT COLOR like warm coral] to [SOFT BRAND COLOR
like peach] in the bottom-right, overlaid with a very subtle honeycomb/mesh
texture. All four corners feature ornate hand-illustrated botanical line-art
clusters ([BRAND COLOR] linework with muted florals and leaves) framing the
composition and bleeding slightly off-canvas, leaving the center 55% clear
for text. A thin hairline border sits just inside the edge. Center-stack the
copy hierarchy: a short eyebrow line, a dominant oversized numeral + bold
headline stating the bundle mechanic in mixed weights (regular for connector
words, black-weight for the emphasis words), a giant [ACCENT COLOR] price
callout as the visual climax, a thin horizontal divider rule, then two lines
of supporting body copy with one bolded phrase, and finally a pill-shaped
[ACCENT COLOR] CTA button with white uppercase text near the bottom. No
people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only, and in this
case no product render at all, purely typographic/illustrative. The
arrangement should feel like a warm, personal invitation to a limited-time
deal — festive and generous rather than aggressive, like a hand-written offer
from a brand that wants to reward loyal customers with a bit of indulgence
and choice.

templates.name: Bundle Value Offer — Illustrated Border Frame
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed illustrated background
in [BRAND COLOR like chartoreuse green] framed by an ornate [ACCENT COLOR like
deep forest green] mandala/paisley border pattern running along all four
edges. Top 35% of the canvas is a bold, oversized stacked headline in
[CONTRAST COLOR like deep plum/purple] announcing the bundle offer — large
numeral-led price format, e.g. "[BUNDLE QUANTITY] for [BUNDLE PRICE]". Center
40% of the frame holds a cluster of [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details]
(5-6 SKUs of varying bottle/dropper/tube shapes) standing upright and grouped
tightly together, front labels facing camera, lit with soft even studio light
and a warm coral-to-cream radial glow rising behind them to separate the
group from the busy background. A floating rounded oval badge in
[ACCENT COLOR like teal] sits mid-right of the product cluster with urgency
copy like "[URGENCY PHRASE like Don't Miss Out!]" in white type. A vertical
fine-print disclaimer sits tight against the left edge. Bottom 10% is a solid
[DARK BRAND COLOR] bar listing the bundle contents and value framing, e.g.
"[BENEFIT 1-4]" with the key value phrase in [ACCENT COLOR]. The arrangement
should feel like a can't-miss seasonal stock-up — abundant, celebratory, and
value-forward, like a shelf display built to make someone want the whole set
rather than a single item. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product
and props only.

templates.name: Duplicate Hero Bottles — Transformation Claim Headline
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a square 1:1 ad on a
soft [BACKGROUND] gradient that fades from a warm blush tone into a paler
cream, lit with soft diffuse studio light. Left 55% of the canvas is open
gradient space reserved for copy: a large mixed-weight headline combining
elegant script and bold serif treatments reading [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10
words], with the key claim phrase in the heaviest weight for emphasis.
Right 45% shows two identical units of [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging
details] staggered side by side as a duplicated hero pairing, standing
upright on an implied surface, with a soft circular halo/ring graphic
glowing behind them in a lighter tint of [ACCENT COLOR] to frame the
product pair. Place a small brand seal/logo mark in the top-right corner.
Anchor the bottom 15% of the frame with a solid [DARK BRAND COLOR] banner
strip running full width, holding a short confident reassurance line in
white bold text: [YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence]. No people, hands, faces, or
body parts — product and props only. The scene should feel like a
before-your-eyes transformation reveal — confident, premium, and
convincing, as if the product pairing itself is the proof.

templates.name: Cart Clearance Flash Sale (Mock Checkout + Neon Callout)
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a mock e-commerce cart
screenshot styled as an ad. Left 55% is a simulated shopping-cart / order-list
UI stacked vertically: 4-5 rows, each showing a small square product thumbnail
of [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] on the left, a 1-2 line product
name/description beside it, a struck-through original price next to a bold
discounted price in [ACCENT COLOR], a quantity stepper control, and a "Total: [PRICE]"
line beneath — on a plain white background with thin divider lines between rows.
Top-right corner shows a small circular [BRAND] logo badge. Right 45% overlaps
the cart as a floating stack of bold callout cards in [ACCENT COLOR like lime green]
with black or [DARK BRAND COLOR] rounded-rectangle text blocks, oversized bold sans-
serif type in [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR], announcing the offer hierarchy: a short punchy
opener like [SHORT HEADLINE], a sentence explaining the promotion [YOUR OFFER like
Free Shipping over $50], and a giant [DISCOUNT like 50%] figure as the visual climax.
A large shocked/surprised-face emoji sticker sits at the top edge overlapping the
cart and callout stack, reinforcing disbelief at the deal. A bottom banner strip in
[DARK BRAND COLOR] spans the full width with a small alarm-clock icon and bold
[URGENCY PHRASE like LOW STOCK ALERT] in [CONTRAST COLOR] type, with a tiny "*Terms
apply" disclaimer in the corner. The scene should feel like a screen recording of a
real cart caught mid-checkout, interrupted by a too-good-to-miss flash discount —
urgent, slightly chaotic, and designed to trigger fear of missing out before the
deal disappears. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only.

templates.name: Clearance Countdown — Illustrated Heritage Frame
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a portrait promotional
card with a diagonal gradient background from [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] in the
top-left to [SOFT BRAND COLOR like warm olive/gold] in the bottom-right.
Along the top and right edge, run a hand-illustrated decorative border in
[ACCENT COLOR] and [CONTRAST COLOR] — a dense mandala/paisley motif with
florals and leaf details — framing an inner arch shape that curves from the
top edge down the right side, echoing a heritage or ethnic pattern style.
Place a small circular [BRAND] logo badge in the top-right corner, inset
inside the decorative border. In the top-left, place a single large emoji or
icon communicating mild shock/urgency. Below it, set a bold two-line stacked
headline in [DARK BRAND COLOR] using [SHORT HEADLINE] — oversized, tight
line-height, left-aligned, occupying roughly the upper third of the frame.
Beneath the headline, add 3–4 sentences of body copy in a smaller serif or
rounded sans font explaining [YOUR OFFER like a limited clearance discount],
with the discount value and urgency phrase bolded inline within the
paragraph. Near the bottom, place a solid [ACCENT COLOR] pill-shaped button
with white bold text reading [CTA like Grab Before They're Gone]. In the
bottom-right corner, add small italic fine-print text for [DISCLAIMER like
*Terms Apply]. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props
only; this is a text-and-graphic promotional card, not a product photo. The
scene should feel like an urgent, slightly apologetic brand announcement —
warm and community-toned rather than corporate, like a trusted local brand
being transparent about a limited-time clearance moment.

templates.name: Vintage Botanical Ingredient Field-Guide
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a vintage botanical
field-guide illustration split layout on an aged, textured parchment-cream
background. Left 50% of the frame is a detailed black-ink botanical engraving
of [KEY INGREDIENT] rendered as a full plant study — root system, stem,
leaves, flowers, and seed pods — with a scatter of loose seeds/pods resting
on the ground beneath it. Top-left corner carries small serif caption text:
the ingredient's scientific/Latin name on one line and "Common Name:
[KEY INGREDIENT]" beneath it in smaller caps. Right 50% is the clear text
zone on the same aged-paper background: a stacked headline in mixed serif
weights building from a plain question lead-in ("Did you know that
[CLAIM] are real and they can transform the look of...") into a bold italic
[ACCENT COLOR like burnt orange/rust] emphasis phrase naming the specific
before-state ([BEFORE STATE]) and body area, ending on a second italic
[ACCENT COLOR] phrase naming the after-state or target zone as a question.
Typography is classic serif for body text, elegant italic serif for the
emphasized colored phrases, small tracked-out caps for labels. No photography,
no gradients — flat aged-paper texture with subtle foxing/stain marks for
authenticity. The scene should feel like a page pulled from a 19th-century
herbal apothecary reference book — quietly authoritative, credible, and
almost scientific, inviting the viewer to trust a natural ingredient the way
they'd trust a field guide rather than an advertisement. No people, hands,
faces, or body parts — product and props only; the "product" in this format
is the ingredient illustration itself, not a packaged item.

templates.name: Before/After Transformation Testimonial
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a 4:5 testimonial ad on a
[BACKGROUND] solid backdrop. Top zone (roughly 10-20% of canvas) holds a large
handwritten-script-meets-bold-serif quote headline reading "[SHORT HEADLINE]"
with one key word set dramatically larger in a heavy serif for emphasis. Below
it, two rounded-corner photo frames sit side by side filling the middle ~45%
of the canvas: left frame is labeled with a dark pill badge reading "BEFORE",
right frame labeled with a dark pill badge reading "AFTER [TIMEFRAME]" —
both frames show the same close, cropped angle on the relevant body area so
the transformation reads instantly by direct visual comparison. Beneath the
photos, a body-copy testimonial block presents [FULL QUOTE 2-3 sentences]
with 2-3 emotionally charged phrases bolded for emphasis, followed by
"-[REVIEWER NAME like Dawn K.]" in italics. A small oversized quotation-mark
graphic sits near the quote for visual texture. A one-line disclaimer in
small gray italic text sits at the very bottom: "Unretouched photo. [TRUST
BADGE like Product given in exchange for honest feedback]. Results may
vary." Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY
REALITY]. Her expression and posture in the "after" frame should evoke
[AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. The scene
should feel like real, unposed proof — a private milestone made visible,
not a staged product shot.

templates.name: Magazine Cover Stat Proof
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Design a faux magazine-cover
layout on a warm [BACKGROUND] gradient (glowing amber-to-cream tone). Top
third: an oversized italic magazine-style wordmark reading [BRAND] in
[PRIMARY BRAND COLOR], with a small masthead-style date/issue line to its
right (e.g. month and year) in a thin sans-serif. Center-to-right of frame:
a close, warmly lit beauty portrait of [AUDIENCE PERSON], cropped from
mid-chest up, gazing slightly off-camera with a soft, confident half-smile,
shot like an editorial cover photo — soft diffused key light, shallow depth
of field, glowing warm skin tones. Lower-left quadrant overlays a giant bold
stat numeral [STAT 1 like 93%] in [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR] with 2-3 lines of
supporting claim text below it in mixed weight serif/italic type, echoing a
study result. Lower-right quadrant overlays a second, slightly smaller stat
numeral [STAT 2 like 92%] with its own 2-line supporting claim directly
beneath it. A short italic pull-quote in quotation marks sits between the two
stat blocks, styled like a magazine cover blurb, with an attribution name
below it in bold ([REVIEWER NAME like Donna D.]). A fine-print disclaimer
line runs along the bottom edge citing the study sample size and duration
([CLAIM]). Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living with
[AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her expression and posture should evoke
[AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. The scene
should feel like a credible editorial feature announcing a visible personal
transformation — confident, glowing, quietly proud rather than overtly
"salesy."

templates.name: Bundle Offer in the Wild — Forest Product Cluster
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a photoreal environmental
product shot: cluster [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details] and its
companion items together in the upper 60% of the frame, arranged at varying
heights on a natural [SURFACE like moss-covered rock] as if staged outdoors,
with a soft-focus [SETTING like a sunlit forest with ferns and tree trunks]
filling the background. Light source is dappled natural sunlight from the
upper left, soft and warm, creating gentle highlights on glass/metal caps and
soft shadows beneath each item; shallow depth of field keeps the background
gently blurred while the product cluster stays sharp. The bottom 40% of the
frame darkens into a high-contrast text zone: a short kicker line stating
[CLAIM] sits above a large, bold price/value callout in [ACCENT COLOR], with
a two-line subhead beneath listing [YOUR OFFER like 3 full-sized favorites +
4 free gifts], and a pill-shaped badge near the bottom in [DARK BRAND COLOR]
background with [ACCENT COLOR] text reading [URGENCY PHRASE like Only For A
Limited Time]. A small circular [BRAND] logo badge sits in a top corner. No
people, hands, faces, or body parts — product and props only. The scene
should feel like a curated haul of trusted essentials found growing straight
out of nature — abundant, earned, and worth stocking up on before the deal
disappears.

templates.name: Testimonial Quote Over Botanical Flat-Lay
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a vertical flat-lay ad.
Top 35% of the frame is a close-up natural texture background (leaves, herbs,
or botanical greenery matching [KEY INGREDIENT] or brand world) with a bold
italic pull-quote overlaid in white serif type: ["PULL-QUOTE — the most
emotional 4-8 word phrase from the review"]. Below the quote, a small green
verified-checkmark icon beside the reviewer's name in italic: [REVIEWER NAME
like Angela W.]. Bottom 65% shows [YOUR PRODUCT with key packaging details],
or a paired set of two, resting diagonally on a natural surface such as
reclaimed wood or stone, partially nestled into the same greenery/herb texture
from the top band so the product feels grown out of its ingredient story.
Labels face camera at a slight diagonal, fully legible. Soft, diffuse
overhead daylight, shallow depth of field, rich saturated greens against warm
label tones. The arrangement should feel like the product was just pulled
fresh from nature and set down mid-ritual — organic, trusted, and
ingredient-honest, letting a real customer's words do the selling before the
product is even fully in view. No people, hands, faces, or body parts —
product and props only.

templates.name: Real Results Before/After — Chat-Tagged Split Comparison
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose a vertical 3:4 layout.
Top 55% is split into two side-by-side unretouched candid photos of the same
treatment area on the same person, shot in plain, unstyled indoor lighting
(bathroom or bedroom, tiled or plain wall visible) — left photo labeled
"before," right photo labeled "after." Over the top-left corner of each photo,
overlay a small rounded chat-bubble caption styled like a messaging app,
showing a small circular avatar icon plus [PERSON DESCRIPTION like woman in
her 50s], with a one-line caption reading "This is before" on the left photo
and "This is after" on the right photo. On both photos, draw a dotted white
circle outline around the specific treatment area to direct the eye to the
change. Below the photos, on plain white background, set a large bold serif
headline in [DARK BRAND COLOR], 3 lines, reading [YOUR HEADLINE, under 10
words] naming the specific visible transformation. At the very bottom, in
small gray sans-serif text, include a disclaimer: "Unretouched photos.
Individual results may vary." No studio lighting, no retouching, no
gradients, no graphic embellishments beyond the dotted circles — the photos
must look like real unedited phone captures, not staged product photography.
Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON like a woman in her late 50s who has quietly started
covering her neck in photos] — her posture and framing should feel candid
and self-documented, not modeled. The scene should feel like a real customer
casually screenshotted her own private before/after check-in, tucked into a
conversation with a friend — proof that feels personal, unscripted, and
completely believable rather than staged marketing.

templates.name: Behind-the-Shoulder Hair Glow Reveal
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Frame [AUDIENCE PERSON] from
behind at the shoulder, her long, glossy hair falling as the dominant visual
element filling the center-right 65% of the frame. She holds [YOUR PRODUCT
with key packaging details] raised near shoulder height in one hand, poised
as if just misted through her hair. Background is a solid [SOFT BRAND COLOR
like warm peach/apricot], seamless studio backdrop with soft, even, diffuse
lighting that makes the hair catch light and shine. Top-left third is clear
negative space reserved for large stacked headline typography in [DARK BRAND
COLOR]; bottom-right corner holds smaller supporting copy in a mix of
[CONTRAST COLOR] and [ACCENT COLOR] highlighted keywords. Shot at 85mm f/2.5,
soft key light from front-left, shallow depth of field keeping hair strands
crisp and product label legible. The scene should feel like a private,
unhurried moment of self-care — someone finally seeing the result she'd
almost stopped hoping for, hair transformed at the source. Cast [AUDIENCE
PERSON] — someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her posture (calm,
turned slightly away, unbothered by the camera) should evoke [AUDIENCE
ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model posing for a product ad.

templates.name: Body-Part Proof Close-Up (Stat-Led Skin Tightening)
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Tight vertical crop from chin
to mid-torso, filling the entire frame with the treated body area relevant
to [PRODUCT CATEGORY] — no product visible in frame, this is a proof shot,
not a product shot. Subject wears simple [SOFT BRAND COLOR like beige/nude]
fitted apparel that frames the treated skin area without distracting from
it. Studio beauty lighting: soft, even, diffuse, front-facing, no harsh
shadows, clean editorial skin-focused rendering, shallow depth of field.
Centered overlay text sits directly on the skin/torso area: [SHORT
HEADLINE, framed as a knowing observation about the visible change] at the
top, followed by a bold stat-led claim combining [STAT like 92%] with
[CLAIM, e.g. "looked Tighter"] and [TIMEFRAME like 8 Weeks] in a mixed
serif/italic display type, using [ACCENT COLOR] to highlight the single
key result word. A small italicized disclaimer line sits at the bottom
edge in low-contrast white text: [CLAIM SUBSTANTIATION, e.g. consumer
testing sample size, age range, and condition tested]. The scene should
feel like quiet, private proof — as if the camera caught the one part of
her body she used to angle away from mirrors and cameras, now offered up
straight-on with no need to hide. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON] — someone living
with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her posture is relaxed, open, and
unguarded, evoking [AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling
a product.

templates.name: Bold Claim Swipe — Product Hero on Color Gradient
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR]
solid-to-dark gradient background filling the entire canvas, no scene or
surface — pure studio color-field composition. [YOUR PRODUCT with key
packaging details] positioned at the lower-left, angled roughly 45 degrees
as if tilted to dispense. A glossy ribbon of product swipes/swatches out of
the opening and arcs diagonally up and across the frame toward the upper
right, acting as the visual bridge between product and headline — the swipe
should be in [ACCENT COLOR] or the product's true dispensed shade, rendered
with realistic sheen and soft shadow. Upper-left to center holds
[YOUR HEADLINE, under 10 words] in a large script/display serif mixed with
a bold sans-serif line for emphasis, set in [CONTRAST COLOR], left-aligned,
taking up roughly 55% of vertical space. Lower-right holds
[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence] in a smaller weight of the same type family,
positioned near where the swipe trail ends. Studio lighting, soft top-down
falloff, glossy specular highlight along the swipe to sell texture and
product efficacy. The composition should feel like a single bold promise
delivered in one confident visual beat — the product doing the talking
through its own texture, not through a lifestyle scene. No people, hands,
faces, or body parts — product and props only. Mood: [MOOD].

templates.name: Before/Now Timestamp Story — Dark Hero Pedestal
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Full-bleed deep [DARK BRAND COLOR]
solid background with a soft vignette, darkest at the edges, subtle glow
concentrated behind the product. Left 60% of the canvas is the text zone,
left-aligned, stacked vertically: a small all-caps timestamp label
([TIMEFRAME like "2 MONTHS AGO"]) in [ACCENT COLOR], below it a medium-weight
serif line stating the "before" state with the final word or phrase italicized
for emotional emphasis, then a smaller all-caps transition word ("NOW"), then
a single oversized serif display line in [ACCENT COLOR] rendered dramatically
larger than every other text element — this is the visual anchor of the whole
composition — with a trailing italic line completing the thought, and finally
a thin horizontal rule followed by a small sentence closing the narrative with
a [TIMEFRAME like "8 weeks"] callback. Right 40% of the canvas shows [YOUR
PRODUCT with key packaging details] standing upright on a dark reflective
pedestal or plinth, shot straight-on at eye level, lit with a warm directional
rim light from the upper right that traces the edge of the product against
the dark background while the label stays crisply legible under soft frontal
fill light, casting a soft realistic shadow onto the pedestal below. Shallow
depth of field keeps the product tack-sharp while the background falls into
soft darkness. The scene should feel like a quiet, confident confession
whispered directly to the viewer — the dramatic lighting and oversized
typography turn a personal before/after admission into a moment of private
triumph, as if the product is the trusted secret behind a transformation the
viewer hasn't announced yet. No people, hands, faces, or body parts — product
and props only.

templates.name: Macro Skin Close-Up "Technique" Educational
templates.template:
Use the attached images as brand reference. Match exact product colors,
typography style, and brand tone precisely. Compose an extreme macro
close-up that fills the entire frame edge-to-edge with [AUDIENCE PERSON]'s
face — no background, no negative space, the skin itself is the canvas.
Frame tightly on the eye area, cheek, and surrounding skin so texture,
fine lines, and the specific concern being addressed are clearly visible
and unretouched. Shot at macro range, soft diffuse natural light, shallow
focus concentrated on the eye/cheek zone, warm true-to-life skin tones,
documentary-clinical realism rather than glossy beauty lighting. In the
bottom third, overlay a stacked three-line headline treatment directly on
the skin: a slim lead-in line reading "[YOUR SUBHEAD, one sentence]" in
[DARK BRAND COLOR], followed by a bolded phrase "[CLAIM]" highlighted on a
[SOFT BRAND COLOR like pale cream/yellow] rectangular text-block, then a
large bold display headline "[SHORT HEADLINE]" in [PRIMARY BRAND COLOR]
set on a second [SOFT BRAND COLOR like pale cream/yellow] highlight block
spanning two lines, left-aligned, bottom-anchored. Cast [AUDIENCE PERSON]
— someone living with [AUDIENCE DAILY REALITY]. Her expression should be
calm and unposed, eye softly closed or downcast, evoking
[AUDIENCE ASPIRATIONAL IDENTITY], not a model selling a product. The scene
should feel like a clinical-but-human diagnostic moment — as if the viewer
is looking closely at their own skin in a mirror and finally seeing the
specific problem named and explained, not glamorized.
